## PAGE 00731: (2026x Refresh1) DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model

- page_id: `303990707`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990707/2026x+Refresh1+DIV-1+Conceptual+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:53.429+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-1-conceptual-data-model', 'div-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

393867802

393867804

393867803

**Description**

The DIV-1, a new DoDAF-described Model in DoDAF V2.0, addresses the information concepts at a high-level on an operational architecture.

The DIV-1 is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.

The intended usage of the DIV-1 includes:

- Information requirements.
- Information hierarchy.

**Implementation**

DIV-1 can be represented using a DIV-1 diagram which is based in the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The DIV-1 defines each kind of information classes associated with the Architectural Description scope, mission, or business as its own entity, with its associated attributes and relationships. These entity definitions correlate to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] information elements and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space=''] inputs, outputs, and controls.

393867801

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating DIV-1 diagram' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990704 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00732: (2026x Refresh1) DIV-2 Logical Data Model

- page_id: `303990704`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990704/2026x+Refresh1+DIV-2+Logical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:53.266+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-2-logical-data-model', 'div-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

394056138

394056140

394056139

**Description**

The DIV-2 allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues. Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level data elements are included in the descriptions.

**Implementation**

DIV-2 can be represented using:

- DIV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='div_2.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.

Data described in a DIV-2 may be related to Information in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic' space=''] or and Activity Resource (where the Resource is Data) flow object in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='']. This relation may be a simple subtype, where the Data is a proceduralized (structured) way of describing something. Recall that Information describes something. Alternatively, the relation may be complex using Information and Data whole part (and overlap) relationships.

The DIV-2 information elements can be constrained and validated by the capture of business requirements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space=''].

The information elements modeled in the DIV-2 also capture the information content of messages that connect life-lines in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description' space=''].

The DIV-2 may capture elements required due to Standards in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) StdV-1 Standards Profile' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space=''].

394056137

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='MED']

This relation may be a simple subtype, where the Data is a proceduralized (structured) way of describing something. Recall that Information describes something. Alternatively, the relation may be complex using Information and Data whole part (and overlap) relationships.</p><p>The DIV-2 information elements can be constrained and validated by the capture of business requirements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) OV-6a Operational Rules Model" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The information elements modeled in the DIV-2 also capture the information content of messages that connect life-lines in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-6c Event-Trace Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>The DIV-2 may capture elements required due to Standards in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) StdV-1 Standards Profile" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) StdV-2 Standards Forecast" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65d590b1-f3f4-41f7-a0a6-4e3241ae8e59"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394056137</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Information Model" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating DIV-2 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00733: (2026x Refresh1) DIV-3 Physical Data Model

- page_id: `303990714`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990714/2026x+Refresh1+DIV-3+Physical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:53.775+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-3-physical-data-model', 'div-3', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

394180921

394180924

394180923

**Description**

The DIV-3 defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architectural Description. The Physical Schema is one of the models closest to actual system design in DoDAF. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space=''] is actually implemented.

While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.

**Implementation**

DIV-3 can be represented using:

- DIV-3 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='DIV-3.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition Diagram.

TheData Modelis one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the Information Model ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='']) is actually implemented.

394180919

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993684 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00734: (2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram

- page_id: `303993684`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993684/2026x+Refresh1+Document+Report+Designer+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:25.643+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Report Designer
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

983358622

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

983358634

985841017

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

983358624

#### NOTE: Note

Note

In this version, Document Report Designer diagram supports only the diagram for making the Microsoft Word document (DOCX) template.

#### Using the Document Report Designer diagram

In this section, you will learn how to create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a DOCX template that you can add in Report Wizard for generating reports later.

To create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a template

1. Right-click a Package in the Containment tree, select Report Designer Diagrams > Document Report Designer Diagram . [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating Report Designer Diagram.png']
2. Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.
3. Arrange diagram elements as desired. [ATTACHMENT filename='Arranging Diagram Elements.png']
4. In the Containment tree, right-click **Document Report Designer Diagram** and select **Generate Template** to generate the template. NoteOnly symbols of elements on the diagram are used to generate the code template. Other elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate the code template. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Refine more layout, style, and code logic in Microsoft Word.
6. Add the complete template to Report Wizard.
7. Generate the report from the added template. [ATTACHMENT filename='Report Generated from Added Template.png']

#### Diagram elements

Document Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive.

###### Text Box

Text Box represents free, uninterpreted static-text area in which you can type anything in single or multiple lines, from plain-format text to Velocity code, one element per paragraph. You can also place Text Box anywhere.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Text Box for holding text to be typed in.

###### Report Data Variable

Report Data Variable represents a variable of an existing Report Data model in a project. When you create the element in the diagram, the **Report Data Variable** dialog opens to select Report Data and select the variable of that Report Data.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Only Report Data in the Containment tree are supported.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Report Data Variable and its dialog for selecting Report Data and their variables.

###### Element Box

Element Box is represented as **#foreach** directive for fetching a collection of elements. After creating the Element Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the **Select Element** dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting a Class element is **#foreach($class in $Class)**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Element Box for fetching a collection of elements.

###### Page Box

Page Box is represented as **#forpage** directive for fetching a collection of elements in each document page. After creating the Page Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the **Select Element** dialog. The statement for the PageBox element is **#foreach($class in $Class)**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Page Box for fetching a collection of elements in each document page.

###### Property Box

Property Box provides a way to get an element property. You can place this element in Element Box, Page Box, and Condition Box that is contained in Element Box. The property of owner's scope element will be listed in Property Box, by which the user selects the property to show in the group.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- If the property value is a single item, the value will be printed in a single row, e.g., $class.name or $class.isActive .
- If the property value is a collection, the value will be printed in multiple rows, e.g., #foreach($member in $class.member) .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Box for obtaining an element property.

###### Element Table

Element Table is represented as **#forrow** directive for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form. After creating the Element Row in the diagram, you need to specify an element type as well as column number and row number in the **Element Table** wizard dialog. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Element Table for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Element Table wizard dialog.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

When you are back to update the element type, column number, or row number in Step 1, the table in Step 2 will be recreated with the blank content.

In Step 2 of the **Element Table** wizard dialog, the blank table is created where you can directly enter and edit text and Velocity code in the table cell. You can also press **CTRL** + **SPACE** to open the Property Guide pop-up dialog. Press **CTRL** + **SPACE** again to navigate you to Report Data Variables and Helper Tools. In this dialog, it guides you to available properties that you can select for the cell.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Property Guide pop-up dialog.

You can insert or delete columns and rows by right-clicking a cell to open the context menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The context menu for inserting and deleting rows and columns.

After creating the Element Table, you can open the **Element Table** wizard dialog again for updating the Element table through the smart manipulator button or the **Element Table** editing context menu.

###### Property Table

Property Table is represented as **#forrow** directive for fetching a collection of property elements in tabular form. You can place this Property Table in Element Box to open the **Property Table** wizard dialog to select the property scope of the Table element in the Specification dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting the member property of the Class element in the first row is **#forrow($member in $class.member)$member.name#endrow**. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table (see the detail in ).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Table and the Property Table wizard dialog for selecting the property scope of the Table element.

###### Include

Include is represented as **#include** and **#includeSection** directives for including content or a document to the template. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Include/IncludeSection** dialog. After selecting the document, **$import.include(****<document_name>.docx****)** is created. There are two options to select an included document: **File** and **Attached File**.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you enter a section name in the **Section** box, **$import.include(****<document_name>.docx****,****<section_name>****)** will be created instead.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Include for including content or a document to the template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Include/IncludeSection dialog for file or attached file selections.

###### Condition Box

Condition Box is a container element for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Condition** dialog. You can enter conditional statements into the statement fields. To guide available properties, you can type a dot (.) and press **CTRL** + **SPACE**.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- The #if statement is required for each Condition Box.
- If you want to add more #elseif statements, you can add them by entering more conditional statements.
- If you want to add an #else statement, select the Add #else option.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Condition Box for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Condition dialog for entering conditional statements into the statement fields.

###### Page Break

You can place the Page Break element on the diagram to be in the template to start a new page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Page Break for starting a new page.

#### Helper Tool

Helper Tool are a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Helper Tool** dialog. You must select or search for a purpose of the function in the list to display the function description.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If the returned object is a collection of functions, select the **Create this function in #foreach directive** option.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Helper Tool as a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates.

983358620

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Report Designer' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9bfd9d3e-138a-465d-83e0-3840e02074e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">983358622</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6aef6c67-73f7-48d1-84e4-4247926d69c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">983358634</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="beb0493c-6012-4059-b689-1de7e89f3156"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">985841017</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1ea7dc1-05bb-4e5f-8d69-19e5ea00f6be"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37f87569-bcb3-4f81-87a8-c6e645d24320"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">983358624</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="def2aaf1-2ee8-4c29-87ef-77edbcf43e0a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In this version, Document Report Designer diagram supports only the diagram for making the Microsoft Word document (DOCX) template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Using the Document Report Designer diagram</h2><p>In this section, you will learn how to create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a DOCX template that you can add in Report Wizard for generating reports later.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a template</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click a Package in the Containment tree, select <strong>Report Designer Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Document Report Designer Diagram</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating Report Designer Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.</li><li>Arrange diagram elements as desired.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Arranging Diagram Elements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click <strong>Document Report Designer Diagram</strong>  and select <strong>Generate Template</strong> to generate the template.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bccdd6ce-5638-40b6-bb8e-811e1d2f858b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only symbols of elements on the diagram are used to generate the code template. Other elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate the code template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generating the Template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li><p>Refine more layout, style, and code logic in Microsoft Word.</p></li><li><p>Add the complete template to Report Wizard.</p></li><li><p>Generate the report from the added template.<br /><br /></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Generated from Added Template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h2>Diagram elements</h2><p>Document Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive.</p><h4>Text Box</h4><p>Text Box represents free, uninterpreted static-text area in which you can type anything in single or multiple lines, from plain-format text to Velocity code, one element per paragraph. You can also place Text Box anywhere.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Text Box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Text Box for holding text to be typed in.</h6><h4>Report Data Variable</h4><p>Report Data Variable represents a variable of an existing Report Data model in a project. When you create the element in the diagram, the <strong>Report Data Variable</strong> dialog opens to select Report Data and select the variable of that Report Data.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb7dd89e-a3ed-44f1-885d-9cdaa6a3bcd6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only Report Data in the Containment tree are supported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Data Variable.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Report Data Variable and its dialog for selecting Report Data and their variables.</h6><h4>Element Box</h4><p>Element Box is represented as <strong>#foreach</strong> directive for fetching a collection of elements. After creating the Element Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting a Class element is <strong>#foreach($class in $Class)</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element Box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Element Box for fetching a collection of elements.</h6><h4>Page Box</h4><p>Page Box is represented as <strong>#forpage</strong> directive for fetching a collection of elements in each document page. After creating the Page Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog. The statement for the PageBox element is <strong>#foreach($class in $Class)</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Page Box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Page Box for fetching a collection of elements in each document page.</h6><h4>Property Box</h4><p>Property Box provides a way to get an element property. You can place this element in Element Box, Page Box, and Condition Box that is contained in Element Box. The property of owner's scope element will be listed in Property Box, by which the user selects the property to show in the group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4ad61ec-1f55-4a28-ae8d-e3eda91f5c62"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the property value is a single item, the value will be printed in a single row, e.g., <strong>$class.name</strong> or <strong>$class.isActive</strong>.</li><li>If the property value is a collection, the value will be printed in multiple rows, e.g., <strong>#foreach($member in $class.member)</strong>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Property Box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property Box for obtaining an element property.</h6><h4>Element Table</h4><p>Element Table is represented as <strong>#forrow</strong> directive for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form. After creating the Element Row in the diagram, you need to specify an element type as well as column number and row number in the <strong>Element Table</strong> wizard dialog. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element Table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Element Table for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form.</h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element Table Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Element Table wizard dialog.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d00fcc8e-2369-4be6-b56b-88005db27ff8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you are back to update the element type, column number, or row number in Step 1, the table in Step 2 will be recreated with the blank content.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In Step 2 of the <strong>Element Table</strong> wizard dialog, the blank table is created where you can directly enter and edit text and Velocity code in the table cell. You can also press <strong>CTRL</strong> + <strong>SPACE</strong> to open the Property Guide pop-up dialog. Press <strong>CTRL</strong> + <strong>SPACE</strong> again to navigate you to Report Data Variables and Helper Tools. In this dialog, it guides you to available properties that you can select for the cell.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Property Guide Pop-up Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Property Guide pop-up dialog.</h6><p>You can insert or delete columns and rows by right-clicking a cell to open the context menu.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Context Menu for Inserting and Deleting Rows and Columns.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The context menu for inserting and deleting rows and columns.</h6><p>After creating the Element Table, you can open the <strong>Element Table</strong> wizard dialog again for updating the Element table through the smart manipulator button or the <strong>Element Table</strong> editing context menu.</p><h4>Property Table</h4><p>Property Table is represented as <strong>#forrow</strong> directive for fetching a collection of property elements in tabular form. You can place this Property Table in Element Box to open the <strong>Property Table</strong> wizard dialog to select the property scope of the Table element in the Specification dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting the member property of the Class element in the first row is <strong>#forrow($member in $class.member)$member.name#endrow</strong>. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table (see the detail in <ac:link ac:anchor="ElementTable"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Element Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Property Table Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property Table and the Property Table wizard dialog for selecting the property scope of the Table element.</h6><h4>Include</h4><p>Include is represented as <strong>#include</strong> and <strong>#includeSection</strong> directives for including content or a document to the template. Creating the element in the diagram opens the <strong>Include/IncludeSection</strong> dialog. After selecting the document, <strong>$import.include(</strong><strong>&lt;document_name&gt;.docx</strong><strong>)</strong> is created. There are two options to select an included document: <strong>File</strong> and <strong>Attached File</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db36aa00-e1fd-4c3b-b6fb-ff8cbadd6fb6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you enter a section name in the <strong>Section</strong> box, <strong>$import.include(</strong><strong>&lt;document_name&gt;.docx</strong><strong>, </strong><strong>&lt;section_name&gt;</strong><strong>)</strong> will be created instead.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Include.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Include for including content or a document to the template.</h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Include Section Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Include/IncludeSection dialog for file or attached file selections.</h6><h4>Condition Box</h4><p>Condition Box is a container element for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives. Creating the element in the diagram opens the <strong>Condition</strong> dialog. You can enter conditional statements into the statement fields. To guide available properties, you can type a dot (.) and press <strong>CTRL</strong> + <strong>SPACE</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b279208-3639-46c7-b587-27a4774df765"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The #if statement is required for each Condition Box.</li><li>If you want to add more #elseif statements, you can add them by entering more conditional statements.</li><li>If you want to add an #else statement, select the <strong>Add #else</strong> option.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Condition Box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Condition Box for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Condition Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Condition dialog for entering conditional statements into the statement fields.</h6><h4>Page Break</h4><p>You can place the Page Break element on the diagram to be in the template to start a new page.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Page Break.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Page Break for starting a new page.</h6><h2>Helper Tool</h2><p>Helper Tool are a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates. Creating the element in the diagram opens the <strong>Helper Tool</strong> dialog. You must select or search for a purpose of the function in the list to display the function description.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8fb04c8-a037-4c3e-9f6a-415ac170ff82"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the returned object is a collection of functions, select the <strong>Create this function in #foreach directive</strong> option.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Helper Tool.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Helper Tool as a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93dbb6b7-1bbc-4b38-a4d0-ca645b2887bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">983358620</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Report Designer" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993792 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00735: (2026x Refresh1) Documentation tab

- page_id: `303993792`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993792/2026x+Refresh1+Documentation+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:50:01.223+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface > (2026x Refresh1) Quick properties panel
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425887055

1425887058

1425887056

INLINE

The **Documentation** tab shows information associated with a selected model element in the Model Browser or on a diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Documentation tab in the quick properties panel.

To open the **Documentation** tab, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, click the Documentation tab.
- On the **Window** menu, click **Documentation**. If there is no documentation for the selected element, the text area is empty.

To write documentation for the selected model element

- In the Documentation tab, click the text area and type the text.

To turn on the HTML text

- In the Documentation tab, click the HTML check box.

The HTML toolbar opens and you can type the HTML text.

1425887054

**Related pages**

- Model Browser

click the <strong>Documentation</strong> tab.</li><li><p>On the <strong>Window</strong> menu, click <strong>Documentation</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ## PAGE 00736: (2026x Refresh1) DOCX template in the Chart tool

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Chart tool

1344514375

##### Creating chart objects and setting chart types and styles

To create a chart in accordance with the specified chart type and style, use the following code:

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- chartType : case-sensitive type of the created chart with one of the following options:
  - "Column"
  - "Line"
  - "Pie"
  - "Bar"
  - "Area"
  - "Surface"
  - "Radar"
- **chartStyle**: case-sensitive style of the created chart with one of the following options:
  - Column
    - "Clustered Column" (by default)
    - "Stacked Column"
    - "100% Stacked Column"
    - "3-D Clustered Column"
    - "3-D Stacked Column"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Column"
    - "3-D Column"
  - Line
    - "Line" (by default)
    - "3-D Line"
  - Pie
    - "Pie" (by default)
    - "3-D Pie"
    - "Doughnut"
  - Bar
    - "Clustered Bar" (by default)
    - "Stacked Bar"
    - "100% Stacked Bar"
    - "3-D Clustered Bar"
    - "3-D Stacked Bar"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Bar"
  - Area
    - "Area" (by default)
    - "Stacked Area"
    - "100% Stacked Area"
    - "3-D Area"
    - "3-D Stacked Area"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Area"
  - Surface
    - "3-D Surface" (by default)
    - "Wireframe 3-D Surface"
    - "Contour"
    - "Wireframe Contour"
  - Radar
    - "Radar" (by default)
    - "Radar with Markers"
    - "Filled Radar"

If you do not specify the chart style, the default style of the chosen chart type is used.

A code example:

```text

```

##### Setting chart titles

To set the chart title, use the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

If you do not enter the chart title, the default title “Sample” is used.

To create a matrix data table, set the data categories or row headers for the tag value of each data series/column by using the following code:



From the sample code above, the row headers of the chart are set.

##### Setting chart data series

To create a matrix data table, set the data series or column headers by using the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

##### Setting a data source for each data series

To prepare a data source for each of the data series, use the following code:

```text

```

For example:


##### Printing a chart to a report

To print a chart to a report, use the following code:


For example:


### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e58c89e0-4d52-4757-afb2-ecdc7a70b5ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1344514374</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c61827db-f9e1-4143-b962-f6d8fafbd612"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1344514385</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc62462b-dc4f-4f09-aac4-c3d0cf6f1eff"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1344514373</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="424e2402-d8cf-4b64-a411-9520513aedcf"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3c9e37a-a0cd-4736-9f49-860fac6876bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1344514375</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Creating chart objects and setting chart types and styles</h3><p>To create a chart in accordance with the specified chart type and style, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="14f18f14-ed8c-4418-bba3-da7e456ef1e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[create(chartType : String, chartStyle : String) : Chart]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where the parameters are:</p><ul><li><strong>chartType</strong>: case-sensitive type of the created chart with one of the following options:<ul><li>&quot;Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;Line&quot;</li><li>&quot;Pie&quot;</li><li>&quot;Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;Area&quot;</li><li>&quot;Surface&quot;</li><li>&quot;Radar&quot;<br /><br /></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>chartStyle</strong>: case-sensitive style of the created chart with one of the following options:</p><ul><li>Column<br /><ul><li>&quot;Clustered Column&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Stacked Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;100% Stacked Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Clustered Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Stacked Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D 100% Stacked Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Column&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Line<br /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Line&quot; (by default)</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>&quot;3-D Line&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Pie<br /><ul><li>&quot;Pie&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;3-D Pie&quot;</li><li>&quot;Doughnut&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Bar<br /><ul><li>&quot;Clustered Bar&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Stacked Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;100% Stacked Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Clustered Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Stacked Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D 100% Stacked Bar&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Area<br /><ul><li>&quot;Area&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Stacked Area&quot;</li><li>&quot;100% Stacked Area&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Area&quot;</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;3-D Stacked Area&quot;</p></li><li>&quot;3-D 100% Stacked Area&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Surface<br /><ul><li>&quot;3-D Surface&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Wireframe 3-D Surface&quot;</li><li>&quot;Contour&quot;</li><li>&quot;Wireframe Contour&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Radar<br /><ul><li>&quot;Radar&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Radar with Markers&quot;</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Filled Radar&quot;</p></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ff663a8-5a8f-4aaa-95a4-909cf4aa491e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span><span>If you do not specify the chart style, the default style of the chosen chart type is used</span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A code example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="49b71698-6a7c-43f4-9c13-da1795a0b1d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($linechart = $chart.create("Line", "Line"))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting chart titles</h3><p>To set the chart title, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3e05969-ca81-46b0-b3e9-82e38d1e887a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[setTitle(title String)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ed7a520-5006-41c6-a511-1d11e7c6b007"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.setTitle("Line Chart")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48e15ca6-fc55-4a73-8154-b975db01cd4e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you do not enter the chart title, the default title “Sample” is used.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting chart data categories</h3><p>To create a matrix data table, set the data categories or row headers for the tag value of each data series/column by using the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b64be4b-6660-487c-9bfe-246019575d19"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.setCategories(categories : Collection)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b744b19c-7bee-444d-b0b3-f44cdc338507"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($categories = $array.createArray())
#set($void = $categories.add("TagA"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagB"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagC"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagD"))

$linechart.setCategories($categories)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>From the sample code above, the row headers of the chart are set.</p><h3>Setting chart data series</h3><p>To create a matrix data table, set the data series or column headers by using the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b6f0dd1-8866-40ee-93f4-9c71ce1b8768"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.createSeries(seriesName : String) : DataSeries]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28dc65b3-cf97-4b7f-938b-3103889f9435"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting a data source for each data series</h3><p>To prepare a data source for each of the data series, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7793b04-cb7a-4f69-916c-5e20fc74196e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[setDataSources(dataSource : Collection)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f32c06d6-7451-4bc7-821d-d8583c928d39"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($element in $sorter.sort($MyElement))
#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))
#set($dataSources = $array.createArray())
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagA))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagB))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagC))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagD))
#set($void = $series.setDataSources($dataSources))
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Printing a chart to a report</h3><p>To print a chart to a report, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b60c62be-6788-4dcc-b7c4-1e45b0c57845"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b7d5a03-9b62-48e1-a357-7a3cfc23609b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sample Model.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) DOCX template in the Chart tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A sample model used to extract values for creating a chart.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tag Values from a Model Mapped.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) DOCX template in the Chart tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Tag values from a model mapped into a matrix table using the Chart tool to create a chart.</h6><p>A full sample of the Chart tool code is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f8c30bd-fb4f-4f64-9858-69d2081bfa1e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('chart', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ChartTool')
#set($linechart = $chart.create("Line", "Line"))
$linechart.setTitle("Sample")

#set($categories = $array.createArray())
#set($void = $categories.add("TagA"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagB"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagC"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagD"))

$linechart.setCategories($categories)
#foreach($element in $sorter.sort($MyElement))
	#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))
	#set($dataSources = $array.createArray())
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagA))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagB))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagC))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagD))
	#set($void = $series.setDataSources($dataSources))
#end
$linechart.buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Chart Printed.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) DOCX template in the Chart tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart printed to a report after applying the full sample code using the Chart tool.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990436 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00737: (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0

- page_id: `303990436`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990436/2026x+Refresh1+DoDAF+2.0
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:29.837+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling
- labels: ['dodaf', 'department-of-defense-architecture-framework', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229190542

229190544

229190543

The Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) is an architecture framework for the United States Department of Defense (DoD) that provides visualization infrastructure for specific stakeholders concerns through viewpoints organized by various views. These views are artifacts for visualizing, understanding, and assimilating the broad scope and complexities of an architecture description through tabular, structural, behavioral, ontological, pictorial, temporal, graphical, probabilistic, or alternative conceptual means.

This Architecture Framework is especially suited to large systems with complex integration and interoperability challenges, and it is apparently unique in its employment of "operational views". These views offer overview and details aimed to specific stakeholders within their domain and in interaction with other domains in which the system will operate.

The DoDAF provides a foundational framework for developing and representing architecture descriptions that ensure a common denominator for understanding, comparing, and integrating architectures across organizational, joint, and multinational boundaries. It establishes data element definitions, rules, and relationships and a baseline set of products for consistent development of systems, integrated, or federated architectures. These architecture descriptions may include families of systems (FoS), systems of systems (SoS), and net-centric capabilities for inter-operating and interacting in the non-combat environment.

The purpose of DoDAF is to define concepts and models usable in DoD's six core processes:

1. Joint Capabilities Integration and Development (JCIDS)
2. Planning, Programming, Budgeting, and Execution (PPBE)
3. Defense Acquisition System (DAS)
4. Systems Engineering (SE)
5. Operational Planning (OPLAN)
6. Capability Portfolio Management (CPM)

According to modeling needs, there are the following DoDAF 2.0 templates for different purposes:

- **DoDAF 2.0 Project**- creates a blank DoDAF 2.0 project. creating a DoDAF 2.0 project will switch the application to the DoDAF 2.0 graphical user interface and use the DoDAF 2.0 model templates.
- **DoDAF Project**- creates a blank DoDAF project. creating a DoDAF project will switch the application to the DoDAF graphical user interface and use the DoDAF model templates.
- **DoDAF 2.0 Project Template with Process Guide**- using this template, you will get a new DoDAF 2.0 project enhanced with a modeling guide. The modeling guide helps you to build the architecture by providing summary information about the DoDAF 2.0 structure and the architecture creation process.

The DoDAF process (the following figure) is only recommendable. You can choose the creation sequence of diagrams according to your needs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

229190541

**DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints**

## PAGE 00738: (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF All Views viewpoint

- page_id: `303990455`

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['all-views-viewpoint', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229241152

229241155

229241154

“There are some overarching aspects of an Architectural Description that are captured in the AV DoDAF-described Models. The AV DoDAF-described Models provide information pertinent to the entire Architectural Description rather than representing a distinct viewpoint. AV DoDAF described Models provide an overview of the architectural effort including such things as the scope, context, rules, constraints, assumptions, and the derived vocabulary that pertains to the Architectural Description. It captures the intent of the Architectural Description to help ensure its continuity in the face of leadership, organizational, and other changes that can occur over a long development effort.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

229241151

**Views**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990576 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00739: (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF Migration to UPDM 1.x

- page_id: `303990576`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990576/2026x+Refresh1+DoDAF+Migration+to+UPDM+1.x
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:50.080+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM projects migration manual
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of this manual is to help you understand the differences between UPDM 1.x and DoDAF 1.5 or any earlier versions, guide you along data mapping and explain how you as a user can have a say in the mapping process.

The following chapters describe how the migration works.

##### Basic Concepts and Instructions

The Unified Profile for DoDAF/MODAF (UPDM) is the standard of an Object Management Group (OMG) initiative to develop a modeling standard that supports both the US Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) and UK Ministry of Defense Architecture Framework (MODAF). As UPDM supports both frameworks, it is particularly important for those used to DoDAF 1.5 or any earlier version to clear some misunderstandings.

The purpose of DoDAF migration to UPDM 1.x is to convert DoDAF 1.5 metamodel projects into UPDM 1.x metamodel projects without loosing data.

Migration to UPDM 1.x is not backward compatible, so before proceeding with migration we highly recommend that you back-up your DoDAF projects.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The purpose of this manual is to help you understand the differences between UPDM 1.x and DoDAF 1.5 or any earlier versions, guide you along data mapping and explain how you as a user can have a say in the mapping process.</p><p>The following chapters describe how the migration works.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f5ba939c-91b7-4f76-908c-642fabb92d72" /></p><h3>Basic Concepts and Instructions</h3><p>The Unified Profile for DoDAF/MODAF (UPDM) is the standard of an Object Management Group (OMG) initiative to develop a modeling standard that supports both the US Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) and UK Ministry of Defense Architecture Framework (MODAF). As UPDM supports both frameworks, it is particularly important for those used to DoDAF 1.5 or any earlier version to clear some misunderstandings.</p><p>The purpose of DoDAF migration to UPDM 1.x is to convert DoDAF 1.5 metamodel projects into UPDM 1.x metamodel projects without loosing data.</p><p>Migration to UPDM 1.x is not backward compatible, so before proceeding with migration we highly recommend that you back-up your DoDAF projects.</p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998796 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00740: (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF Projects Migration

- page_id: `303998796`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998796/2026x+Refresh1+DoDAF+Projects+Migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:06.744+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Migration User Guides > (2026x Refresh1) UPDM projects migration manual > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF Migration to UPDM 1.x
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Most of the data mapping problems you may encounter come from the fact that certain concepts and model names in DoDAF 1.5 standard differ in UPDM 1.x standard. If you are a DoDAF user and are not familiar with UPDM concepts, we strongly recommend that you first read this document and UPDM specifications before proceeding with migration.

DoDAF 1.5 models in MagicDraw are represented in the forms of diagrams, matrixes or tables. As explained in sub-section 3.1 below (Product Mapping), while representation formats have not changed, separate product representation has.

Please also note that all reports differ in DoDAF and UPDM. If you are familiar with report templates and velocity language, you can make DoDAF reports compatible with UPDM ones manually by changing the names of the stereotypes used to gather project data. However, if you are not, we recommend that you use predefined UPDM reports.

This section includes the following:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Most of the data mapping problems you may encounter come from the fact that certain concepts and model names in DoDAF 1.5 standard differ in UPDM 1.x standard. If you are a DoDAF user and are not familiar with UPDM concepts, we strongly recommend that you first read this document and UPDM specifications before proceeding with migration.</p><p>DoDAF 1.5 models in MagicDraw are represented in the forms of diagrams, matrixes or tables. As explained in sub-section 3.1 below (Product Mapping), while representation formats have not changed, separate product representation has.</p><p>Please also note that all reports differ in DoDAF and UPDM. If you are familiar with report templates and velocity language, you can make DoDAF reports compatible with UPDM ones manually by changing the names of the stereotypes used to gather project data. However, if you are not, we recommend that you use predefined UPDM reports.</p><p>This section includes the following:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="bf2daf1c-ca91-4be9-9f6b-c5c80be5bfc7" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992998 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00741: (2026x Refresh1) Dragging objects

- page_id: `303992998`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992998/2026x+Refresh1+Dragging+objects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:41.901+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['dragging-in-diagram', 'dragging-in-activity-diagram', 'dragging-in-state-machine-diagram', 'dragging-in-sequence-diagram', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

567604035

567604039

567604033

**On this page**

3

567604037

##### Dragging files and elements

The drag-and-drop capability allows you to:

- Drag files from your file system onto an element in the Model Browser or a symbol on a diagram. A hyperlink to the dragged file is automatically created.Dragged images are set as the **Image** property value of the element.If an image is dragged to a Stereotype, it is set as a Stereotype icon.
- Drag a project to the diagram pane to open the project.
- Drag source code files from your file system to the Code Engineering Set.
- Drag any elements to any properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space='']. For example, you can drag a Class element from the Model Browser to the **Type** property in the **Operation** Specification window. That Class element is assigned as the Operation type in the Specification window.
- Drag any elements from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] onto:
  - An empty place on a diagram. In this case, the class symbol is created on the diagram.
  - A shape on a diagram. In this case, a new attribute is created.
  - An element in the Model Browser. In this case, a new model element is created.
- Drag a property to create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MED'] :
  - Drag a property from the classifier (for example, class, component, subsystem) compartment to a diagram pane.
  - Drag a property from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space=''] to a diagram pane. In both cases, an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MED']is drawn from the classifier[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED']to a property's type. The property itself is set as an association end. If the classifier symbol is not present on the diagram, it is added as a result of the created association.

If selected model elements are not compatible with an open diagram, you will not be allowed to drag those model elements.

##### Dragging in diagrams

You can drag-and-drop relationships to a diagram.The path for the relationship with shapes at the end of the path and the path between the shapes (if the shapes at the end of the path are already represented on the diagram) is created.

You can also drag-and-drop model elements to a note or a text box symbol in a diagram to add a hyperlink to that element.

###### Dragging in Sequence diagrams

In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Sequence diagram' space=''] you can:

- Drag an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MED'] from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space=''] to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MED'] in a Sequence diagram.The Message becomes a Call Messageonce the operation is assigned. The[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='MED']type must have or inherit this Operation.
- Drag an Interaction to an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MED']. The Interaction will be set as the Refers To element.
- Drag a Signal to a Message. The Message is converted into a Send Signal Message and the Signal is assigned to the Message.

###### Dragging in State Machine diagrams

- In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) State Machine diagram' space=''] you can d rag an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='MED'] element to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MED'] element. A Trigger with this Event is created for the Transition element.
- You can drag behaviors from one state shape to another.

###### Dragging in Activity diagrams

In an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activity diagram' space=''] you can:

- Drag an Activity, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MED'], or a State Machine onto an Activity diagram to create a[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Call Behavior Action' space='MED']Call Behavior Action.
- Drag a Signal onto an Activity diagram to create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Send Signal Action' space='MED'].
- Drag a Signal onto a Send Signal Action to set or change the Signal.
- Drag a Signal onto an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accept Event Action' space='MED'] to set or change the Signal. Once the Signal is assigned to the Accept Event Action, the Signal Event for the diagram and the Trigger for the action will be created automatically.
- Drag an Event onto an Activity diagram to create an Accept Event Action.
- Drag an Event onto an Accept Event Action to set the Event.
- Drag a Signal Event to an Activity diagram to create an Accept Event Action.
- Drag a Property, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'] to an Activity diagram to create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MED']. Elements will be set as representative elements.
- Drag a Property, Actor, Class or Instance Specification to an Activity diagram by using the right mouse button. From the shortcut menu, select to create a Swimlane, Central Buffer Node, or available Actions. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

567640426

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) State Machine diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagramming' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993115 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00742: (2026x Refresh1) Drawing diagram shapes

- page_id: `303993115`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993115/2026x+Refresh1+Drawing+diagram+shapes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:48.567+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

568251568

568251570

568251569

You can draw diagram shapes in any type of diagram.

To display a diagram shape on a diagram pane

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space=''] , select the diagram.
2. Drag the diagram to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dgr_shape_on_dgr_pane.png']

You can change the representation of the diagram shape by changing the diagram stereotype display mode.

To change the representation of the diagram shape

1. From the diagram shape's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog , select a new value for the Show Stereotypes property. [ATTACHMENT filename='changing_dgr_shape_repres_sbml_prop.png']

You can also select whether to show or hide the diagram constraints or tagged values on the diagram shape.

To show diagram stereotype constraints and tagged values on the diagram shape

1. From the diagram shape's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog , select the Show Constraints and Show Tagged Values check boxes.

To display the context stereotype icon instead of the diagram stereotype icon on the diagram shape

1. From the diagram shape's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog , change the Use Stereotype property value to Context .

The **Use Stereotype** property takes effect only in case the **Show Stereotypes** property value is **Text and Icon** or **Icon**.

There is a possibility to show the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagram frame' space='']of a diagram type instead of the full diagram type on the diagram shape.

To display the abbreviated diagram type

- From the diagram shape shortcut menu, open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog and select the Show Abbreviated Type check box.

568251567

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Diagramming' space='']
