/><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[abbreviation ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of a diagram type instead of the full diagram type on the diagram shape.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display the abbreviated diagram type</p><hr /><ul><li>From the diagram shape shortcut menu, open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> and select the <strong>Show Abbreviated Type</strong> check box.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="063aa60d-df06-4958-aa40-3d3ff9160cd2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">568251567</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Diagramming" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000759 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00743: (2026x Refresh1) EA with BPMN Project

- page_id: `304000759`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000759/2026x+Refresh1+EA+with+BPMN+Project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:40.106+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

EA with BPMN Project is a template you can use if you want to work with a simplified Enterprise Architecture project with BPMN, which is based on the UAF framework and designed for enterprise and IT architecture modeling.

Creating an EA with the BPMN project will switch the modeling tool to the EA-BPMN Architect graphical user interface and use the EA with the BPMN model template.

You can fing the sample project *Insurance Enterprise Architecture (EA-BPMN).mdzip*at <modeling tool installation folder>\samples\UAF

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172B4D);">EA with BPMN Project is a template you can use if you want to work with a simplified Enterprise Architecture project with BPMN, which is based on the UAF framework and designed for enterprise and IT architecture modeling. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172B4D);">Creating an EA with the BPMN project will switch the modeling tool to the EA-BPMN Architect graphical user interface and use the EA with the BPMN model template.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26b1041f-5060-4d0a-ade0-ab73057f25a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can fing the sample project <em>Insurance Enterprise Architecture (EA-BPMN).mdzip </em>at &lt;modeling tool installation folder&gt;\samples\UAF</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="EA_simplified_framework.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) EA with BPMN Project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992724 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00744: (2026x Refresh1) Easier refactoring of composite used projects

- page_id: `303992724`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992724/2026x+Refresh1+Easier+refactoring+of+composite+used+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:10.679+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Project partitioning > (2026x Refresh1) Automated project usages
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Complex Composition

761371663

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Complex Composition

761371665

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Complex Composition

761371664

One of the problems caused by the old approach described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Automated project usages' space=''],was in refactoring composite used projects (used projects composed of sub-used projects). Whenever a composite used project was re-arranged by removing or re-importing sub-used projects, all of the projects or used projects using this used project more often than not became affected.

The following examples illustrate this problem.

#### INFO: Another types of problems

Example 1

Consider a used project *ElectricComponents* with 3 sub-used projects: *Capacitors*, *Inductances*, and *Resistances*. Consider another project *RadioSet*, which uses the used project *ElectricComponents*.

Now, use several model elements from each sub-used project in the project *RadioSet*.

###### Old problematic behavior

Previous versions of our modeling tool would create these three direct user-defined usages:

*RadioSet*→*Capacitors,*

*RadioSet*→*Inductances,*and

*RadioSet*→*Resistances.*

Effectively, those versions were “freezing” the contents of the used project *ElectricComponents.*

If you tried to refactor the used project *ElectricComponents*by removing its sub-used projects and re-importing their contents, the project *RadioSet* would be affected and start complaining about the missing used projects.

No earlier version of our modeling tool could rearrange the user-defined usages for fear of losing the user-specified used project composition data.

###### New behavior

As of version 17.0.3, a program creates automated used project usages instead of creating user-defined ones. So when the used project *ElectricComponents* is refactored, it keeps the same model elements but changes its structure. Furthermore, the automated usages in the project *RadioSet* are automatically rearranged.

#### NOTE: Another types of problems

Another types of problems

#### INFO: Complex Composition

Example 2

Another problem was “freezing” the used project's version. For instance, the project *RadioSet* uses the 100th version of the used project *ElectricComponents,* which in turn uses the 150th version of the sub-used project *Resistances.*

###### Old problematic behavior

Whenever the project *RadioSet* referenced the sub-used project *Resistances,* the direct usage would be created to the 150th version of it. If you wanted to migrate the project *RadioSet* to use the 200th version of the used project *ElectricComponents,* which used the 300th version of the sub-used project *Resistances,* a version conflict would occur, since the direct usage would be still pinned to the 150th version.

**New behavior**

As of version 17.0.3, automated usages do not carry information about the version and do not preclude the version update through the user-defined usage paths.

#### NOTE: Complex Composition

Complex Composition

If you already have projects with complex composition (having used projects or sub-used projects) that are prepared in any earlier version, you may want to refactor your used project structure to reap the benefits of this feature.

761396174

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db9beb52-9ede-4aa7-86b4-e01ea55c580e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">761371663</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de9ee522-0a26-4b77-840d-bd5c83cee616"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">761371665</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="211f478b-40a3-40e6-9d47-89470b830f71"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">761371664</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>One of the problems caused by the old approach described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Automated project usages" /></ac:link>, </span><span>was in refactoring composite used projects (used projects composed of sub-used projects). Whenever a composite used project was re-arranged by removing or re-importing sub-used projects, all of the projects or used projects using this used project more often than not became affected. </span></p><p><span>The following examples illustrate this problem. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ee5272f-9035-4b7b-af56-560a6b8307cb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Consider a used project <em>ElectricComponents</em> with 3 sub-used projects: <em>Capacitors</em>, <em>Inductances</em>, and <em>Resistances</em>. Consider another project <em>RadioSet</em>, which uses the used project <em>ElectricComponents</em>.</p><p>Now, use several model elements from each sub-used project in the project <em>RadioSet</em>.</p><h4>Old problematic behavior</h4><p><span>Previous versions of our modeling tool would create these three direct user-defined usages:</span></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span><em>RadioSet</em> </span><span>→ </span><span><em>Capacitors, </em></span></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span><em>RadioSet</em> </span><span>→ </span><span><em>Inductances, </em>and</span></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span><em>RadioSet</em> </span><span>→ </span><span><em>Resistances.</em> </span></p><p><span>Effectively, those versions were “freezing” the contents of the used project <em>ElectricComponents.</em></span></p><p><span>If you tried to refactor the used project <em>ElectricComponents </em>by removing its sub-used projects and re-importing their contents, the project <em>RadioSet</em> would be affected and start complaining about the missing used projects. </span></p><p><span>No earlier version of our modeling tool could rearrange the user-defined usages for fear of losing the user-specified used project composition data. </span></p><h4><span>New behavior </span></h4><p><span>As of version 17.0.3, a program creates automated used project usages instead of creating user-defined ones. So when the used project <em>ElectricComponents</em> is refactored, it keeps the same model elements but changes its structure. Furthermore, the automated usages in the project <em>RadioSet</em> are automatically rearranged. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb0a945-9776-4277-a14d-78b7adbb4fac"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Another types of problems</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Now problems could occur only if the rearranged used project does not contain all the same elements it had before the rearrangement. Thus some automated usages may turn into unconfirmed ones. For more information about unconfirmed usages, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Controlling dependency creation between used projects" /></ac:link>. </ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7706641-5f41-4d39-ac76-ab0415ad651d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Another problem was “freezing” the used project's version. For instance, the project <em>RadioSet</em> uses the 100<sup>th</sup> version of the used project <em>ElectricComponents,</em> which in turn uses the 150<sup>th</sup> version of the sub-used project <em>Resistances.</em></p><h4>Old problematic behavior</h4><p>Whenever the project <em>RadioSet</em> referenced the sub-used project <em>Resistances,</em> the direct usage would be created to the 150<sup>th</sup> version of it. If you wanted to migrate the project <em>RadioSet</em> to use the 200<sup>th</sup> version of the used project <em>ElectricComponents,</em> which used the 300<sup>th</sup> version of the sub-used project <em>Resistances,</em> a version conflict would occur, since the direct usage would be still pinned to the 150<sup>th</sup> version.</p><p><strong>New behavior</strong></p><p>As of version 17.0.3, automated usages do not carry information about the version and do not preclude the version update through the user-defined usage paths.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a6abb8f3-6263-4d53-8b06-52a3b73f9e93"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Complex Composition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>If you already have projects with complex composition (having used projects or sub-used projects) that are prepared in any earlier version, you may want to refactor your used project structure to reap the benefits of this feature. </ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f625112b-15a2-469a-8aaf-e30e9f7f088f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">761396174</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993980 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00745: (2026x Refresh1) Ecore modeling

- page_id: `303993980`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993980/2026x+Refresh1+Ecore+modeling
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:46.777+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Ecore support
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Diagrams and elements in Ecore models

There are no specific diagrams for editing Ecore models in the modeling tool. You can use the same Class diagrams as you use for your UML models. Since Ecore is almost a subset of UML (with a few additions), familiar UML elements are used for the modeling. You can also develop Ecore models without using the Ecore profile at all. If your Ecore model uses only UML-specific information, you can develop it using plain UML and export it to Ecore without any problem.

Ecore is even more similar to EMOF. You can export the same model to both Ecore and EMOF.

Class, DataType, Enumeration, Package, Operation, Parameter have a direct one-to-one correspondence between UML and Ecore.

Ecore has two flavors of structural features, EAttribute and EReference, while UML has just one – Property. Fortunately a differentiation between an attribute and a reference is unambiguous and automatically resolved: the property, whose type is a data type is treated as EAttribute; the property, whose type is a class is treated as EReference. Hence the user does not need to worry about this - he/she can simply use properties.

There are no standalone Association and Generalization model elements in Ecore, but there is analogous information in Ecore: two EReferences, pointing to each other by their **opposite** property is equivalent to the association; the EClass::**eSuperTypes** property is equivalent to a generalization. Hence it is possible and meaningful to draw associations and generalizations in your model for exporting this information to Ecore.

Ecore generics (templates) are also supported. You can use the UML template support to model Ecore generics. While the modeling is not trivial (and not one-to-one due to weak semantics of Ecore's EGenericType), it is possible to model all cases of template types, even ones with complexly nested type bounds like, for example, **SortedList<T extends Comparable<? super T>>**. 
Your models can also contain any other UML elements, which are not present in Ecore. These elements are simply skipped during the export to Ecore. A warning is given about these elements.

##### Element properties in Ecore models

There are few Ecore-specific properties, which are brought in when the Ecore profile is used. These properties are used to capture Ecore specific information, not existing in UML. MOF-specific properties are also relevant for Ecore. These special properties are as follows:

- The Ecore package has the additional properties: nsPrefix ( Namespace Prefix ) and nsURI (corresponds to the URI property specified in the UML v2.4.1). [ATTACHMENT filename='additional_properties_ecore_package.png']

- The Ecore classifier (class, data type, enumeration) has the additional properties: instanceClassName ( Instance Class Name ) and instanceTypeName ( Instance Type Name ). [ATTACHMENT filename='additional_properties_ecore_clasiffier.png']

- Ecore attributes and references (modeled as the UML property) have the additional properties: volatile ( Volatile ), transient ( Transient ), unsettable ( Unsettable ), and resolveProxies ( Resolve Proxies , used for references only). [ATTACHMENT filename='additional_properties_ecore_attribute.png']

##### Annotation modeling

Ecore annotations are modeled as UML comments. For simple annotations no additional actions are necessary.

However, Ecore annotations have more powerful semantic than UML comments – they can have an internal substructure. In particular they can have an additional key-value map. For this additional information, there is a special «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype, that can be applied on an annotating comment. After applying the stereotype, the key-value map can be entered in a separate node of the annotating comment Specification window. Key-value pairs are stored as internal subcomment elements of the annotation. 
 
To create an Ecore annotation

1. In the Ecore model, create a Comment element.
2. Apply the «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype to the element. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_annotations_specification_window.png']

To create a key-value pair

1. In the Ecore annotation's Specification window, select the Annotation Details property group. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_annotation_details.png']
2. Click the Create button. The key-value pair's Specification window opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_string_to_string.png']
3. Enter values for both the Value and Key properties.
4. Click the Back button when you are done. You will see the key-value pair created. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_annotation_vision.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Diagrams and elements in Ecore models</h3><p>There are no specific diagrams for editing Ecore models in the modeling tool. You can use the same Class diagrams as you use for your UML models. Since Ecore is almost a subset of UML (with a few additions), familiar UML elements are used for the modeling. You can also develop Ecore models without using the Ecore profile at all. If your Ecore model uses only UML-specific information, you can develop it using plain UML and export it to Ecore without any problem.</p><p>Ecore is even more similar to EMOF. You can export the same model to both Ecore and EMOF.</p><p>Class, DataType, Enumeration, Package, Operation, Parameter have a direct one-to-one correspondence between UML and Ecore.</p><p>Ecore has two flavors of structural features, EAttribute and EReference, while UML has just one – Property. Fortunately a differentiation between an attribute and a reference is unambiguous and automatically resolved: the property, whose type is a data type is treated as EAttribute; the property, whose type is a class is treated as EReference. Hence the user does not need to worry about this - he/she can simply use properties.</p><p>There are no standalone Association and Generalization model elements in Ecore, but there is analogous information in Ecore: two EReferences, pointing to each other by their <strong>opposite</strong> property is equivalent to the association; the EClass::<strong>eSuperTypes</strong> property is equivalent to a generalization. Hence it is possible and meaningful to draw associations and generalizations in your model for exporting this information to Ecore.</p><p>Ecore generics (templates) are also supported. You can use the UML template support to model Ecore generics. While the modeling is not trivial (and not one-to-one due to weak semantics of Ecore's EGenericType), it is possible to model all cases of template types, even ones with complexly nested type bounds like, for example, <strong>SortedList&lt;T extends Comparable&lt;? super T&gt;&gt;</strong>. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />Your models can also contain any other UML elements, which are not present in Ecore. These elements are simply skipped during the export to Ecore. A warning is given about these elements.</p><h3>Element properties in Ecore models</h3><p>There are few Ecore-specific properties, which are brought in when the Ecore profile is used. These properties are used to capture Ecore specific information, not existing in UML. MOF-specific properties are also relevant for Ecore. These special properties are as follows:</p><ul><li>The Ecore package has the additional properties: nsPrefix (<strong>Namespace Prefix</strong>) and nsURI (corresponds to the <strong>URI</strong> property specified in the UML v2.4.1).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="additional_properties_ecore_package.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>The Ecore classifier (class, data type, enumeration) has the additional properties: instanceClassName (<strong>Instance Class Name</strong>) and instanceTypeName (<strong>Instance Type Name</strong>). <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="additional_properties_ecore_clasiffier.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>Ecore attributes and references (modeled as the UML property) have the additional properties: volatile (<strong>Volatile</strong>), transient (<strong>Transient</strong>), unsettable (<strong>Unsettable</strong>), and resolveProxies (<strong>Resolve Proxies</strong>, used for references only). <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="additional_properties_ecore_attribute.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Annotation modeling</h3><p>Ecore annotations are modeled as UML comments. For simple annotations no additional actions are necessary.</p><p>However, Ecore annotations have more powerful semantic than UML comments – they can have an internal substructure. In particular they can have an additional key-value map. For this additional information, there is a special «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype, that can be applied on an annotating comment. After applying the stereotype, the key-value map can be entered in a separate node of the annotating comment Specification window. Key-value pairs are stored as internal subcomment elements of the annotation. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To create an Ecore annotation</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Ecore model, create a Comment element.</li><li>Apply the «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype to the element.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_annotations_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To create a key-value pair</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Ecore annotation's Specification window, select the <strong>Annotation Details</strong> property group. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_annotation_details.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button. The key-value pair's Specification window opens. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_string_to_string.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Enter values for both the <strong>Value</strong> and <strong>Key</strong> properties.</li><li>Click the <strong>Back</strong> button when you are done. You will see the key-value pair created. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_annotation_vision.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992248 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00746: (2026x Refresh1) Ecore support

- page_id: `303992248`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992248/2026x+Refresh1+Ecore+support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:44.551+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305838392

305838394

305838393

Ecore model is a model type supported by Eclipse Modeling Framework (EMF). This model type can be colloquially called the EMF model (even though EMF supports many types of models, e.g., UML models).

Ecore models can be used for various purposes:

- Metamodeling purposes, where its expressive power is roughly similar to EMOF (and even slightly higher than EMOF).
- Simple class modeling purposes, where Ecore model is used as a subset of UML.

Ecore models made using the modeling tool can be exported as Ecore models for the further processing (generating model repositories, code or XML parsing and storing, etc.) with other EMF tools.

305838390

**Related pages**

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a73692bb-2a50-492c-bc72-b528ce3723c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305838392</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b61a912b-cd5a-43d8-b06e-86686bc50550"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305838394</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c91a9c73-bfb2-464e-89e2-50cd978ac195"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305838393</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Ecore model is a model type supported by Eclipse Modeling Framework (EMF). This model type can be colloquially called the EMF model (even though EMF supports many types of models, e.g., UML models).</p><p>Ecore models can be used for various purposes:</p><ul><li>Metamodeling purposes, where its expressive power is roughly similar to EMOF (and even slightly higher than EMOF).</li><li>Simple class modeling purposes, where Ecore model is used as a subset of UML.</li></ul><p>Ecore models made using the modeling tool can be exported as Ecore models for the further processing (generating model repositories, code or XML parsing and storing, etc.) with other EMF tools.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13b48495-04a9-4916-b419-21693e6a1a0a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305838390</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="adfefb38-4bfc-49a1-b3bc-f6d93ba5028e" /><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00747: (2026x Refresh1) Edit Hyperlink dialog

- page_id: `303996720`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996720/2026x+Refresh1+Edit+Hyperlink+dialog
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:19.553+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Manage Navigation > (2026x Refresh1) Defining hyperlinks
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add new or manage existing hyperlinks in the **Edit Hyperlink** dialog, consisting of:

- Three main tabs containing all modeling tools: **Address** tab, **File** tab, and **Element/Symbol** tab.
- One tab required to install the Cameo DataHub Plugin : **OSLC Link** tab .

The following figure illustrates the tabs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All available tabs in the Edit Hyperlink dialog.

##### Hyperlink types

The following table provides details of available hyperlink types.

| Tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog | Hyperlink type | Required plugins | Tab descriptions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Address tab | Links to webpages or other external resources. | - | Learn more about Address tab >> |
| File tab | Opens the file from your file system. | Learn more about File tab >> |  |
| Element/Symbol tab | In HTML text, opens the element Specification window.In plain text, on element/symbol, or diagram, opens the diagram. | Learn more about Element/Symbol tab >> |  |
| OSLC Link tab | Links to OSLC resources. | Cameo DataHub Plugin. How to install the Cameo DataHub Plugin >> | Learn more about OSLC Link tab >> |

##### Opening the Edit Hyperlink dialog

To open the **Edit Hyperlink** dialog

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Hyperlinks in texts' space='']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Hyperlinks on elements' space='']>]]>

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Defining hyperlinks' space='']

- Linking model elements to OSLC resources
- Managing hyperlinks

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991965 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00748: (2026x Refresh1) Edit Item Flow Realization Between Activities dialog

- page_id: `303991965`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991965/2026x+Refresh1+Edit+Item+Flow+Realization+Between+Activities+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:22.104+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Item Flow management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Edit Item Flow Realization Between Activities** dialog allows you to review Activities for a selected Item Flow.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Edit Item Flow Realization Between Activities** dialog

- In the Specification window of the Item Flow, select From Activity or To Activity property value cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] .
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Item Flow Manager dialog' space='']**Item Flow Manager** dialog , select the From Activity or To Activity cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] .

The buttons of the dialog are described in the following table.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Remove From Table | Click to delete the selected Activity pair. |
| Show Full Paths | Toggle either to show or hide the full element paths. |

To remove the realization between listed Activity pairs

1. Select an Activity pair.
2. Click the Remove From Table button.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999008 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00749: (2026x Refresh1) Edit Project Status in Lr diagram

- page_id: `303999008`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999008/2026x+Refresh1+Edit+Project+Status+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:22.426+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) Lr Lines of Development > (2026x Refresh1) Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart
- labels: ['apply-project-status-to-lr', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To edit the Project Status

1. Right-click the Milestone and select Edit Status . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Project Status Application Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Edit the Project status by following the steps of the wizard. If the Milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select **Edit Status** and then select the Milestone you want to edit the status for.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To edit the Project Status<span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Milestone and select <strong>Edit Status</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link> opens.</li><li><p class="confluence-link">Edit the Project status by following the steps of the wizard.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09bbfbf5-0d29-4034-afb9-5987f3f65b10"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the Milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select <strong>Edit Status</strong> and then select the Milestone you want to edit the status for.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994887 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00750: (2026x Refresh1) Editing logical values

- page_id: `303994887`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994887/2026x+Refresh1+Editing+logical+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:51.704+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Editing property values
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Logical (boolean) type property value can be either *true*or *false*. Examples of logical type properties: *Is Abstract*, *Is Read Only*.

To edit a logical property value, do one of the following

- Select the check box to set the value to *true*.
- Clear the check box selection to set value to *false*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true1[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing property values' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996531 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00751: (2026x Refresh1) Editing long textual values

- page_id: `303996531`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996531/2026x+Refresh1+Editing+long+textual+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:09.472+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Editing property values > (2026x Refresh1) Editing textual properties
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In case of editing a value that consists of several words, the value cell may seem too short. In order to avoid this inconvenience you can edit the property value in a dialog specially designated for typing long values.

To edit a textual value consisting of several words

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Edit button (see the highlighted button in the following figure).
3. In the opened dialog, type a value and click **OK**.

There are some textual properties, whose values can keep text formatting settings. You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) HTML editor' space='']HTML editorin such cases. To use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) HTML editor' space=''], select the **HTML**check box in the dialog opened for editing this special kind of textual property value.

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing textual properties' space='']modified1

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996529 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00752: (2026x Refresh1) Editing multiline textual values

- page_id: `303996529`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996529/2026x+Refresh1+Editing+multiline+textual+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:09.418+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Editing property values > (2026x Refresh1) Editing textual properties
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some properties can have more than one textual value, for example, the **Body**property of an opaque expression, the **Pre Condition**and **Post Condition**properties of a use case. You can use a special editor to edit multiline textual values.

Using the editor for multiline textual values, you can perform the following actions:

- Create a new value.
- Remove a selected value.
- Edit each value in the HTML editor separately.
- Reorder values.
- Strip long values (available only in the Specification window).

To strip long values, make sure that the Strip Multiline Text mode isturned on in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''].

The following table describes functions of buttons used in the editor.

| Button | Shortcut Key | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | Select the HTML check box to edit a selected row in the HTML mode.The HTML editor toolbar will appear. For more information about theHTML toolbar, refer to "HTML editor toolbar" on page 428. |
|  | ALT+ENTER | Click to add an empty row after the selected one to type a new value. |
|  | ALT+BACKSPACE | Click to remove a selected row. |
|  | ALT+SHIFT+UPARROW | Click to moves up a selected row. |
|  | ALT+SHIFT+DOWNARROW | Click to moves down a selected row. |

Mac OS users should use MAC shortcut key as an alternative for the ALT key.

editor.</span></p><table><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Shortcut Key</th><th colspan="1">Description</th></tr><tr><td> </td><td> </td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Select the HTML check box to edit a selected row in the HTML mode.<br /><span>The HTML editor toolbar will appear. For more information about the<br /><span>HTML toolbar, refer to <span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);">&quot;HTML editor toolbar&quot; <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">on page 428.</span></span><br /></span></span></span></td></tr><tr><td> </td><td><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ALT+ENTER<br /></span></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Click to add an empty row after the selected one to type a new value.<br /></span></td></tr><tr><td> </td><td><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ALT+BACKSPACE<br /></span></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Click to remove a selected row.<br /></span></td></tr><tr><td> </td><td><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ALT+SHIFT+UP<br /><span>ARROW</span><br /></span></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Click to moves up a selected row.<br /></span></td></tr><tr><td> </td><td><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">ALT+SHIFT+DOWN<br /><span>ARROW</span><br /></span></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Click to moves down a selected row.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a322d848-4edb-4c86-ab89-5456991339ae"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Mac OS users should use MAC shortcut key <span>as an alternative for the ALT key.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994883 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00753: (2026x Refresh1) Editing property values

- page_id: `303994883`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994883/2026x+Refresh1+Editing+property+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:51.318+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can edit property values in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Tables' space='']
- Element tab on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Properties tab' space='']**Properties**tabin the Model Browser

Property values in gray are non-editable. They are derived from other elements used in your model.

Learn about editing values of different property types in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing textual properties' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing logical values' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting values' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Cases of advanced editing' space='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true1

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996650 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00754: (2026x Refresh1) Editing property values in property groups

- page_id: `303996650`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996650/2026x+Refresh1+Editing+property+values+in+property+groups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:12.762+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Editing property values > (2026x Refresh1) Cases of advanced editing
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some elements can be related to each other as the owner and the owned one, for example, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MED'] can be an owner of a trigger, and the trigger can be owned by the transition.

The modeling tool allows the handy editing of owned element property values via owner’s properties. Owned element properties are available as appropriately named property groups, for example, the *Entry*, *Do Activity*, and *Exit*property groups in the state’s Specification window (see the following figure).

When editing owned element properties, keep in mind that you create a new element in your model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

A property setin the property group may vary before and after owned element specification. For example, let’s say,we have an activity assigned as a behavior type in the **Entry**property group. As a result the additional properties appear in the **Entry** property group (**Name** and **Owned Diagram**) after creating a new activity (see the following figure).

Keep in mind that the owner’s specification contains only ownership-relevant properties of an owned element. 
To view and edit all other properties of the owned element, open its Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an owned element as a property value

1. In the property group, wherein you want to create the owned element, select either a type or an elementproperty specification cell.
2. From the list, select an element you need to create.
3. Specify additional properties corresponding to the created element.

To remove an owned element, do one of the following

- Either

1. Select the **<owned element type>**property specification cell.
2. In the drop-down list, select *<UNSPECIFIED>*.

- Or

1. Select the <owned element> property cell and click **...** button.
2. From the list, select **Delete <element>**.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994896 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00755: (2026x Refresh1) Editing textual properties

- page_id: `303994896`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994896/2026x+Refresh1+Editing+textual+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:52.532+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Editing property values
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Textual type property values are usually simply typed in a property value cell. In some specific cases you can also use advanced editing features, i.e., long textual values editor and multiline textual values editor (both available in HTML). 
Examples of textual type properties: *Name*, *Body*.

To edit a textual property value

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Type a value.

Learn about advanced cases of editing textual values in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing long textual values' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing multiline textual values' space='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996568 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00756: (2026x Refresh1) Editor Tabs

- page_id: `303996568`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996568/2026x+Refresh1+Editor+Tabs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:10.135+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Editor tabs allow you to access diagrams and model samples.

On this page:

##### Editor tab types

The modeling tool contains the following editor tabs:

- Welcome tab. The welcome tab is displayed once the modeling tool is launched. It allows you to create new or open other projects, view model samples provided with the modeling tool installation, etc.
- Samples tab. Displays the model samples provided with the modeling tool installation.
- Diagram Editor tab. It allows you to display and work with diagrams. You can have multiple diagram tabs open simultaneously.

##### Editor tabs commands

Editor tabs' commands can be found:

- In the Main Menu , click Window , then Editor Tabs . In the dropdown menu, select the appropriate command.

##### Viewing samples

You can view the model samples provided with the modeling tool installation either through the Samples tab or the Welcome tab.

###### Viewing samples in the Welcome tab

To view samples in the Welcome tab

- Once the modeling tool is launched, in the Welcome tab, click Samples .

###### Invoking the samples tab

To invoke the samples tab

- In the Main Menu , click Help , then Show Samples .

##### Navigating content in the Welcome or Samples tabs

You can use the search bar to navigate the content in the Welcome and Samples tabs. This is especially useful for locating specific samples.

To navigate content in the Welcome or Samples tabs

1. Use the Ctrl+F shortcut keys to invoke the search bar.
2. In the Find input field, type the search keyword.
3. (Optional) Use the commands in the search bar for efficient search.

The table below displays the search bar's commands.

| Command icon | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Click to close the search bar. |
|  | Type the search keyword(s). |
|  | Click to highlight the next search result. |
|  | Click to highlight the previous search result. |
|  | Click to highlight all search results. |
|  | Select the check box to find results matching the search input letter case. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Editor tabs allow you to access diagrams and model samples.</p><p>On this page:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8559c47d-2bff-4120-903a-cf033734b14c" /></p><h3>Editor tab types</h3><p>The modeling tool contains the following editor tabs:</p><ul><li>Welcome tab.<br />The welcome tab is displayed once the modeling tool is launched. It allows you to create new or open other projects, view model samples provided with the modeling tool installation, etc.</li><li>Samples tab.<br />Displays the model samples provided with the modeling tool installation.</li><li>Diagram Editor tab. <br />It allows you to display and work with diagrams. You can have multiple diagram tabs open simultaneously.</li></ul><h3>Editor tabs commands</h3><p>Editor tabs' commands can be found:</p><ul><li>In the <strong>Main Menu</strong>, click <strong>Window</strong>, then <strong>Editor Tabs</strong>. In the dropdown menu, select the appropriate command.</li></ul><h3>Viewing samples</h3><p>You can view the model samples provided with the modeling tool installation either through the Samples tab or the Welcome tab.</p><h4>Viewing samples in the Welcome tab</h4><p>To view samples in the Welcome tab</p><hr /><ul><li>Once the modeling tool is launched, in the Welcome tab, click <strong>Samples</strong>.</li></ul><h4>Invoking the samples tab</h4><p>To invoke the samples tab</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Main Menu</strong>, click <strong>Help</strong>, then <strong>Show Samples</strong>. </li></ul><h3>Navigating content in the Welcome or Samples tabs</h3><p>You can use the search bar to navigate the content in the Welcome and Samples tabs. This is especially useful for locating specific samples.<br /><br /></p><p>To navigate content in the Welcome or Samples tabs</p><hr /><ol><li>Use the Ctrl+F shortcut keys to invoke the search bar.</li><li>In the Find input field, type the search keyword.</li><li>(Optional) Use the commands in the search bar for efficient search.</li></ol><p>The table below displays the search bar's commands.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Command icon</th><th scope="col">Description</th></tr><tr><td><div <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993247 space=MT version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 00758: (2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit

- page_id: `303993247`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993247/2026x+Refresh1+Electrical+Circuit
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:18.027+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Modelica export > (2026x Refresh1) Simulating exported Modelica models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to simulate *ElectricCircuit* example model in Dymola. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Electric Circuit sample' space='']Learn more about *ElectricCircuit* sample model >>

You can find the *ElectricCircuit* sample model in:

- the modeling tool: Welcome window > Samples > Simulink and Modelica Transformation > ElectricCircuit.
- the Installation directory: <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\*ElectricCircuit.*

To simulate the Circuit Modelica model

1. Export the Circuit Block to Modelica file. How to >>
2. Make sure the Dymola tool is installed. How to >>
3. Start the Dymola.
4. Click File > Open > Load... and select saved *Circuit.mo* file from your file directory. A*Circuit*model is displayed in the **Package Browser**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. In the **Graphics**tab toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to check model for syntax and semantic errors. The result panel displays whether the model is ready for simulation. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Setup the simulation run for the model: 
 a. Select the******Simulation**tab and in its toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. b. In the**Simulation Setup**dialog, specifythe**Start time******value to**0**and the**Stop time******value to**60**seconds(or any other reasonable amount of time; for this model, 60 seconds is enough). c. Click**OK**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. In the**Simulation** tab toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. When the simulation is completed, the output is displayed in the panel at the bottom and the *Circuit*variables are displayed in the **Variable Browser**.
8. To see input and output of the Circuit model, select variables in the Variable Browser as follows: Circuit > _Circuit > c, i, rc, ri, s > iThru , vDrop . T he current ( iThru ) and voltage ( vDrop ) variable across each of the circuit’s 5 components are displayed on the **Plot**window: the capacitor ( c ), the inductor ( i ), the voltage source ( v ), and the resistors ( rc, ri ). In the example below, only iThru , vDrop of s variable are displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='plot_diagram_s_variable.png']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993256 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00759: (2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model

- page_id: `303993256`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993256/2026x+Refresh1+Electrical+Circuit+example+to+Simulink+and+Simscape+model
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:14.522+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Simulink export > (2026x Refresh1) Simulating exported Simulink models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to simulate *ElectricCircuit* example model in Simulink. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Electric Circuit sample' space='']Learn more about *ElectricCircuit* sample model >>

You can find the *ElectricCircuit* sample model in:

- the modeling tool: Welcome window > Samples > Simulink and Modelica Transformation > ElectricCircuit.
- the Installation directory: <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\*ElectricCircuit.*

To simulate the *Electrical Circuit*model

1. Export the Circuit Block to Simulink file. How to >>
2. In the Simulink Export Options dialog select the following options:
  - **Format**: XML (.slx)
  - **S-Function or Simscape**: Simscape
  - Simscape port libraries : Create new port types
  - Composite Signals : Bus Creators/Selectors
3. Make sure the MATLAB tool is installed.
4. Double-click the MATLAB icon to start it.
5. In the file directory select the location of your exported ElectricalCircuit model Simulink files and choose the +CircuitLibrary folder. The simscape files (.ssc) are displayed in the Current Folder panel. [ATTACHMENT filename='file_directory_simulink_files_circuit.png']
6. In the Command Window type ssc_build (or sscbuild depending on the Matlab version) and press Enter . This generates a folder sscprj in the +CircuitLibrary directory as well as a CircuitLibrary_lib.slx file in the directory with the original Circuit.slx file. [ATTACHMENT filename='generating_simulink_library.png']
7. Go back to the file directory, select the ElectricalCircuit package with exported Simulink files and in the Current Folder select the Circuit.slx . [ATTACHMENT filename='current_folder_circuit_slx.png']
8. Double-click on Circuit.slx to open the Simulink/Simscape model. Rearrange the blocks to get a better picture of the blocks and links. [ATTACHMENT filename='circuit_model_simscape.png']
9. On the **SIMULATION** tab, click the arrow on the **PREPARE** area and select the **Model Settings**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. In the **Configuration Parameters** dialog, on the left side of the dialog: 
a. Select a **Solver**property group****and specify the following on the right side of the dialog: 
****-****In the the **Simulation time** group, change **Start time**to **0.0**, **Stop time**to **60**(or any other reasonable number of seconds). 
 - In the **Solver selection** group, select**Type****Variable-step** or any other desirable solver that is suitable. 
 - Click the arrow of the **Solver details** group and change the **Max step size** to **0.01**. b. Select Simscape property group and specify the following on the right side of the dialog: - In the Data Logging group, change Log simulation data to All . - Make sure Limit data points is unchecked. [ATTACHMENT filename='configuration_parameters_dialog.png']
11. Press Apply > OK .
12. In the Circuit Simulink window, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
13. Once the model has compiled, o n the main toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='simscape_results_button.png'] to open a Simscape Results Explorer : Circuit window where you can expand the Circuit blocks to see how their properties simulated through time. For the circuit components c (capacitor), i (inductor), rc (resistor in series with the capacitor), ri (resistor in series with the inductor), and s (source), the properties iThru (current) and vDrop (voltage) can be selected to see the flow of charge (current) and its potential to flow (voltage) through each component. To see the simulation’s specific data points, select the mouse pointer on the plot. [ATTACHMENT filename='simscape_results_explorer_circuit.png']
14. To change the default value or initial value parameters of the model, double-click the blocks in the Circuit Simulink model. A Block Parameters dialog opens where yoy can change parameters. Repeat steps from 5 to 12 to run the simulation with (new) block parameters. [ATTACHMENT filename='rc_block_prameters.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>This page describes how to simulate <em>ElectricCircuit</em> example model in Simulink.  <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electric Circuit sample" /><ac:link-body>Learn more about <em>ElectricCircuit</em> sample model &gt;&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92b70d06-6f5d-4d3c-aca6-61891a66027f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can f<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ind the <em>ElectricCircuit</em> sample model in:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the modeling tool: Welcome window &gt; Samples &gt; Simulink and Modelica Transformation &gt; ElectricCircuit.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">the Installation directory: </span><em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\<em>ElectricCircuit.</em></em></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To simulate the <em>Electrical Circuit </em>model</p><hr /><ol><li>Export the <em>Circuit </em>Block to Simulink file. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Simulink+export#Simulinkexport-ExportingmodeltotheSimulinkfile" rel="nofollow">How to &gt;&gt;</a></li><li>In the <strong>Simulink Export Options</strong> dialog select the following options:<br /><ul class="diff-block-target"><li><p><strong>Format</strong>: XML (.slx)</p></li><li><p><strong>S-Function or Simscape</strong>: Simscape</p></li><li><strong>Simscape port libraries</strong>: Create new port types</li><li><strong>Composite Signals</strong>: Bus Creators/Selectors</li></ul></li><li>Make sure the MATLAB tool is installed.</li><li>Double-click the MATLAB icon to start it.</li><li>In the file directory select the location of your exported<em> ElectricalCircuit</em> model Simulink files and choose the <em>+CircuitLibrary</em> folder. The simscape files (.ssc) are displayed in the <strong>Current Folder</strong> panel. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="file_directory_simulink_files_circuit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Command Window</strong> type <strong>ssc_build</strong> (or<strong> sscbuild</strong> depending on the Matlab version) and press <strong>Enter</strong>. This generates a folder <strong>sscprj</strong> in the <strong>+CircuitLibrary</strong> directory as well as a<strong> CircuitLibrary_lib.slx</strong> file in the directory with the original <strong>Circuit.slx</strong> file.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generating_simulink_library.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Go back to the file directory, select the <em>ElectricalCircuit</em> package with exported Simulink files and in the <strong>Current Folder</strong> select the <em>Circuit.slx</em>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="current_folder_circuit_slx.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Double-click on <strong>Circuit.slx</strong> to open the Simulink/Simscape model. Rearrange the blocks to get a better picture of the blocks and links.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="circuit_model_simscape.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">On the <strong>SIMULATION</strong> tab, click the arrow on the <strong>PREPARE</strong> area and select the <strong>Model Settings</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_settings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Configuration Parameters</strong> dialog, on the left side of the dialog:<br />a. Select a  <strong>Solver </strong>property group<strong> </strong>and specify the following on the right side of the dialog:<br /><strong>  </strong>-<strong> </strong>In the the <strong>Simulation time</strong> group, change <strong>Start time </strong>to <strong>0.0</strong>, <strong>Stop time </strong>to <strong>60 </strong>(or any other reasonable number of seconds).<br />  - In the <strong>Solver selection</strong> group, select<strong> Type </strong><strong>Variable-step</strong> or any other desirable solver that is suitable.<br />  - Click the arrow of the <strong>Solver details</strong> group and change the <strong>Max step size</strong> to <strong>0.01</strong>.<br /></span>b. Select <strong>Simscape</strong> property group <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">and specify the following on the right side of the dialog:<br /></span>  - In the <strong>Data Logging</strong> group, change <strong>Log simulation data</strong> to <strong>All</strong>.<br />  - Make sure <strong>Limit data points</strong> is unchecked.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="configuration_parameters_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Press<strong> Apply</strong> &gt;<strong> OK</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Circuit</strong> Simulink window, click<strong> <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="24"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Run_button_matlab.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Once the model has compiled, o</span>n the main toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="64"><ri:attachment ri:filename="simscape_results_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> to open a <strong>Simscape Results Explorer</strong>: <strong>Circuit</strong> window where you can expand the <strong>Circuit</strong> blocks to see how their properties simulated through time. For the circuit components<strong> c</strong> (capacitor),<strong> i</strong> (inductor), <strong>rc</strong> (resistor in series with the capacitor),<strong> ri</strong> (resistor in series with the inductor), and <strong>s</strong> (source), the properties<strong> iThru</strong> (current) and <strong>vDrop</strong> (voltage) can be selected to see the flow of charge (current) and its potential to flow (voltage) through each component. To see the simulation’s specific data points, select the mouse pointer on the plot.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simscape_results_explorer_circuit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>To change the default value or initial value parameters of the model, double-click the blocks in the<strong> Circuit Simulink</strong> model. A <strong>Block Parameters</strong> dialog opens where yoy can change parameters. Repeat steps from 5 to 12 to run the simulation with (new) block parameters.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="rc_block_prameters.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996885 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00760: (2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a

- page_id: `303996885`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996885/2026x+Refresh1+Electrical+Circuit+example+to+Simulink+and+Simscape+model+R2016a
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:33.020+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Simulink export > (2026x Refresh1) Simulating exported Simulink models > (2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To generate a Simulink model directly from the modeling tool

1. Right-click the *Block* **TestCaseCircuit::Circuit** and select **Tools**> **Export to Simulink.**
2. Set the options listed below and click OK :
  - Format : XML (.sdl)
  - S-Function or Simscape : Simscape
  - Simscape port libraries : Create new port types
3. Launch Matlab with Simulink and Simscape extensions.
4. In Matlab’s Current Directory navigation bar, search for the file directory where the Simulink/Simscape files (as well as the SysML v1 files) are saved, and set it as the current directory. The Current Folder panel should display the generated files. [ATTACHMENT filename='Change_Directory_in_Matlab.png']
5. In the Current Folder panel, double-click on the folder named +CircuitLibrary (this sets the current directory to this folder). Type ssc_build (or sscbuild depending on the Matlab version) in the Command Window and press Enter . This will generate a folder sscprj in the +CircuitLibrary directory as well as a CircuitLibrary_lib.slx file in the directory with the original Circuit.slx file. Next, once again, search for and go back to the file directory where the Simulink/Simscape files (as well as the SysML v1 files) are saved, and then set it as the current directory. [ATTACHMENT filename='Using_the_Ssc_Build_Command.png']
6. In the Current Folder panel, double-click on Circuit.slx to open the Simulink/Simscape model. Rearrange the blocks to get a better picture of the blocks and links. [ATTACHMENT filename='Circuit Simulink_Simscape_Diagram.png']
7. Go to Simulation > Model Configuration Settings . This allows to select the types of solver and the runtime of the simulation. Select a Start Time of 0 , and a Stop Time of 60 (or any other reasonable number of seconds). Change the Max step size to 0.01 . Under Solver Options , keep the solver Type to Variable-step (or any other desirable solver that is suitable). Press Apply . [ATTACHMENT filename='Simulink_Simscape_Model_Configuration_Settings_for_Simulation.png']
8. In Model Configuration Settings , select Simscape in the Commonly Used Parameters panel. Change Log simulation data to All . Make sure Limit data points is unchecked. Press Apply . Press OK . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. In the Circuit Simulink window, go to Simulation > Run .
10. Once the model has compiled, go to Matlab’s Command Window , type ssc_explore(simlog) (make sure that either the version of Matlab has the function ssc_explore, or a file ssc_explore.m is in the same directory as Circuit.slx in the Current Folder panel) and press Enter. A Simscape data logging explorer : Circuit window will open up. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
11. In the**Simscape data logging explorer**, the **Circuit** blocks can be expanded to see how their properties simulated through time. For the circuit components**c** (capacitor),**i** (inductor), **rc** (resistor in series with the capacitor),**ri** (resistor in series with the inductor), and **s** (source), the properties**iThru** (current) and **vDrop** (voltage) can be selected to see the flow of charge (current) and its potential to flow (voltage) through each component. To see the simulation’s specific data points, the **Data cursor** icon needs to be selected and points on the plot need to be chosen. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
12. To change the default value or initial value parameters of the model, double-click the blocks in the Circuit Simulink model. A window will pop out for block parameters that can be changed. Run through Steps 4 through 8 to run the simulation with (new) block parameters again. [ATTACHMENT filename='Changing_Block_Parameters.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To generate a Simulink model directly from the modeling tool</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Right-click the <em>Block</em> <strong>TestCaseCircuit::Circuit</strong> and select <strong>Tools </strong>&gt; <strong>Export to Simulink.</strong></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-magenta,#943d73);"><br /></span></li><li>Set the options listed below and click <strong>OK</strong>:<br /><ul><li><strong>Format</strong>: XML (.sdl)</li><li><strong>S-Function or Simscape</strong>: Simscape</li><li><strong>Simscape port libraries</strong>: Create new port types</li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Launch Matlab with Simulink and Simscape extensions.<br /></span></li><li>In Matlab’s<strong> Current Directory</strong><span> navigation bar, search for the file directory where the Simulink/Simscape files (as well as the SysML v1 files) are saved, and set it as the current directory. The </span><strong>Current Folder</strong><span> panel should display the generated files. </span><br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="650"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Change_Directory_in_Matlab.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the <strong>Current Folder</strong> panel, double-click on the folder named<strong> +CircuitLibrary</strong> (this sets the current directory to this folder). Type <strong>ssc_build</strong> (or<strong> sscbuild</strong> depending on the Matlab version) in the <strong>Command Window</strong> and press <strong>Enter</strong>. This will generate a folder <strong>sscprj</strong> in the <strong>+CircuitLibrary</strong> directory as well as a<strong> CircuitLibrary_lib.slx</strong> file in the directory with the original <strong>Circuit.slx</strong> file. Next, once again, search for and go back to the file directory where the Simulink/Simscape files (as well as the SysML v1 files) are saved, and then set it as the current directory.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="1000"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Using_the_Ssc_Build_Command.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the <strong>Current Folder</strong> panel, double-click on <strong>Circuit.slx</strong> to open the Simulink/Simscape model. Rearrange the blocks to get a better picture of the blocks and links.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Circuit Simulink_Simscape_Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Go to <strong>Simulation</strong> &gt; <strong>Model Configuration Settings</strong>. This allows to select the types of solver and the runtime of the simulation. Select a<strong> Start Time</strong> of <strong>0</strong>, and a <strong>Stop Time</strong> of<strong> 60</strong> (or any other reasonable number of seconds). Change the<strong> Max step size</strong> to <strong>0.01</strong>. Under <strong>Solver Options</strong>, keep the solver <strong>Type</strong> to <strong>Variable-step</strong> (or any other desirable solver that is suitable). Press <strong>Apply</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="800"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Simulink_Simscape_Model_Configuration_Settings_for_Simulation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In <strong>Model Configuration Settings</strong>, select<strong> Simscape</strong> in the <strong>Commonly Used Parameters</strong> panel. Change <strong>Log simulation data</strong> to <strong>All</strong>. Make sure <strong>Limit data points</strong> is unchecked. Press <strong>Apply</strong>. Press <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /><strong><ac:image ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Simulink_Simscape_,Model_Configuration_Settings_for_Simscape.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong><br /><strong><br /></strong></li><li>In the <strong>Circuit</strong> Simulink window, go to<strong> Simulation</strong> &gt;  <strong>Run</strong>.</li><li>Once the model has compiled, go to Matlab’s <strong>Command Window</strong>, type<strong> ssc_explore(simlog)</strong> (make sure that either the version of Matlab has the function <strong>ssc_explore,</strong> or a file <strong>ssc_explore.m</strong> is in the same directory as <strong>Circuit.slx</strong> in the <strong>Current Folder</strong> panel) and press Enter. A <strong>Simscape data logging explorer</strong>: <strong>Circuit</strong> window will open up.<br /><br /><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sscexplore(simlog)_Command_and_the_Simscape_Data_Logging_Explorer.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p>In the<strong> Simscape data logging explorer</strong>, the <strong>Circuit</strong> blocks can be expanded to see how their properties simulated through time. For the circuit components<strong> c</strong> (capacitor),<strong> i</strong> (inductor), <strong>rc</strong> (resistor in series with the capacitor),<strong> ri</strong> (resistor in series with the inductor), and <strong>s</strong> (source), the properties<strong> iThru</strong> (current) and <strong>vDrop</strong> (voltage) can be selected to see the flow of charge (current) and its potential to flow (voltage) through each component. To see the simulation’s specific data points, the <strong>Data cursor</strong> icon needs to be selected and points on the plot need to be chosen.</p><p><br /><ac:image ac:width="750"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Using_the_Simscape_Data_Logging_Explorer.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>To change the default value or initial value parameters of the model, double-click the blocks in the<strong> Circuit Simulink</strong> model. A window will pop out for block parameters that can be changed. Run through Steps 4 through 8 to run the simulation with (new) block parameters again.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:width="750"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Changing_Block_Parameters.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````
