If you want to specify a custom delimiter in the next box, select </span><strong>Other</strong><span>. By default, the comma (,) is selected.</span><br /><span>It is available only for CSV files.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="121"><ri:attachment ri:filename="csv_delimiter.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Import dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Identification Property</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Select the property that identifies if the imported element already exists in the model. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">It allows to avoid element duplication.</span></p><p>Select the <span><strong>Default</strong> value to identify elements by their Name and requirements by their ID. The</span> rest of the values are the same as those selected in the <strong>Select Properties to Map </strong>option but only <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Primitive" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Primitive data types]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0fc117fa-6270-4213-b0a3-6821ec11692f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If the imported element <strong>Identification Property</strong> option value matches with an already-existing element property, the identified element properties are updated.<br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by:</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a7a73fb-6e0b-488f-902a-dbb605261a9d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This option is displayed only if a<span>ny kind of direct relationship is selected as the </span><strong>Element Type</strong><span> option value.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Import dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the property from the list that identifies the source of the relationship end.</p><p>Select the <strong>Default</strong> value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select &lt;UNSPECIFIED &gt; value to create the same relationship between the same elements.</p><p>The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:</p><ul><li data-uuid="6a403344-fc48-4dd4-8bf8-3c7ceec5a26f">If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.</li><li data-uuid="b300c5b9-9ae4-4ec7-a6cf-3b370c76cb2f"><span>The mechanism identifies relationships according to<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><strong>Identification Property</strong> option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="33850164-c0b8-4f82-a024-2b21cdc8a06c"><span>If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the <strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by </strong>option value. </span></li><li data-uuid="304aaca2-2587-4870-9227-ab15cc886eab">If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">properties </span>and tags that are common for both of them.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Identify Relationship (end) Target by:</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c1989d2-3dfd-4c23-b5ac-e5bae50d230e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This option is displayed only if a<span>ny kind of direct relationship is selected as the </span><strong>Element Type</strong><span> option value.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Import dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span><span> and select the property from the list that identifies the target of the relationship end. </span>Select the <strong>Default</strong> value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select &lt;UNSPECIFIED &gt; value to create the same relationship between the same elements.</p><p>The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:</p><ul><li data-uuid="0f2d399e-460a-43bc-9296-69cb8a0ce362">If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.</li><li data-uuid="087d6451-c3d3-4881-8309-0d5d8dfe459d"><span>The mechanism identifies relationships according to<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><strong>Identification Property</strong> option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.</span><span><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="d383f97f-5667-4bc4-8c35-3528a36d3480"><span>If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the <strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by </strong>option value. </span></li><li data-uuid="af6a7837-a0df-4bd7-831f-40680c47b19a">If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">properties </span>and tags that are common for both of them.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>First Row Contains Headings </strong><span>check box</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Clear the check box </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">if your Excel/CSV file does not contain headings.</span><span> The check box is selected by default.</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Import Plain Text</strong> check box    </td><td colspan="1">Select the check box to import/export plain text only (without HTML). The check box is not selected by default.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4><br />Mapping area</h4><p>The Mapping area allows you to manage mapping between element properties <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>and Excel/CSV file columns </span></span>by creating new mappings, re-mapping, or deleting them:<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><br class="auto-cursor-target" />          -  To create a new map or re-map individually, drag columns from the left tree/right tree onto columns in the right tree/ left tree<span>.<br />          -  To create a new map or re-map all columns in the ordinal position<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995047 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00796: (2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog

- page_id: `303995047`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995047/2026x+Refresh1+Excel+and+CSV+Sync+Options+dialog
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:33.463+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Sync with Excel or CSV files
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

155935942

155935944

155935941

**On this page**

43

155935943

The **Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog allows you to specify settings before syncing data between an Excel/CSV file and a table. You can select files for syncing and define syncing and mapping options in this dialog.

##### Opening the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog

To open the**Excel/CSV Sync** **Options** dialog

- In the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='excel_csv_sync.png'] and do one of the following: - Select the Sync Options command. - Select the Excel/CSV File > Select File . Click the Details button on the left bottom corner of the dialog. The Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog opens.

##### Description of the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog areas

The **Excel/CSV Sync Options** dialog consists of the following areas:

44

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The main areas, highlighted in orange, in the Excel/CSV Sync Options dialog: select file, sync, mapping options, and mapping.

###### Select Excel/CSV file area

The Select Excel/CSV file area allows you to select the Excel or CSV file from your file system or from your model if the Excel or CSV file is attached to a project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files' space='']]]>

You can link only one Excel/CSV file with the same modeling tool table.

The Select Excel/CSV file area contains the buttons described in the following table.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | If you select From file system, the Open dialog opens. The selected file path is displayed in the Excel/CSV File field.If you select From model, the Select Excel/CSV file dialog opens. The selected attached file name is displayed in the Excel/CSV File field. |
|  | Refreshes the mapping area after changing the file. |

###### Sync Options area

The Sync Options area contains one option that allows you to specify the sync mechanism behavior when importing data to a table if rows in your file are deleted. All available values are described below.

| Sync option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| If rows in the file are deleted | Delete elements from the model - deletes the same elements that were deleted in the file from your model.If some elements are read-only in Excel file, they cannot be deleted from the model.Remove elements form the table - removes the same elements that were deleted in the file only from the modeling tool table, not from the model.Mark as obsolete (default) - The table Legend automatically displays and highlights the removed elements (obsolete) in red. |

###### Mapping Options area

The Mapping Options area allows you to specify mapping options. The following table lists all available options.

| Mapping option | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Sheet | Select the Excel file sheet you want to sync. The first sheet is selected by default.It is available only for Excel files. |
| First cell | Select the Excel file cell you want to start syncing from. The first cell filled with data is read by default. If the selected sheet is empty, it reads from the A1 cell.It is available only for Excel files. |
| CSV delimiter | Select the delimiter of the CSV file from the drop-down list. If you want to specify the custom delimiter in the next box, select Other. By default, the comma (,) is selected.It is available only for CSV files. |
| Identification Property | Select the property that identifies if the element already exists in the model. It allows to avoid element duplication.The Default value identify elements by their Name and requirements by their ID first. The rest of the values are the same as columns shown in the table of your model but only of .If the imported element Identification Property option value matches with an already-existing element property, the identified element properties are updated. |
| Identify Relationship (end) Source by: | This option is displayed only if any kind of direct relationship is selected as the Element Type option value in the .Click and select the property from the list that identifies the source of the relationship end.Select the Default value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select <UNSPECIFIED > value to create the same relationship between the same elements.The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.The mechanism identifies relationships according to Identification Property option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the Identify Relationship (end) Source by option value. If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those properties and tags that are common for both of them. |
| Identify Relationship (end) Target by: | This option is displayed only if any kind of direct relationship is selected as the Element Type option value in the .Click and select the property from the list that identifies the target of the relationship end.Select the Default value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select <UNSPECIFIED > value to create the same relationship between the same elements.The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.The mechanism identifies relationships according to Identification Property option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the Identify Relationship (end) Source by option value.If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those properties and tags that are common for both of them. |
| First row contains headings check box | Clear the check box if your Excel/CSV file does not contain headings. The check box is selected by default. |
| Sync Plain Text check box | Select the check box to import/export plain text only (without HTML). The check box is not selected by default. |

###### Mapping area

The Mapping area allows you to manage mapping between modeling tool table columns and Excel/CSV table columns by creating new mappings, re-mapping, or deleting them:- To create a new map or re-map one by one, drag columns from the left tree/right tree onto columns in the right tree/left tree. 
 - To create a new map or re-map all columns in the ordinal position, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the mapping area. 
 - To delete mapping one by one, select an appropriate map and click[IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the mapping area. 
 - To delete all mappings, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the bottom of the mapping area.

If you do not define your own mapping - that is, the mapping area is empty - the columns by default are mapped on the ordinal position.

The hashtag (#) column from the modeling tool table is not included in the mapping.

155947252

If the selected sheet is empty, it reads from the A1 cell.<br />It is available only for Excel files.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>CSV delimiter</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Select the delimiter of the CSV file from the drop-down list. If you want to specify the custom delimiter in the next box, select <strong>Other</strong>. By default, the comma (,) is selected.<br />It is available only for CSV files.<br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="121"><ri:attachment ri:filename="csv_delimiter.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Identification Property</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Select the property that identifies if the element already exists in the model. It allows to avoid element duplication.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>The </span><span><strong>Default</strong> value identify elements by their Name and requirements by their <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2186fac8-f852-4b88-bbfd-f8bd51c81c9c">ID first</ac:inline-comment-marker>. The</span><span> rest of the values </span>are the same<span> as columns shown in the table</span><span> of your model but only of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Primitive" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Primitive data types]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f620f492-819c-4f3e-867a-e5fcf8d1b4f4"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">If the imported element </span><strong>Identification Property</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> option value matches with an already-existing element property, the identified element properties are updated.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by:</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a7a73fb-6e0b-488f-902a-dbb605261a9d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span><span>This option is displayed o</span>nly if a<span>ny kind of direct relationship is selected as the </span><strong>Element Type</strong><span> option value</span> </span><span>in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Table Criteria area" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Criteria area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the property from the list that identifies the source of the relationship end.</p><p>Select the <strong>Default</strong> value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select &lt;UNSPECIFIED &gt; value to create the same relationship between the same elements.</p><p>The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:</p><ul><li>If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.</li><li><span>The mechanism identifies relationships according to<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><strong>Identification Property</strong> option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.<br /></span></li><li><span>If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the <strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by </strong>option value. </span></li><li>If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">properties </span>and tags that are common for both of them.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Identify Relationship (end) Target by:</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e82097bf-6da5-413a-bdfe-00a194dd62d6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>This option is displayed only if any kind of direct relationship is selected as the <strong>Element Type</strong> option value </span><span>in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Table Criteria area" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Criteria area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_map_groups.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the property from the list that identifies the target of the relationship end.</p><p>Select the <strong>Default</strong> value to identify relationships by their name or id. Select &lt;UNSPECIFIED &gt; value to create the same relationship between the same elements.</p><p>The mechanism identifies relationships according to the following rules:</p><ul><li>If more than one relationship is found with the same Identification Property, the first is updated.</li><li>The mechanism identifies relationships according to<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><strong>Identification Property</strong> option value first unless the Default value is selected and relationship ends are specified with particular tag or property.</li><li>If it doesn't find anything or find more than one relationship, then the mechanism proceeds to search according to the <strong>Identify Relationship (end) Source by </strong>option value.</li><li>If you import two different relationships (e.g. Satisfy and Dependency) that ends are of different types, this option allows you to select only those <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">properties </span>and tags that are common for both of them.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><strong>First row contains headings </strong>check box</td><td>Clear the check box <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">if your Excel/CSV file does not contain headings.</span> The check box is selected by default.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Sync Plain Text</strong> check box</td><td colspan="1">Select the check box to import/export plain text only (without HTML). The check box is not selected by default.</td></tr></tbody></table><h4>Mapping area</h4><p>The Mapping area allows you to manage mapping between <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">modeling tool table columns and Excel/CSV table columns</span> by creating new mappings, re-mapping, or deleting them:<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br class="auto-cursor-target" />          -  To create a new map or re-map <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">one by one, drag</span> columns from the left tree/<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">right tree</span> onto columns in the right tree/<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> left tree</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">.<br />          -  <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create a new map or re-map</span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">all columns in the ordinal position</span></span></span>, click <ac:image ac:title="Map 1:1" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="map_1_1_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">at the bottom of the mapping area</span></span></span>.<br />          -  <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">delete mapping</span> one by one</span></span></span>, select an appropriate map and c</span>lick<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <ac:image ac:title="Delete " ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Delete " ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> at the bottom of the mapping area<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">.<br />          -  <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To delete all mappings</span></span></span></span></span>, click <ac:image ac:title="Delete All" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Delete All" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_all_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">at the bottom of the mapping area</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">. <br /></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></p><p>If you do not define your own mapping - that is, the mapping area is empty - the columns by default are mapped on the ordinal position.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ded18c57-dbe0-4132-898a-917401c53c18"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The hashtag (#) column from the modeling tool table is not included in the mapping.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fb3948b1-3153-4fe5-aae3-1113b27e23d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">155947252</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><span class="current"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Sync with Excel or CSV files" /></ac:link></span></p></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><span class="current"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Table toolbars" /></ac:link></span></p></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Legends" /></ac:link></p></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><a class="current" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Excel+and+CSV+Import+dialog">Excel and CSV Import dialog</a></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00797: (2026x Refresh1) Exception

- page_id: `303999270`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999270/2026x+Refresh1+Exception

- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Debug report template

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The warning message, see the figure below, will open whenever an exception, such as IndexOutOfBoundException, occurs during the report generation.

###### Exception Warning Message.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000548 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00798: (2026x Refresh1) Exchanging data between tables and Excel or CSV files

- page_id: `304000548`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000548/2026x+Refresh1+Exchanging+data+between+tables+and+Excel+or+CSV+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:24.449+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can exchange data between the tables on your modeling tool and Excel/CSV files. Depending on the expected end result, choose the most appropriate data exchange method from those listed below:

- (import/export)
- (import/export)
- (import/export)
- (import)
- (export)
- (export)

| Data Exchange Method | Description | Key Benefits | Details |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | You can transfer information from any textual resource, including HTML, by simply copying and pasting data. Data should be in a tabular format to be pasted correctly. copy and paste | No additional tools or plugins are required.Data can be copied from external resources to modeling tool tables, from modeling tool tables to other resources, and between the cells in the modeling tool table.New elements are created in the model upon copying and pasting data from the external resource to the modeling tool table.Elements already existing in the model are automatically updated upon copying. are set automatically after pasting data into the modeling tool table. | Copy/paste destinationRowsColumns |
| Pasting data to the Excel fileTextBooleansNumbersModel elements (converted to textual data)Data in an HTML format (converted to the style used by Excel) |  |  |  |
| Pasting data to the CSV fileTextBooleansNumbersModel elements (converted to textual data)Data in an HTML format |  |  |  |
| Pasting data to the modeling tool tableTextBooleansNumbersExcel data with the applied style (converted to HTML on the modeling tool) |  |  |  |
| Pasting data to the specific table cellsTextBooleansNumbers |  |  |  |
| LimitationsMerged cells in the Excel/CSV file.Non-editable cells.If the number of columns in the Excel/CSV file and the modeling tool table does not match, some columns are left out.No data mapping.ID prefixes can only be imported/exported as plain text.Images are unsupported. |  |  |  |
|  | You can sync information in tables and Excel/CSV files using the Excel/CSV Sync feature that comes packed with the modeling tool. The mechanism is bidirectional and ensures the continuity between Excel/CSV files and modeling tool tables.excel csv sync | Excel/CSV Sync enables you to:link an existing Excel/CSV file with a modeling tool table for a quicker and more convenient data import/export.create a new Excel/CSV file directly from the modeling tool table and link it at the same time.open the linked Excel/CSV file directly from the modeling tool table.import data from a selected sheet in the Excel file into a modeling tool table.conveniently track data import status.specify sync and mapping options using the . | Import/Export destinationRowsColumnsExporting data to the Excel/CSV fileTextBooleansNumbersModel elements (converted to textual data)Data in an HTML format (converted to the style in the Excel file only)Importing data from CSV to the modeling tool tableTextBooleansNumbersExcel data with the applied style (converted to HTML on the modeling tool) |
|  |  | Data Mapping During data import, elements in the model are searched by the following criteria:stereotype tag value. It must be of a String type and have the Is ID property value set to true.name. |  |
|  |  | CustomizationPossibility to:specify the Excel/CSV file location: file system or model.specify mapping options, including the sheet number (Excel files) to import data from, the first cell to start the data import from, and the delimiter type (CSV file).display/hide row headings.specify how the modeling tool table is updated when elements are deleted from the source file, i.e., Excel/CSV.customize data mapping between Table columns and Excel/CSV file columns. |  |
|  |  | LimitationsSyncs only one data unit (table) from a single sheet at a time.Non-editable cells (data import only).Impossible to import/export relationships between elements.ID prefixes can only be imported/exported as plain text.Images are unsupported. |  |
|  | Data Hub provides a complete solution for requirement management. It can be used to copy, link to one another, and synchronize requirements with system design models, e.g., Use Cases, artifacts, and test cases. Before you can use it, you need to install it via the . data hub | Elements can be transferred to and from CSV file repositories. Supports almost all types of MagicDraw model elements. Supports external requirement management tools, including IBM® Rational® DOORS®, IBM® Rational® DOORS® Next Generation, and HP Application Lifecycle Management.Provides unidirectional and bidirectional synchronization capabilities between target and source nodes. Allows creating and maintaining DHLink and DHTrace links between requirements and SysML v1 use cases and test cases. | Exporting data to the CSV fileTextNumbersBooleansModel elements (they are converted to textual data)Importing data to the modeling tool tableTextNumbersBooleansDelimiters (indicates separate table columns)Elements in textual data (e.g., SysML v1 requirements)Each column in the CSV file represents a column in the modeling tool table.Data MappingTwo modes are used:Group type mapping mode (imported nodes are arranged into groups and can be mapped to the selected model element all at once).Individual type mapping mode (imported nodes are arranged into a tree-view structure and can be mapped to different model element types).CustomizationAttribute mapping is customizable via the .LimitationsThe Excel file is not supported by the plugin.CSV file with columns is needed before DataHub can be used to export model to the CSV file. DataHub does not support the direct data import into the table, i.e. the CSV file is imported as model elements. |
| (based on the framework)export button | Report Wizard is a report engine that supports text-based templates to generate report documents from the models. report wizard | Reports can be generated from a predefined template.The format of the generated report file depends on the selected template.Custom templates can be created (some knowledge is required).The template style can be easily formatted. For instance, you can add page numbers, headers and footers, and a table of contents.A template can be "attached" to the project to share it with other users who open the same project.You can set up an automatic report generating and uploading to a predefined remote server using the Command line (CMD). | Exporting data to the Excel fileRowsColumnsHeadingsTextModel elements (converted to textual data)The applied style, e.g., colors.ImagesData is exported using text-based templates.CustomizationPossibility to:create a new or modify existing template variables, e.g., title, author, and purpose.create a new customized template.specify the report file output location.specify how empty values will be displayed.specify the desired scope for generating a report, for example, export several tables at the same time.Five keyboard shortcuts that allow generating reports with the last used options from the most recent template are assigned automatically.Reports can be automatically generated using the Command line.LimitationsGenerates only one report at a time.No default template for the data export to the CSV file. |
| The Export button allows exporting tables from the modeling tool to a .csv or .xlsx file with a single click. | Enables to export data from the modeling tool without creating a reference, i.e., without linking the table and the exported file. | Exporting data to the ExcelData from the modeling tool table is exported using the style specified in the template file. RowsColumnsHeadingsTextModel elements (converted to textual data)The applied style, e.g., colorsImagesCustomizationPossibility to specify the specific diagram to export and the location of the exported file.Exporting data to the CSV fileTextNumbersBooleansModel elements (they are converted to textual data)LimitationsExports only one diagram at a time. |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can exchange data between the tables on your modeling tool and Excel/CSV files. Depending on the expected end result, choose the most appropriate data exchange method from those listed below: </p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link ac:anchor="copy and paste"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Copy and Paste]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (import/export)</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link ac:anchor="excel csv sync"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Sync]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (import/export)</p></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="data hub"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Data Hub]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (import/export)</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link ac:anchor="csv import plugin"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CSV Import Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (import)</p></li><li><p><ac:link ac:anchor="report wizard"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report Wizard ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>(export)</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target _mce_tagged_br"><ac:link ac:anchor="export button"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Export button]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (export)</p></li></ul><table class="relative-table" style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;width: 100.0%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 11.6905%;" /><col style="width: 17.9473%;" /><col style="width: 22.1186%;" /><col style="width: 48.2437%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Data Exchange Method</th><th>Description</th><th>Key Benefits</th><th>Details</th></tr><tr><td rowspan="6"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Copying and pasting data" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Copy and Paste]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></div></td><td rowspan="6"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>You can transfer information from any textual resource, including HTML, by simply <strong>copying and pasting</strong> data. Data should be in a tabular format to be pasted correctly. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ce23da8-516b-4d98-b011-a91e671f4213"><ac:parameter ac:name="">copy and paste</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></div></td><td rowspan="6"><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li>No additional tools or plugins are required.</li><li>Data can be copied from external resources to modeling tool tables, from modeling tool tables to other resources, and between the cells in the modeling tool table.</li><li>New elements are created in the model upon copying and pasting data from the external resource to the modeling tool table.</li><li>Elements already existing in the model are automatically updated upon copying.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Element Numbering dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ID prefixes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are set automatically after pasting data into the modeling tool table.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="id_prefix.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exchanging data between tables and Excel or CSV files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ul></div></td><td><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Copy/paste destination</strong></p><ul><li>Rows</li><li>Columns</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pasting data to the Excel file</strong></p><ul><li>Text</li><li>Booleans</li><li>Numbers</li><li>Model elements (converted to textual data)</li><li>Data in an HTML format (converted to the style used by Excel)</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pasting data to the CSV file</strong></p><ul><li>Text</li><li>Booleans</li><li>Numbers</li><li>Model elements (converted to textual data)</li><li>Data in an HTML format</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pasting data to the modeling tool table</strong></p><ul><li>Text</li><li>Booleans</li><li>Numbers</li><li>Excel data with the applied style (converted to HTML on the modeling tool)</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pasting data to the specific table cells</strong></p><ul><li>Text</li><li>Booleans<strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li><li>Numbers</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Limitations</strong></p><ul><li>Merged cells in the Excel/CSV file.</li><li>Non-editable cells.</li><li>If the number of columns in the Excel/CSV file and the modeling tool table does not match, some columns are left out.</li><li>No data mapping.</li><li>ID prefixes can only be imported/exported as plain text.</li><li>Images are unsupported.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Sync]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><br /></p></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>You can sync information in tables and Excel/CSV files using the <strong>Excel/CSV </strong><strong>Sync</strong> feature that comes packed with the modeling tool. The mechanism is bidirectional and ensures the continuity between Excel/CSV files and modeling tool tables.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="968c011e-c986-461a-98de-a82eba966635"><ac:parameter ac:name="">excel csv sync</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Excel/CSV Sync enables you to:</p><ul><li>link an existing Excel/CSV file with a modeling tool table for a quicker and more convenient data import/export.</li><li>create a new Excel/CSV file directly from the modeling tool table and link it at the same time.</li><li>open the linked Excel/CSV file directly from the modeling tool table.</li><li>import data from a selected sheet in the Excel file into a modeling tool table.</li><li>conveniently track data import status.</li><li>specify sync and mapping options using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Excel and CSV Sync Options dialog" /></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exchanging data between tables and Excel or CSV files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></div></td><td><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Import/Export destination</strong></p><ul><li>Rows</li><li>Columns</li></ul><p><strong>Exporting data to the Excel/CSV file</strong></p><ul><li>Text</li><li>Booleans</li><li>Numbers</li><li>Model elements (converted to textual data)</li><li>Data in an HTML format (converted to the style in the Excel file only)</li></ul><p><strong>Importing data from CSV to the modeling tool table</strong></p><ul><li>Text</li><li>Booleans</li><li>Numbers</li><li>Excel data with the applied style (converted to HTML on the modeling tool)<br /><br /></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /></td><td><br /></td><td><p><strong>Data Mapping </strong></p><ul><li>During data import, elements in the model are searched by the following criteria:</li></ul><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li><strong>stereotype tag value</strong>. It must be of a <em>String</em> type and have the <em>Is ID</em> property value set to <em>true</em>.</li><li><strong>name</strong>.</li></ol></li></ol></td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td><p><br /></p></td><td><p><strong>Customization</strong></p><p>Possibility to:</p><ul><li>specify the Excel/CSV file location: file system or model.</li><li>specify mapping options, including the sheet number (Excel files) to import data from, the first cell to start the data import from, and the delimiter type (CSV file).</li><li>display/hide row headings.</li><li>specify how the modeling tool table is updated when elements are deleted from the source file, i.e., Excel/CSV.</li><li>customize data mapping between Table columns and Excel/CSV file columns.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td><p><br /></p></td><td><p><strong>Limitations</strong></p><ul><li><p>Syncs only one data unit (table) from a single sheet at a time.</p></li><li>Non-editable cells (data import only).</li><li>Impossible to import/export relationships between elements.</li><li>ID prefixes can only be imported/exported as plain text.</li><li>Images are unsupported.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH" ri:content-title="Cameo DataHub Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Data Hub]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Data Hub</strong><span> provides a complete solution for requirement management. It can be used to copy, link to one another, and synchronize requirements with system design models, e.g., Use Cases, artifacts, and test cases. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Before you can use it, you need to install it via the </span><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Resource Manager" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resource/Plugin Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ce6858a8-8233-49b7-8d27-7801d1a1edd0"><ac:parameter ac:name="">data hub</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span></p></div></td><td><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Elements can be transferred to and from CSV file repositories. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Supports almost all types of MagicDraw model elements. </span></li><li>Supports external requirement management tools, including I<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">BM</span><sup>®</sup><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> Rational</span><sup>®</sup><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> DOORS</span><sup>®,</sup><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> IBM</span><sup>®</sup><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> Rational</span><sup>®</sup><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> DOORS</span><sup>®</sup><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> Next Generation, and HP Application Lifecycle Management.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Provides unidirectional and bidirectional synchronization capabilities between target and source nodes. </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Allows creating and maintaining DHLink and DHTrace links between requirements and SysML v1 use cases and test cases. </span></li></ul></td><td><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Exporting data to the CSV file</strong></p><ul><li>Text</li><li>Numbers</li><li>Booleans</li><li>Model elements (they are converted to textual data)</li></ul><p><strong>Importing data to the modeling tool table</strong></p><ul><li>Text</li><li>Numbers</li><li>Booleans</li><li>Delimiters (indicates separate table columns)</li><li>Elements in textual data (e.g., SysML v1 requirements)</li><li>Each column in the CSV file represents a column in the modeling tool table.</li></ul><p><strong>Data Mapping</strong></p><ul><li>Two modes are used:</li></ul><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li>Group type mapping mode (imported nodes are arranged into groups and can be mapped to the selected model element all at once).</li><li>Individual type mapping mode (imported nodes are arranged into a tree-view structure and can be mapped to different model element types).</li></ol></li></ol><p><strong>Customization</strong></p><ul><li>Attribute mapping is customizable via the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CDH" ri:content-title="Schema Map Manager" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><p><strong>Limitations</strong></p><ul><li>The Excel file is not supported by the plugin.</li><li>CSV file with columns is needed before DataHub can be used to export model to the CSV file. </li><li>DataHub does not support the direct data import into the table, i.e. the CSV file is imported as model elements.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard" /></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exporting tables to Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Export button]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>(based on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard" /></ac:link> framework)<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="773e8363-8987-4e91-826b-fc4b281501a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">export button</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Report Wizard</strong> is a report engine that supports text-based templates to generate report documents from the models. <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae7c9002-9fb1-4056-b4e2-67f4e82cc386"><ac:parameter ac:name="">report wizard</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li>Reports can be generated from a predefined template.</li><li>The format of the generated report file depends on the selected template.</li><li>Custom templates can be created (some <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard template language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Velocity scripting language]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> knowledge is required).</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exchanging data between tables and Excel or CSV files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li>The template style can be easily formatted. For instance, you can add page numbers, headers and footers, and a table of contents.</li><li>A template can be &quot;attached&quot; to the project to share it with other users who open the same project.</li><li>You can set up an automatic report generating and uploading to a predefined remote server using the Command line (CMD).</li></ul></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Exporting data to the Excel file</strong></p><ul><li>Rows</li><li>Columns</li><li>Headings</li><li>Text</li><li>Model elements (converted to textual data)</li><li>The applied style, e.g., colors.</li><li>Images</li><li>Data is exported using text-based templates.</li></ul><p><strong>Customization</strong></p><p>Possibility to:</p><ul><li>create a new or modify existing template variables, e.g., title, author, and purpose.</li><li>create a new customized template.</li><li>specify the report file output location.</li><li>specify how empty values will be displayed.</li><li><p>specify the desired scope for generating a report, for example, export several tables at the same time.</p></li><li>Five keyboard shortcuts that allow generating reports with the last used options from the most recent template are assigned automatically.</li><li>Reports can be automatically generated using the Command line.</li></ul><p><strong>Limitations</strong></p><ul><li>Generates only one report at a time.</li><li>No default template for the data export to the CSV file.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>The<strong> Export</strong> button allows exporting tables from the modeling tool to a<em> .csv</em> or<em> .xlsx</em> file with a single click.</p></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li>Enables to export data from the modeling tool without creating a reference, i.e., without linking the table and the exported file.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="export_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exchanging data between tables and Excel or CSV files" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></div></td><td><p><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Exporting data to the Excel</span></strong></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Data from the modeling tool table is exported using the style specified in the template file. </span></li><li>Rows</li><li>Columns</li><li>Headings</li><li>Text</li><li>Model elements (converted to textual data)</li><li>The applied style, e.g., colors</li><li>Images<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li></ul><p><strong>Customization</strong></p><ul><li>Possibility to specify the specific diagram to export and the location of the exported file.</li></ul><p><strong>Exporting data to the CSV file</strong></p><ul><li>Text</li><li>Numbers</li><li>Booleans</li><li>Model elements (they are converted to textual data)</li></ul><p><strong>Limitations</strong></p><ul><li>Exports only one diagram at a time.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991664 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00799: (2026x Refresh1) Exchanging Messages

- page_id: `303991664`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991664/2026x+Refresh1+Exchanging+Messages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:58.943+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Functional analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling interactions with Sequence Diagrams
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

372179598

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

372179600

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

372179599

#### EXCERPT: Example

INLINE

A Message defines a particular communication between [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='MED'] of an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MED'], such as raising a Signal, invoking an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MED'], or creating or destroying an Instance. Messages specify the kind of communication, the sender, and the receiver.

A Message is represented by arrows between the lifelines. The style of the Message line and arrowhead reflect the types of the Message.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] [IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can see the description of a selected property in the description area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space='']. To see descriptions, be sure the **Show Description** option is selected.

##### Message sorts

| Message Sort | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| synchCall | The Message was generated by a synchronous call to an Operation. Synchronous Messages can overtake each other. When a Message represents an Operation, the arguments of the Message must correspond to the parameters of the Operation. |  |
| asynchCall | Asynchronous means that the caller continues immediately after the call. asynchCall Messages do not have reply Messages. |  |
| asynchSignal | The Message was generated by an asynchronous send Action. The argument of the Message must correspond to the attributes of the Signal. |  |
| reply | A reply Message to an Operation call. |  |
| createMessage | The Message designates the creation of another Lifeline object. No other Messages on a given Lifeline in an Interaction operand may appear above a Lifeline. |  |
| deleteMessage | The Message designates the termination of another Lifeline. |  |

##### Message syntax

The Message name is displayed on the message path on the diagram pane. The syntax of the message name is as follows:

*<messageident> ::= [<attribute> '='] <signal or operation name> ['(' [<argument> [','*

*<argument>]* ')'] [':' <return value>]*

*<argument> ::= ([<parameter name> '='] <argument-value>) / '-'*

#### TIP: Example

Example

*v=fo(P1="a", P2=True):12*

****

**Common actions with Messages**

To set an action type for a Message

- In the Message [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] , click the Message Sort property value cell and select the action type from the drop-down list.
- On the diagram pane, right-click the Message and select a desired action type from the shortcut menu.

To show / hide Message numbers

1. Right-click the diagram pane to open its shortcut menu.
2. Then do one of the following:

- In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Sequence diagram' space=''] or Time diagram , select / clear Show Message Numbers .
- In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Communication diagram' space=''] , select / clear Numbering > Show Message Numbers .

372179596

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MED']

::= ([&lt;parameter name&gt; '='] &lt;argument-value&gt;) / '-'</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32eca1a1-94ec-4a40-9b52-7326980fbe2d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> <em>v=fo(P1=&quot;a&quot;, P2=True):12</em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;line-height: 1.5;"> <br /></strong></p><p><strong style="color: rgb(0,0,0);font-size: 16.0px;line-height: 1.5;">Common actions with Messages</strong></p><p>To set an action type for a Message</p><hr />Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>In the Message <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification window" /></ac:link>, click the <strong>Message Sort</strong> property value cell and select the action type from the drop-down list.</li><li>On the diagram pane, right-click the Message and select a desired action type from the shortcut menu.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To show / hide Message numbers</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the diagram pane to open its shortcut menu.</li><li>Then do one of the following:</li></ol><ul><li>In a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sequence ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>or <span class="confluence-link">Time diagram</span>, select / clear <strong>Show Message Numbers</strong>.</li><li>In a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Communication diagram" /></ac:link>, select / clear <strong>Numbering</strong> &gt; <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989721 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00800: (2026x Refresh1) Exchanging requirements with ReqIf

- page_id: `303989721`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989721/2026x+Refresh1+Exchanging+requirements+with+ReqIf
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:17:50.907+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Requirements are gathered and managed in dedicated requirements tools. When it comes to requirements refinement and integration with business, software, and system architecture, different requirements interchange formats are used. It can be comma separated value, MS Excel, Word, or XML. These are nonstandard ways, which bring drawbacks. It is clear that need for dedicated common format exists. This is why the German automotive industry started the open, non-proprietary format for requirements exchange development.

Starting with the upcoming v18.0, all MagicDraw-based Cameo Suite products will support ReqIF import as part of the new Cameo Requirements Modeler plugin.

##### What is ReqIF?

ReqIF is the XML-based international standard for requirement data exchange, standardized by the Object Management Group (OMG). It has solid recognition in the industry and adoption by many requirements management tool vendors. It is used to exchange requirement information between different tools and toolchains.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']“For requirement analysis, ReqIF is the same as Unified Modeling Language (UML) for modeling – it is the most popular and dedicated requirements interchange format.”

##### ReqIF Support in MagicDraw

ReqIF Importer imports and updates (previously imported) requirements in models with the following capabilities:

- Import process includes the ability for custom mapping with the option to import all data and dynamically create properties.
- Update process includes changing management support with requirements status identification. After import, new, changed, updated or obsolete requirements are identified with the ability to check the impact of changes.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once imported, requirements become first-class citizens in the modeling world. That means they can be:

- Integrated with other models: business, software, and systems architecture, test cases, and toolchains: PLM tools (e.g. Teamcenter), CAD tools (e.g. Catia), and others. This enables requirements-driven design and communication of changes with all stakeholders.
- Reviewed with visualization in diagrams, tables, matrices, and structure maps.
- Analyzed with built-in and custom validation suites, coverage metrics, traceability.
- Collaborated with a global modeling project’s repository, supporting collaboration inside a project, change and configuration management, multisite support.
- Simulated with OMG standard-based model execution, debugging, animation, and user interface prototyping supporting framework.
- Published with MS Office and Open Office docs, Web-based reports, with the ability to have custom reports incorporating required data.

##### ReqIF Importer Features

1. Import data that originated in a wide variety of tools* [ATTACHMENT filename='021714_2253_Requirement3.png'] * Import tested with: IBM Rational DOORS 9.4, 9.5, Next Generation, Polarion, PTC Integrity, Siemens TC, and other ReqIF 1.0 compatible data sources.
2. Update existing data. It is possible to create relations to any other model element, e.g. test cases, or architectural components, to realize total traceability as required by the processes. On update, all custom relations are left untouched.
3. Change status identification (updated, new, unchanged, or obsolete) [ATTACHMENT filename='status5.png']
4. Requirements structure and structure changes support [ATTACHMENT filename='Structure1.png']
5. Physical requirements remove action [ATTACHMENT filename='remove-action2.png']
6. Status and summary notification message [ATTACHMENT filename='021714_2244_Requirement7.png']
7. Custom mapping 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Dynamic properties discovery , no need to determine what data is in ReqIF file, all the properties can be imported
9. Omit the data you don’t need to import
10. Rich text support [ATTACHMENT filename='rich.png']

##### More About ReqIF

###### ReqIF Recognition

The group working on the initial release of ReqIF consists of the ProSTEP iViP Association, Atego Systems GmbH, Audi AG, BMW AG, Continental AG, Daimler AG, HOOD GmbH, IBM, MKS GmbH, PROSTEP AG, Robert Bosch GmbH, and Volkswagen AG.

###### ReqIF Sources

- Rational DOORS, DOORS Next generation, IBM Rational Requirements composer ( http://www-01.ibm.com/software/rational/ )
- Polarion ( http://www.polarion.com/2014/reqif-solution.php )
- PTC Integrity ( http://www.mks.com/platform/our-product )
- Siemens Teamcenter ( http://m.plm.automation.siemens.com/en_us/Images/Siemens-PLM-Teamcenter-Requirements-Integrator-for-RIF-ReqIF-fs_tcm1224-213684.pdf )
- LieberLieber for EA ( http://www.lieberlieber.com/en/model-engineering/reqif/ )
- Agosense ( http://www.agosense.com/english/products/platform-und-adapter/agosensesymphony )
- ReqIF Server ( http://enso-managers.de/ )
- MS Excel to ReqIF exporter ( http://www.pyrrho.com/en/ReqIF/ReqIF-Intro.htm )
- GitHub EnterpriseArchitect_ReqIF_AddIn ( https://github.com/redsteve/EnterpriseArchitect_ReqIF_AddIn )
- Requisis Rex ([https://requisis.com/de/produkte/rex-doors-reqif-rif-export.html](https://requisis.com/de/produkte/rex-doors-reqif-rif-export.html))
- VISURE ([http://www.visuresolutions.com/](http://www.visuresolutions.com/))
- Formalmind ([www.formalmind.com/](http://www.formalmind.com/))

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Requirements are gathered and managed in dedicated requirements tools. When it comes to requirements refinement and integration with business, software, and system architecture, different requirements interchange formats are used. It can be comma separated value, MS Excel, Word, or XML. These are nonstandard ways, which bring drawbacks. It is clear that need for dedicated common format exists. This is why the German automotive industry started the open, non-proprietary format for requirements exchange development.</p><p>Starting with the upcoming v18.0, all MagicDraw-based Cameo Suite products will support ReqIF import as part of the new Cameo Requirements Modeler plugin.</p><h3>What is ReqIF?</h3><p>ReqIF is the XML-based international standard for requirement data exchange, standardized by the Object Management Group (OMG). It has solid recognition in the industry and adoption by many requirements management tool vendors. It is used to exchange requirement information between different tools and toolchains.</p><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="65"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Requirements_Interchange_Format_ReqIF_Importer.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exchanging requirements with ReqIf" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>“For requirement analysis, ReqIF is the same as Unified Modeling Language (UML) for modeling – it is the most popular and dedicated requirements interchange format.”</p><h3>ReqIF Support in MagicDraw</h3><p>ReqIF Importer imports and updates (previously imported) requirements in models with the following capabilities:</p><ul><li data-uuid="6c735381-a3d1-4cc5-ae1c-c87f25f950be">Import process includes the ability for custom mapping with the option to import all data and dynamically create properties.</li><li data-uuid="734b585d-b5fe-4f81-aa37-310dbc4b9e07">Update process includes changing management support with requirements status identification. After import, new, changed, updated or obsolete requirements are identified with the ability to check the impact of changes.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="main-diagram-good.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exchanging requirements with ReqIf" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Once imported, requirements become first-class citizens in the modeling world. That means they can be:</p><ul><li data-uuid="b87d8bd9-48e6-433f-b54d-514946d3c396"><strong>Integrated</strong> with other models: business, software, and systems architecture, test cases, and toolchains: PLM tools (e.g. Teamcenter), CAD tools (e.g. Catia), and others. This enables requirements-driven design and communication of changes with all stakeholders.</li><li data-uuid="2117ebda-94bc-4b6e-b587-fdce3cd31424"><strong>Reviewed</strong> with visualization in diagrams, tables, matrices, and structure maps.</li><li data-uuid="4a324bf1-4373-4dbe-b82d-05f63d58f6a3"><strong>Analyzed</strong> with built-in and custom validation suites, coverage metrics, traceability.</li><li data-uuid="67ce5276-3a8b-4826-9c10-4e2c2e821a76"><strong>Collaborated</strong> with a global modeling project’s repository, supporting collaboration inside a project, change and configuration management, multisite support.</li><li data-uuid="cd11e1bd-6e2b-4932-b4b8-868042ba547a"><strong>Simulated</strong> with OMG standard-based model execution, debugging, animation, and user interface prototyping supporting framework.</li><li data-uuid="d0c0bf00-b955-4b56-9ba5-131935f163ef"><strong>Published</strong> with MS Office and Open Office docs, Web-based reports, with the ability to have custom reports incorporating required data.</li></ul><h3>ReqIF Importer Features</h3><ol><li data-uuid="32f5fd6f-1dd3-42f5-8491-a8ab0c34f034"><strong>Import</strong> data that originated in a wide variety of tools*<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="021714_2253_Requirement3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exchanging requirements with ReqIf" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />* Import tested with: IBM Rational DOORS 9.4, 9.5, Next Generation, Polarion, PTC Integrity, Siemens TC, and other ReqIF 1.0 compatible data sources.</li><li data-uuid="4cec91ce-f5d1-4447-bb90-74fe4db673c6"><strong>Update</strong> existing data. It is possible to create relations to any other model element, e.g. test cases, or architectural components, to realize total traceability as required by the processes. On update, all custom relations are left untouched.</li><li data-uuid="ae5ac5d8-371b-4f0e-9292-42990fac91d3"><strong>Change status</strong> identification (updated, new, unchanged, or obsolete)<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="status5.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exchanging requirements with ReqIf" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="43625ef0-c861-45b3-b2ab-49c70d9b461d"><strong>Requirements structure</strong> and structure changes support<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Structure1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exchanging requirements with ReqIf" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="87fc2c8f-ee8d-4ad9-839e-fc54c509a986"><strong>Physical requirements remove</strong> action<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove-action2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exchanging requirements with ReqIf" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="5673146d-14fc-4ccb-8a13-db88001ced11"><strong>Status and summary </strong>notification message<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="021714_2244_Requirement7.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exchanging requirements with ReqIf" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="c5a605a2-90d8-4349-8c67-cad96c818b2c"><strong>Custom mapping<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="mapping2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exchanging requirements with ReqIf" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></li><li data-uuid="aaa94928-0de1-4d0d-b2df-d9d1b37123e3"><strong>Dynamic properties discovery</strong>, no need to determine what data is in ReqIF file, all the properties can be imported</li><li data-uuid="f9c7a088-5e48-4637-ba85-b5682639e9c5"><strong>Omit the data</strong> you don’t need to import</li><li data-uuid="6331ceae-f82a-43e8-b4f1-3399aceaf41f"><strong>Rich text</strong> support<br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rich.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Exchanging requirements with ReqIf" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h3>More About ReqIF</h3><h4>ReqIF Recognition</h4><p>The group working on the initial release of ReqIF consists of the ProSTEP iViP Association, Atego Systems GmbH, Audi AG, BMW AG, Continental AG, Daimler AG, HOOD GmbH, IBM, MKS GmbH, PROSTEP AG, Robert Bosch GmbH, and Volkswagen ## PAGE 00801: (2026x Refresh1) Exclusive Gateway
````

- page_id: `303998920`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998920/2026x+Refresh1+Exclusive+Gateway
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:31.631+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Gateways
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1485706809

1485706810

1485706810

**Description**

A diverging Exclusive Gateway (Decision) is used to create alternative paths within a process flow. This is basically the diversion point in the road for a process. Only one alternative path can be taken for a given instance of the process.

A Exclusive Gateway can be thought of as a question that is asked at a particular point in the process. The question has a defined set of alternative answers. Each question is associated with two or more condition expressions associated with outgoing sequence flows of the Gateway.

A converging Exclusive Gateway is used to merge alternative paths. All incoming sequence Flows tokens will be routed to the outgoing sequence flow without synchronizing them.

There are two icons defined for an Exclusive Gateway in the BPMN2 Specification.It can be displayed with or without an internal marker.

**Notation**

- Without an Internal Marker Exclusive Gateway [ATTACHMENT filename='gateway_exclusive.png']
- An Internal Marker Exclusive Gateway [ATTACHMENT filename='gateway_exclusive_with_marker.png']

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Diverging Exclusive Gateway

1485706808

- Sequence Flow

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

The question has a defined set of alternative answers. Each question is associated with two or more condition expressions associated with outgoing sequence flows of the Gateway.</p><p>A converging Exclusive Gateway is used to merge alternative paths. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995443 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00802: (2026x Refresh1) Executable concepts

- page_id: `303995443`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995443/2026x+Refresh1+Executable+concepts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:34.276+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

We call executable such concepts that can be specified by some expressions and calculated. In modeling tools developed by No Magic, executable are:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Validation' space=''] - that are predefined or custom constraints having a purpose to validate a model or particular model elements.
- Derived properties - properties that are automatically calculated from the other properties.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart Package' space='MED'] - that are special collections of model elements.
- Executable opaque behaviors - reusable expression fragments callable form other executable concepts.
- Metric definitions - calculate aggregate parameters of models.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Macro engine' space=''] - a script that is created using a particular script language. With the help of macros, you can control items that are allowed in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Developer Guides Home' space='DEVG'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opaque Behavior' space='MED'] - a behavior with implementation-specific semantics.

In a modeling tool, executable concepts are defined in one of the languages that our modeling tool can evaluate:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Getting started with specifying criteria' space=''] - a graphical way to construct expressions out of predefined blocks.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Object Constraint Language' space='']ObjectConstraint Language .
- Binary - an expression that is written in Java, compiled and added to our modeling tool with a plugin.
- One of the following scripting languages:

- 
  - Groovy
  - JavaScript
  - JavaScript Rhino
  - Jython

#### INFO: Additional scripting languages

Additional scripting languages

Additional scripting languages can be plugged into our modeling tool, if they have JSR-223-compliant implementation.

You can test and evaluate your expressions with the help of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Expression Evaluation' space=''].

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999292 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00803: (2026x Refresh1) Exercise: Building a simple class diagram report

- page_id: `303999292`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999292/2026x+Refresh1+Exercise+Building+a+simple+class+diagram+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:45.138+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Template Creation Tutorial
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1351791524

1351791526

1351791523

**On this page**

3

1351791526

#### Building a Class Diagram Report

In this exercise, you will learn how to create a simple Class Diagram report. A Class Diagram report must contain the following information:

- A class diagram's name.
- A diagram.
- A table that describes the class (we will call it a class description table) based on the diagram shown below.
- The table must have the following structure: Class NameDocumentation:AttributesAttribute Type 1NameAttribute Type 2NameAttribute Type 3NameAttribute Type 4Name
- The layout of the report should look like the example below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The layout of a basic class diagram on the diagram pane.

###### Instructions

The first and most important step in creating a report is setting up the document and its layout.

1. Open Microsoft Word.
2. Create a table that will be used as the basic layout for a class description table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The layout for a class description table in a Word document containing the name of the class, the attributes, and the relationships.

As shown in the preceding figure, a diagram will appear at the top of the report followed by a class description table. Accordingly, each diagram on the Class diagrams list has a class description table describing each component in the diagram. Follow the next steps to create the document template:

Iterates through a list of diagrams.

1. Print the diagram and its name.
2. Get a list of class elements that are displayed in the diagram.
3. Iterate through the list of class elements.
4. Create a class description table for each class element in the list. 
 
The steps listed above contain two **#foreach** loops: the outer loop that iterates through a list of diagrams, and the inner loop that iterates through a list of Class elements. The list of diagrams in a project is represented by **$Diagram**. 
****
5. At the top of the page of the word document type: text **$Diagram** is a list of all the diagrams in a project. This means that a project will be in the list if it contains more than one type of diagram. To filter out other types of diagrams, add the **#if** directive with a conditional statement to test whether or not **$diagram** is a class diagram: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Next, you need to get all the elements contained in the diagram by using **$report**. **$report** is a standard tool containing helpful methods such as **getDiagramElements()** that returns a list of elements inside a diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. In the example above, **$eList** has been assigned with a list of Elements inside **$diagram**. Now, you need to create an inner loop that will cover the table that you created earlier, and then filter out the elements inside the diagram whose type is not Class element. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Enter the information of the class in the table by calling its attributes, etc.: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Class Name and Documentation values are obtained by referencing the name and documentation attributes of **$e**.
9. Use the **#forrow** directive as the attribute to print rows of the class attribute type (**$[att.type.name](http://att.type.name)**) and the class attribute name (**$[att.name](http://att.name)**).
10. Save the file as Rich Text Format. Open the modeling tool and draw a simple class diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] A simple class diagram.
11. Add the template to Report Wizard, and generate a class diagram report using this template. The following figure shows the result of the generated report. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The generated report of the class diagram in the Word document.

#### Glossary

- Class diagram A class diagram is a diagram in which the primary symbols in the contents area are class symbols.

#### More information about working with report templates using Report Wizard

To learn more about working with report templates, visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating a report including the Actor, Use Cases, and diagram image names' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
