The extraction functionality allows for extracting a selected part of the state machine diagram to a newly created diagram. After extraction, a submachine state is created in the source diagram in place of the extracted part(s). The submachine state is a referencing element that references the newly created state machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As you can see in the example, during the extraction, the selected part of the state machine diagram is moved to the newly created Login state machine diagram. In the source diagram, a Login submachine state is created in place of extracted elements.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996612 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00834: (2026x Refresh1) Extraction rules in Activity diagram

- page_id: `303996612`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996612/2026x+Refresh1+Extraction+rules+in+Activity+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:09.316+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Extracting > (2026x Refresh1) Extracting in Activity diagram
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

263943965

263943977

263943967

Extracting rules are the rules that should be followed in order to proceed with the correct extraction. For example, there can be invalid selections in a source diagram. Or there are unsupported elements that cannot be extracted. Without knowing the rules you will not be able to proceed with extraction (you will have to change your selected elements and/or re-select elements according to the rules) or you may lose some data. Here are the extraction rules:

###### Rule 1. Only one incoming and/or outgoing control flow is allowed for a valid diagram extraction

If the selected part of an activity diagram has two or more intersected incoming or outgoing control flows, the extraction is not valid.

Let’s analyze an example of the extraction when two incoming control flows are selected in the activity diagram as depicted in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After you start the extraction, you will be warned that only one incoming control flow and only one outgoing control flow is allowed. Click the **OK** button to close the message and then change the selected elements in the activity diagram.

###### Rule 2. The selected elements should exist in the same owner, action, or activity partition

The extraction will be invalid if:

- The selected elements do not belong to the same owner. For example, if one of the selected actions is located in a structured activity node and the other does not belong to the same structured activity node. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract_activity15.png']
- Selected elements are in different activity partitions. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract_activity17.png']

If an attempt is made to extract using an invalid selection, a warning will be displayed. Make a valid selection in order to proceed with the extraction.

###### Rule 3. The intersected Exception Handler is not extracted to a new diagram

The Exception Handler may be drawn either from an Action to an Input Pin or from an Output Pin to an Input Pin. The extraction procedure differs based on the chosen option. Let’s analyze examples when the intersected exception handler is selected in the source activity diagram.

**Extracting when the Exception Handler is drawn from an Action to an Input Pin**

1. In the source activity diagram, select an Exception Handler along with an action. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract_activity24.png']
2. Start the extraction. The following message is displayed. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Click **OK**. Extracting an action with an Exception Handler that is drawn from an Action to an Input Pin is not allowed. Select other elements to be extracted and try extracting again.

**Extracting when the Exception Handler is drawn from an Output Pin to an Input Pin**

1. In the source activity diagram, select an Exception Handler along with an action and the Output Pin. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract_activity_exception_handler_from_output_pin.png']
2. Start the extraction. The following message is displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='extract_wizard_exception_handler_from_output_pin.png']
3. Click **Yes** to continue with the extraction. See the example of the extraction result in the following figures. After the extraction, the referencing element is created in the source activity diagram in place of the selected exception handler and the *Confirm Return* action. The path of the exception handler is not represented in the new target diagram because in this case, the exception handler does not have the two symbols between which it could be added. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Click No to close the message. The extraction is canceled and you can select elements for extraction again.
- Use Undo to restore the original element and its data.

263943964

**Related pages**

- Extracting
- Extracting in Activity diagram
- Extract Activity Wizard
- Working with model elements

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7ac46fa6-9d01-406f-adcc-101d805e2c4f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">263943965</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70bd2fe5-eac8-46ba-8b47-52d9d5bca462"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">263943977</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d523c53-fb12-4941-837e-b2cccb459a1e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">263943967</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Extracting rules are the rules that should be followed in order to proceed with the correct extraction. For example, there can be invalid selections in a source diagram. Or there are unsupported elements that cannot be extracted. Without knowing the rules you will not be able to proceed with extraction (you will have to change your selected elements and/or re-select elements according to the rules) or you may lose some data. Here are the extraction rules:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fca64af9-ecb2-4d0c-b54a-6f864262ac56" /></p><h4>Rule 1. Only one incoming and/or outgoing control flow is allowed for a valid diagram extraction</h4><p>If the selected part of an activity diagram has two or more intersected incoming or outgoing control flows, the extraction is not valid.</p><p>Let’s analyze an example of the extraction when two incoming control flows are selected in the activity diagram as depicted in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_activity28.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>After you start the extraction, you will be warned that only one incoming control flow and only one outgoing control flow is allowed. Click the <strong>OK</strong> button to close the message and then change the selected elements in the activity diagram.</p><h4>Rule 2. The selected elements should exist in the same owner, action, or activity partition</h4><p>The extraction will be invalid if:</p><ul><li>The selected elements do not belong to the same owner. For example, if one of the selected actions is located in a structured activity node and the other does not belong to the same structured activity node.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_activity15.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Selected elements are in different activity partitions.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_activity17.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p>If an attempt is made to extract using an invalid selection, a warning will be displayed. Make a valid selection in order to proceed with the extraction.</p><h4>Rule 3. The intersected Exception Handler is not extracted to a new diagram</h4><p>The Exception Handler may be drawn either from an Action to an Input Pin or from an Output Pin to an Input Pin. The extraction procedure differs based on the chosen option. Let’s analyze examples when the intersected exception handler is selected in the source activity diagram.</p><p><strong>Extracting when the Exception Handler is drawn from an Action to an Input Pin </strong></p><ol><li>In the source activity diagram, select an Exception Handler along with an action.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_activity24.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Start the extraction. The following message is displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:height="165"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_activity_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />Click <strong>OK</strong>. Extracting an action with an Exception Handler that is drawn from an Action to an Input Pin is not allowed. Select other elements to be extracted and try extracting again.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><p><strong>Extracting when the Exception Handler is drawn from an Output Pin to an Input Pin</strong></p><ol><li>In the source activity diagram, select an Exception Handler along with an action and the Output Pin.<br /><ac:image ac:height="132"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_activity_exception_handler_from_output_pin.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Start the extraction. The following message is displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:height="197"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_wizard_exception_handler_from_output_pin.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>Click <strong>Yes</strong> to continue with the extraction. See the example of the extraction result in the following figures. After the extraction, the referencing element is created in the source activity diagram in place of the selected exception handler and the <em>Confirm Return</em> action. The path of the exception handler is not represented in the new target diagram because in this case, the exception handler does not have the two symbols between which it could be added.<br /><ac:image ac:height="243"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_activity_exception_handler_from_output_pin_final.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Extraction rules in Activity diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b6d8b6c-21e5-4cd6-9619-c6bedce3bde9"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Click <strong>No</strong> to close the message. The extraction is canceled and you can select elements for extraction again.</li><li>Use <strong>Undo</strong> to restore the original element and its data.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3bd0f00c-76ce-4791-80ca-acf5b7795522"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">263943964</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Extracting">Extracting</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Extracting+in+Activity+diagram">Extracting in Activity diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Extract+Activity+Wizard">Extract Activity Wizard</a></li><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Working+with+model+elements">Working with model elements</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995745 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00835: (2026x Refresh1) FAQs

- page_id: `303995745`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995745/2026x+Refresh1+FAQs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:59.387+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1208381210

1208381231

1208381209

**On this page**

3

1208381221

##### Generating an output report document from Report Wizard

To generate an output report document from Report Wizard

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select the template or create a new one. Click Next .
3. Specify Report Data and add information for the variables. Click Next .
4. Select the document scope from the corresponding packages. Click Next .
5. Select the output location, images format, and text for blank fields.
6. Click Generate .

##### Adding a new report template

To add a new report template

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Click New . The New dialog will open.
3. Enter the template name and description. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='button-1.png'] button to specify the template file location.
4. Click Create .

Once a new template has been created, Report Wizard will add a folder with the entered template name and save the selected template in this folder.

##### Removing a template

To remove a template

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template from the list and click Delete .

Once removed, the selected template with its folder and reports cannot be recovered.

##### Modifying a template file

To modify a template file

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template from the list and click Open . The default editor and a template file for editing will open.
3. Modify the template and perform the save command in the editor.

A different editor will be used for each template format. For example, MS Word could be used for **.rtf* template modification, or Macromedia Dreamweaver could be used for **.html* template editing.

##### Adding **Report Data** into the template

To add **Report Data** into the template

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template or create a new one. Click Next . The Select Report Data pane will appear.
3. Click New . The New dialog will appear.
4. Enter the Report Data name and description . Click Create . A new Report Data will be created. In the next step, you may add new fields or delete the existing ones.

##### Removing **Report Data** from the template

To remove **Report Data** from the template

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template or create a new one. Click Next . The Select Report Data pane will appear.
3. Select the Report Data from the list and click Delete .

##### Setting the default viewer option for the report file

To set the default viewer option for the report file

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template and Report Data and specify the variables and package scope. Click the Next button to proceed.
3. At the last wizard step, select the Display in viewer after generating report check box. The report output will be displayed in the default editor or browser.

##### Changing an output image format

To change an output image format

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template and Report Data and specify the variables and package scope. Click the Next button to proceed.
3. At the last wizard step, select the output image format from the Report Image Format box.

The supported image formats include:

****.PNG*** and ****.JPG*** 
for all supported report templates. 
****.SVG*** 
for *HTML, XML* and Text report templates. 
****.EMF*** and ****.WMF*** 
for *RTF, OOXML* and *Text* report templates

##### Changing an empty value configuration

To change an empty value configuration

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template and Report Data and specify the variables and package scope each time.Click the Next button to proceed.
3. At the last wizard step, select an option for output on blank fields:

- 
  - Select Display empty value or Display value as , and select NA or
  - Enter the text to represent other than null value when the template query fields return empty.

##### Adding a new variable

To add a new variable

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template and Report Data . Click the Next button to proceed.
3. Click the New button when the Variable pane opens. The Variables dialog will open.
4. Enter the name of a new variable and its value (the value can be modified later after the variable has been created).
5. Click Create .

##### Deleting a variable

To delete a variable

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template and Report Data . Click the Next button to proceed.
3. Select a field and click Delete when the Variable step opens.

You cannot recover any deleted **Report Data**.

##### Modifying a variable

To modify a variable

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template and Report Data . Click the Next button to proceed with the steps.
3. Select a field and modify its value when the Variable step opens. The value can be modified in the properties list or in the Field Value box below the properties list.

##### Selecting a package for report generation

To select a package for report generation

1. On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will open.
2. Select a template and Report Data and specify the variables. Click the Next button to proceed.
3. The package tree will open. Select the package of the project and click the Add button to add the elements from the package to the report.

Clicking the **Add** button will add only the selected element, not its children, to the report scope. In order to include all children, click the**Add Recursively** button instead.

##### Generating a normal text output format

To generate a normal text output format

1. Create the query in the text file using the text editor; see the figure below. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Entering Query in the Normal Text Template.
2. Create a new template in the **Report Wizard** dialog through the **New Template** dialog; see figure below. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating New Text Template.
3. Select Text Template, see the first figure below, and generate the output report; see the second figure below. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Selecting Text Template to Generate Report Output. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Text Output Report.

##### Calculating formulas on exported XLSX file

If formulas are used in a template file, Excel will not automatically calculate them when the file is opened. It is caused by a limitation of the Velocity language, as the engine is not designed to work with Excel files. See the options below for fixing this issue.

To calculate formulas on exported XLSX file

- A single formula is calculated when the text field is double-clicked.
- Search and Replace triggers the formula's recalculation. Press Ctrl+H, search for "=" and replace it with any symbol that is not used in the file, e.g., "[". Then, after replacing the symbols back, all formulas are recalculated.

Press Ctrl+H, search for &quot;=&quot; and replace it with any symbol that is not used in the file, e.g., &quot;[&quot;. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995253 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00836: (2026x Refresh1) Favorites

- page_id: `303995253`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995253/2026x+Refresh1+Favorites
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:24.429+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can mark any element as a favorite, including diagrams, types, packages, etc., that you regularly use in your model and need to access quickly.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Using favorites saves time, as you are able to:

- Navigate quickly to any desired place in your model.
- Easily narrow the search scope to get rid of unneeded elements and thus find desired elements faster.
- Easily narrow the scope for model analysis.

In a server project, each user has his own list of favorites.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can mark any element as a favorite, including diagrams, types, packages, etc., that you regularly use in your model and need to access quickly.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Favorites in Containment tree" ac:alt="Favorites in Containment tree"><ri:attachment ri:filename="favorites.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Favorites" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Using favorites saves time, as you are able to:</p><ul><li>Navigate quickly to any desired place in your model.</li><li>Easily narrow the search scope to get rid of unneeded elements and thus find desired elements faster.</li><li>Easily narrow the scope for model analysis.</li></ul><p>In a server project, each user has his own list of favorites.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcbe4679-96d1-43f2-9c89-c1f9d66303ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="bf1d5e01-c2e6-4371-82ba-c9e56160387e" /> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998107 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00837: (2026x Refresh1) Features and layout

- page_id: `303998107`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998107/2026x+Refresh1+Features+and+layout
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:57.287+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Generating Reports from Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Web Publisher 2.0 report
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1335772202

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1335772203

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1335772203

The Web Publisher 2.0 report consists of four panels:

1. Containment panel - This panel contains two tabs: Containment and Diagrams . The Containment tab shows data of a project in a tree structure, and the Diagrams tab shows all diagrams in a project. In each tab, there are four toolbar buttons as follows.
  - Collapse All : Collapses all expanded elements in the active tab.
  - Collapse Selected Recursively : Collapses all child elements of the selected element in the active tree recursively.
  - Open In New Tab : Opens a new tab of the specific element. When you select an element in the tab and click this button, the selected element and its children will be listed as a new tree recursively in a new tab.
  - **Favorite**: Navigates to the favorite elements listed in this menu. When you select a favorite element, you will be navigated to that favorite element in the active tab. NoteIf there is no favorite element, the **Favorite** button will be hidden.
2. Content panel - This panel shows an element's content.
3. Search panel - This panel contains a Quick Search box. You can search for an element in a project by either typing in a specific keyword or by using a regular expression as a keyword.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Web Publisher 2.0.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Containment tab.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Diagrams tab.

The Content panel of Web Publisher contains three tabs: Diagram, Specification, and Appears in. You can click any element in the Containment tree of Web Publisher to open three tabs.

- The Diagram tab shows diagram images.
- The Specification tab shows elements specification.
- The Appears in tab shows you all diagrams in which a particular element is present. You need to select the element in the Containment tree to open the Appears in tab.

| Tab name | UI component |
| --- | --- |
| Diagram |  |
| Specification |  |
| Appears in |  |

1335772201

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Web Publisher 2.0' space='']

You need to select the element in the Containment tree to open the <strong>Appears</strong> in tab.</li></ul><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Tab name</th><th><div class="content-wrapper"><p>UI component</p></div></th></tr><tr><td>Diagram</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Diagramtab_WP.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Features and layout" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td></tr><tr><td>Specification</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Specification Row.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Features and layout" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td>Appears in</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Appears In Row.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Features and layout" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d611e69-c60c-48ff-b85c-3da10d80d09e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1335772201</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Web Publisher 2.0" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997746 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00838: (2026x Refresh1) File tab

- page_id: `303997746`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997746/2026x+Refresh1+File+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:43:25.570+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Manage Navigation > (2026x Refresh1) Defining hyperlinks > (2026x Refresh1) Edit Hyperlink dialog
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use the**File** to add, edit, or delete hyperlinks to files on your file system.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']An example of the File tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog. The text "SysML specification" links to the SysML specification pdf document from your computer.

The table below describes the boxes and buttons found in the **File** tab.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Text to display box | Shows the text to be displayed as a hyperlink. If you select the text before opening this dialog, you will see that text in this box. If you change the displayed text in this box, it will be changed in the HTML text field, too. If this field is empty, you can type a text and it will be added in the HTML text field.It is available only when adding a hyperlink on text. |
| Type the file name box | Allows typing or pasting the directory to reference to an appropriate file, or click next to the box to select it from your file system. |
| Select from list box | Displays all previously used directories. You can select them to fill in the Type the file name box. |
|  | Opens the the structure of your file system. |
| The Clear button | Deletes all previously used directories from the Select from list box. |
| The Active check box | Activates the hyperlink if selected. It is available only when adding a hyperlink on an element or entire diagram.Active check box ruleOnly one hyperlink can be active. By default the last added hyperlink is active. |

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Defining hyperlinks' space='']

- Managing hyperlinks
- Linking model elements to OSLC resources

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00839: (2026x Refresh1) File tool

- page_id: `303998282`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998282/2026x+Refresh1+File+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:18.984+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Helper tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1267293507

1267293511

1267293503

**On this page**

33

1267293511

$file allows generating an output report file in a template file.

##### **$file.silentCreate(templateFilename)**

A shortcut to create a file, the output filename of which is the template name, not an import context object. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| Return | - | void |  |

##### **$file.silentCreate(attachedFile)**

A shortcut to create a file, the output filename of which is the template name, not an import context object. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| Return | - | void |  |

##### **$file.silentCreate(templateFilename, importObject)**

A shortcut to create a file, the output filename of which is the template name. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| importObject | java.lang.Object | An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data. |  |
| Return | - | void | - |

##### **$file.silentCreate(attachedFile, importObject)**

A shortcut to create a file, the output filename of which is the template name. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| importObject | java.lang.Object | An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data. |  |
| Return | - | void | - |

##### **$file.silentCreate(templateFilename, outputFileName, importObject)**

Generate a report output from a given template name. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| outputFileName | java.lang.String | The output filename. |  |
| importObject | java.lang.Object | An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data. |  |
| Return | - | void | - |

##### **$file.silentCreate(attachedFile, outputFileName, importObject)**

Generate a report output from a given template name. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| outputFileName | java.lang.String | The output filename. |  |
| importObject | java.lang.Object | An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data. |  |
| Return | - | void | - |

##### **$file.silentCreate(templateType, templateFilename, outputname, importObject)**

Generate a report output from a given template name. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateType | java.lang.String | A template type such as rtf, html, or htm. |
| templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |  |
| outputFileName | java.lang.String | An output filename without a filename extension. |  |
| importObject | java.lang.Object | An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data. |  |
| Return | - | void | - |

##### **$file.silentCreate(templateType, attachedFile, outputname, importObject)**

Generate a report output from a given template name. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateType | java.lang.String | A template type such as rtf, html, or htm. |
| attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |  |
| outputFileName | java.lang.String | An output filename without a filename extension. |  |
| importObject | java.lang.Object | An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data. |  |
| Return | - | void | - |

##### **$file.create(templateFilename)**

A shortcut to create a file, the output filename of which is the template name, not an import context object. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error. |

| importObject | java.lang.Object | An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(templateFilename)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be given the template filename. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(attachedFile)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be given the template filename. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(templateFilename, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be named after the template filename. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return |  | java.lang.String |  |

##### **$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be named after the template filename. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return |  | java.lang.String |  |

##### **$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. "importer" will be used as the name of a context value. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return |  | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. "importer" will be used as the name of a context value. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return |  | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, ContextName, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| contextName | java.lang.String | Add an object context's name. |  |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, ContextName, contextValue)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| contextName | java.lang.String | Add an object context's name. |  |
| contextValue | java.lang.Object | An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, context)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | templateFilename | java.lang.String | The templateFilename parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| context | java.util.Map<String, Object> | An additional context added to a new template. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, context)**

Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | attachedFile | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An Attached File Element. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output report filename. |  |
| context | java.util.Map<String, Object> | An additional context added to a new template. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path to generate a file. |

##### **$file.copy(inputFilename)**

Copy an input file to an output file using the same name in the binary format. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | inputFilename | java.lang.String | An input filename |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error. |

****

##### **$file.copy(inputFilename, outputFilename)**

Copy an input file to an output file in the binary format. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | inputFilename | java.lang.String | An input filename. |
| outputFilename | java.lang.String | An output filename. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error. |

****

##### **$file.exists(pathname)**

Test if a file denoted by a specific pathname exists. By default, the current directory refers to a template location. For example:

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | pathname | java.lang.String | A pathname string. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | True if and only if a file or directory denoted by a specific pathname exists; otherwise, false. |

****

##### **$file.computeName(directory, name)**

Create a pathname string from a given directory and name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | directory | java.lang.String | A parent directory of a file. |
| name | java.lang.String | A filename (excludes the filename extension). |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A pathname from the given directory and name. |

For example:

```text

```

##### ****

##### **$file.computeName(directory, name, fileType)**

Create a pathname string from a given directory, name, and file type.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | directory | java.lang.String | A parent directory of a file. |
|  | name | java.lang.String | A filename (excluding the extension). |
|  | fileType | java.lang.String | A file type, such as rtf, txt, and html. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A pathname from the given directory, name, and file type. |

For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1bb8a7a4-4050-4de9-94ad-78b62e4c54ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.create('overview.html','overview.html','')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 111.0px;" /><col style="width: 132.0px;" /><col style="width: 125.0px;" /><col style="width: 666.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong><br /><br /></td><td>templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>outputFileName </td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output filename. </td></tr><tr><td> importObject</td><td> java.lang.Object</td><td>An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td> java.lang.String</td><td>An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.create(attachedFile, outputFileName, importObject)</strong></h3><p>Generate a report output from a given template name. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0972e03f-63c4-4b89-9eae-d4aedddbf427"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.create($attachedFile,'overview.html','')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong><br /><br /></td><td><p>attachedFile</p></td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>outputFileName </td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output filename. </td></tr><tr><td>importObject</td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.create(templateType, templateFilename, outputname, importObject)</strong></h3><p>Generate a report output from a given template name. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9ac4eda-1a9a-459f-9043-677cdd80e7ca"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.create('html','overview','overview','')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong><br /><br /><br /></td><td> templateType</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A template type such rtf, html, and htm. </td></tr><tr><td> templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td> outputname</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output filename without a filename extension.</td></tr><tr><td> importObject</td><td> java.lang.Object</td><td>An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.create(templateType, attachedFile, outputname, importObject)</strong></h3><p>Generate a report output from a given template name. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3d16afed-26cc-466a-93d3-ddc2b00e1b11"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.create('html',$attachedFile,'overview','')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong><br /><br /><br /></td><td>templateType</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A template type such rtf, html, and htm. </td></tr><tr><td style="text-align: left;"><p>attachedFile</p></td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>outputname</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output filename without a filename extension.</td></tr><tr><td>importObject</td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>An object reference, which will be $importer in the template file. You can use the $importer variable in the template file to get data.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output pathname. It will return an empty string if there is an error.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFilename)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be given the template filename. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b399fb7-8d35-4ec3-8603-3dddfbba447d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td> templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path <br />to generate a file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be given the template filename. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57f32d7f-5374-4aad-95fb-0bae58b41316"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td><p>attachedFile</p></td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to generate a file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFilename, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be named after the template filename. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1bb8a654-eab2-47f6-ad09-f7696141b25e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td> templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td> contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td><br /></td><td>java.lang.String</td><td><br /></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. The generated report will be named after the template filename. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4cc32ce4-9e74-4469-afb8-d6949a127b29"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile,$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td style="text-align: left;">attachedFile</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td><br /></td><td>java.lang.String</td><td><br /></td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. &quot;importer&quot; will be used as the name of a context value. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7c477362-e675-4790-ac30-9226ac65ca7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html','overview.html',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s)   </strong></td><td>templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>outputFilename </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td>contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return </strong></td><td><br /></td><td><span>java.lang.String</span> </td><td>An absolute path to generate a file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. &quot;importer&quot; will be used as the name of a context value. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="156632f6-c44c-41b0-ac55-86b60402559c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile,'overview.html',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s)   </strong></td><td><p>attachedFile</p></td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>outputFilename </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td>contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return </strong></td><td><br /></td><td><span>java.lang.String</span> </td><td> An absolute path to generate a file.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, ContextName, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7eedfdf7-a0a6-44fd-a99e-252291e3e3e5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html','overview.html','var',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s)    </strong></td><td> templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td> outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td> contextName</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>Add an object context's name.</td></tr><tr><td> contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to generate a file. </td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, ContextName, contextValue)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="89bf2ffa-199a-4dba-816c-644b91b0e25d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile,'overview.html','var',$var)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s)    </strong></td><td style="text-align: left;">attachedFile</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td>contextName</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>Add an object context's name.</td></tr><tr><td>contextValue</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>An additional object added to a new template context with a default $importer name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to generate a file. </td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(templateFileName, outputFileName, context)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3ee3f106-bc69-4f6b-a535-0ee7556a25e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait('overview.html','overview.html',$map)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong> Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td>templateFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><ul><li>The <strong>templateFilename</strong> parameter can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, the filename would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td> outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output report <br />filename.</td></tr><tr><td> context</td><td>java.util.Map&lt;String, Object&gt; </td><td>An additional context <br />added to a new template. </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to <br />generate a file. </td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.createAndWait(attachedFile, outputFileName, context)</strong></h3><p>Open a new template engine to generate a report and return a path to generate the file. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29501624-dafa-48c3-8c3e-775bc804ebef"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.createAndWait($attachedFile,'overview.html',$map)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong> Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td style="text-align: left;">attachedFile</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An Attached File Element.</td></tr><tr><td>outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output report filename.</td></tr><tr><td>context</td><td>java.util.Map&lt;String, Object&gt; </td><td>An additional context added to a new template. </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An absolute path to generate a file. </td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$file.copy(inputFilename)</strong></h3><p>Copy an input file to an output file using the same name in the binary format. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="625ab104-2416-42d6-9de4-a9ba3ed8e694"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.copy('icon.gif')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td> inputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An input filename </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output pathname. <br />It will return an empty <br />string if there is an error. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$file.copy(inputFilename, outputFilename)</strong></h3><p>Copy an input file to an output file in the binary format. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24c27b18-a4f5-4e27-b697-be8abcbb628f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ $file.copy('icon.gif','icon.gif')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td> inputFilename </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An input <br />filename. </td></tr><tr><td> outputFilename</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An output <br />filename. </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>An output pathname. <br />It will return an empty <br />string if there is an error.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$file.exists(pathname)</strong></h3><p>Test if a file denoted by a specific pathname exists. By default, the current directory refers to a template location. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="314ef9bc-4803-426d-abd4-b76ea31633b5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.exists("$template.resourcesLocation/myimage.png")
````html
$file.exists("C:/myfolder/myimage.png")
$file.exists("mytemplate.txt")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>pathname </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A pathname string. </td></tr><tr><td><strong> Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>True if and only if a file <br />or directory denoted by <br />a specific pathname exists; <br />otherwise, false. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$file.computeName(directory, name)</strong></h3><p>Create a pathname string from a given directory and name.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"> Parameter(s) </td><td>directory </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A parent directory <br />of a file. </td></tr><tr><td> name</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A filename (excludes <br />the filename extension).</td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A pathname from the <br />given directory and name.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f0d0e6c-2f43-4bb7-8592-7b1e84f8e817"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$file.computeName('actors', $ac.ID, 'html')
output: 'actor/_123456789.html']]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><strong><br /></strong></h3><h3><strong>$file.computeName(directory, name, fileType)</strong></h3><p>Create a pathname string from a given directory, name, and file type.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>directory </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A parent directory <br />of a file. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>name</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A filename (excluding <br />the extension).</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>fileType  </td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A file type, such as rtf, <br />txt, and html. </td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A pathname from the <br />given directory, name, <br />and file type. </td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:49.076+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Code engineering
- labels: ['supported-languages', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Code can be generated out of prepared UML models. Mapping should be implemented between the identifiers used in the UML model and the language to which the model is being generated. This mapping includes the following sections:

- Built-in types (their default values)
- Generalization types
- Possible class declarations. Attributes and operations declaration and visibility modifiers
- Code generation options.

A separate *prop* file is created for every language supported in the modeling tools developed by us. Files are located in the *<modeling tool installation directory>\data* folder. The file name pattern is *lang.prop*, where lang stands for the name of the programming language.

The following table lists supported languages:

| Supported Language | File of Properties |
| --- | --- |
| DDL | ddl.prop |

Files of language properties are separated into sections where all related elements are grouped. You can edit existing entities, add new ones, and change the default values.

We strongly recommend that you edit default values only. In general, all sections have a list of possible and default values for the element.

**Related Pages**:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Code engineering' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span>Code can be generated out of prepared UML models. Mapping should be implemented between the identifiers used in the UML model and the language to which the model is being generated. This mapping includes the following sections:</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="13bcf5ec-cdb1-4024-ac22-e6eef5675d2a">Built-in types (their default values)</li><li data-uuid="713711c8-2912-47cf-821d-c01dc7b30850">Generalization types</li><li data-uuid="c9aa93c8-b116-47bc-8b50-ff1b43470b4e">Possible class declarations. Attributes and operations declaration and visibility modifiers</li><li data-uuid="2c880a54-8a40-4b5a-bd46-cf10d685bce8">Code generation options.</li></ul><p><span>A separate <em>prop</em> file is created for every language supported in the modeling tools developed by us. Files are located in the <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\data</em> folder. The file name pattern is <em>lang.prop</em>, where lang stands for the name of the programming language.</span></p><p>The following table lists supported languages:</p><table><colgroup class=""><col class="" /><col class="" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Supported Language</th><th>File of Properties</th></tr><tr class=""><th><strong>DDL</strong></th><td>ddl.prop</td></tr></tbody></table><p>Files of language properties are separated into sections where all related elements are grouped. You can edit existing entities, add new ones, and change the default values.</p><p>We strongly recommend that you edit default values only. In general, all sections have a list of possible and default values for the element.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Related Pages</strong>:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="78c3af0d-e770-4041-bca6-0f5573937396"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Code engineering" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993125 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00841: (2026x Refresh1) Filling in SOV-4a table

- page_id: `303993125`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993125/2026x+Refresh1+Filling+in+SOV-4a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:49.353+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF. SOV-4a Service Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To fill in a SOV-4a table, do one of the following

- Add a new Service Policy by clicking the Add New button and select a constrained Service Interface. Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example, English or OCL). Click the intersection again and the relation will be deleted.
- Add an Existing Service Policy by clicking the Add Existing button and select a Service Policies.

- You can add or edit constrained elements for every row in the table.
- You can remove Rows (Service Policy) from the model or only from the table, order and export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on the cell to open more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To fill in a SOV-4a table, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>Add a new Service Policy by clicking the Add New button and select a constrained Service Interface. Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example, English or OCL). Click the intersection again and the relation will be deleted.</li><li>Add an Existing Service Policy by clicking the Add Existing button and select a Service Policies.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="046f2bc2-6273-43fd-b42b-7562bc642176"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can add or edit constrained elements for every row in the table.</li><li>You can remove Rows (Service Policy) from the model or only from the table, order and export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on the cell to open more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994861 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00842: (2026x Refresh1) Filling the table with elements

- page_id: `303994861`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994861/2026x+Refresh1+Filling+the+table+with+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:50.539+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Generic table
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can fill the Generic Table for the specified Element Type in the following ways:

43

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing data from Excel and CSV files' space='']

##### Creating new elements

At least one element type must be selected in the **Element Type** box before adding a new element.

To create a new element

1. Click the **Add New** button on the Edit toolbar. If there is more than one element type selected, a submenu with the list of available element types opens. Only element types available to create in a possible diagram owner which contains the Generic Table will be displayed on the submenu.
2. Select an element type. The element of the selected type will be added to the last row of the table and to the model.
3. Name the new element in the table.

##### Adding existing elements from the model

Elements you want to add should correspond to selected element types.

To add an existing element from the model

1. Click the Add Existing button on the Edit toolbar. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting elements' space='']**Select Element** dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='gt_select_elements.png']
2. Select the element you want to add to the Generic Table. This element and its name will be added to the table as the last row. Use the **Multiple Selection**mode to add more than one element at a time.

If you have not defined the element type before starting to add elements to the table, it is automatically set after the first element is added to the table. The type of this element becomes the value of the **Element Type** box.

To add an existing element, you can drag a selected element to the contents of the Generic Table directly from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space='']. 
If you drag a container, for example, a package or a smart package, the Generic Table will display its contents, but not the container itself.

##### Copying and pasting elements from another resource

You can copy the data from other sources such as HTML, Excel, Word, and paste it to the Generic Table. The main rules for successful copy-and-paste operations in Generic Tables are described on the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Copying and pasting data' space=''] page. The copy-and-paste operations create new elements or update already existing elements in the Generic Table.

To create elements in the table by pasting data from another resource

1. Copy a table from another resource. The number of columns in that table should be the same as the number of columns in the Generic Table.The data types of columns in the Generic Table must be compatible with copied information.
2. Open the Generic Table.
3. In the Criteria area, click [ATTACHMENT filename='three-dot-btn1.png'] to specify the Element Type.
4. In the table toolbar, click the **Show Columns** button and choose the columns that you want to fill with the copied information. The cells must be editable in the Generic Table.
5. Do one of the following: 
 - Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). 
 - In the main toolbar, select **Edit** > **Paste.** 
Each row displays one element with its properties. If the element already exists in the model, the information updates.If elements have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set only for pasted elements in the Generic Table after the copy-and-paste command. Manage ID prefixes in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Element Numbering dialog' space='']**Element Numbering** dialog. The following example demonstrates the main steps for pasting data into a Generic Table. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
