<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995013 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00843: (2026x Refresh1) Filling the table with instances

- page_id: `303995013`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995013/2026x+Refresh1+Filling+the+table+with+instances
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:59.617+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Instance table
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following outlines how to fill the Instance Table for the specified classifier:

- 
- 
- 
- [ATTACHMENT filename='instance_table_example_two_classifiers.png']

create_new_instanceTo create a new instance

1. On the table toolbar, click the **Create** button. The new instance can be created only if the classifier is already specified for the Instance Table. Otherwise, the **Create** button is not available. If there is more than one classifier specified, from the menu of the button, select a classifier for which you need to create an instance. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The new instance is added to the Instance Table as the last row.
2. Type the instance name and specify slot values.

create_new_with_partsTo create a new instance with parts

1. On the table toolbar, click **Create**>**Create with Parts**. The new instance with nested parts is created if:the classifier is specified for the Instance Table. Otherwise, the **Create with Parts** button is not available.the in the Instance Table.

add_existing_instancesTo add an existing instance

1. On the table toolbar, click Create > Add Existing . The Select Instance Specification dialog opens.
2. In the tree or list on the left side of the dialog, select an element and add it to the Selected elements list. You can select more than one element. Elements available for selection:
  - Instances of one or more classifiers specified for the table. An instance is available if it belongs to at least one of the classifiers.
  - Packages that contain these instances. In this case, the contents of a selected package, but not the package itself, will be added to the table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. 
The selected instance is added to the table as the last row. You can drag a selected element to the contents of the Instance Table directly from the Model Browser.To select adjacentelements, select a single element and then hold down the Shift key while you click other elements.To select nonadjacentelements, select a single element and then hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements.

copy_instancesTo copy and paste instances from another resource

1. Copy the content of the table from another resource. The number of columns in that table should be the same as the number of columns in the Instance Table.The data types of columns in the Instance Table must be compatible with copied information.
2. Open the Instance Table where you want to paste the copied data.
3. Make sure the Classifier is specified in the Criteria area.
4. In the table toolbar, click the **Columns** button and choose columns of instance slot values that you want to fill with copied information. The cells must be editable in the Instance Table.
5. Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). 
Each row displays one instance with its slot values. If an instance already exists in the model, the information updates.If instances have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set after the copy-and-paste command only for pasted instances in the Instance Table. Manage ID prefixes in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Element Numbering dialog' space='']**Element Numbering** dialog. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The following outlines how to fill the Instance Table for the specified classifier:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="create_new_instance"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create new instances.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="create_new_with_parts"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create new instances with parts.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="add_existing_instances"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add already existing instances.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="copy_instances"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Copy and paste instances from another resources (HTML, Excel, Word, etc.).]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br /><ac:image ac:title="Instance Table filled with instances of two different classifiers" ac:alt="Instance Table filled with instances of two different classifiers"><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_example_two_classifiers.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filling the table with instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="444e4d94-a797-41cf-8913-1ad0808846ef"><ac:parameter ac:name="">create_new_instance</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To create a new instance</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the table toolbar, click the <strong>Create</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2571bcca-245f-4d62-801e-feb092543c8a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The new instance can be created only if the classifier is already specified for the Instance Table. Otherwise, the <strong>Create with Parts</strong> button is not available.</li><li>the <ac:link ac:anchor="nested_properties"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[nested properties are displayed as columns]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the Instance Table.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d77dd28c-caf8-47cd-82f0-0777dd16ca43"><ac:parameter ac:name="">add_existing_instances</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add an existing instance</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click <strong>Create</strong> &gt;<strong> Add Existing</strong>. In this case, the contents of a selected package, but not the package itself, will be added to the table.</li></ul></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The selected instance the <strong>Columns</strong> button and choose columns of instance slot values that you want to fill with copied information.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d73030b-a3ae-4e47-8de0-b50cc603e144"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The cells must be editable in the Instance Table.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X).<br />Each row displays one instance with its slot values.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1629c608-6598-43f1-949a-ac594a25d3fb"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If an instance already exists in the model, the information updates.</li><li>If instances have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set after the copy-and-paste command only for pasted instances in the Instance Table. Manage ID prefixes in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Element Numbering dialog" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:title="Copy and paste instances in the Instance Table from another resource" ac:alt="Copy and paste instances in the Instance Table from another resource"><ri:attachment ri:filename="paste_table_in_the_instance_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filling the table with instances" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995258 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00844: (2026x Refresh1) Filter By Package Imports filter

- page_id: `303995258`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995258/2026x+Refresh1+Filter+By+Package+Imports+filter
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:24.624+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Selecting elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1038774317

1038774329

1038774315

**On this page**

****

1038774319

##### Restricting type selection list to imported elements

To restrict the type selection list to imported elements

1. Create a Package diagram.
2. Drag the package with typed elements, as well as packages with types, on the diagram pane.
3. Create a package import between them by clicking Package Import in the diagram palette. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_package_import_button.png']

To apply the filter

1. Click a typed element to open the **Select Type** dialog.
2. Apply the **Filter By Package Imports filter**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As a result, the type selection list is restricted to imported elements only.

There are more [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting elements' space=''] you can use for the same purpose.

##### Different abstraction levels

You can also use a Model instead of a package, in which case, the elements cannot be accessed from outside, and you can only select the types from packages that are owned within the Model element.

To only show elements from the desired abstraction level

1. Right-click the package to which you want to restrict your type selection, and select Refactor > Convert To > Model .
2. Select a typed element nested in that model, and specify its type. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_port_type.png']
3. Apply Filter By Package Imports to only list the elements that belong to the same abstraction level.

##### Validation

Use active validation to evaluate which element types are out of the scope specified by package imports.

1. Go to Analyze > Validation > Validate.
2. In the Validation dialog , select UML correctness constraints from the Validation Suite drop-down list.
3. Click Validate .
4. In the Validation Results panel, right-click the element you want to change or unset type for, and select Change Type , Unset Type , or Create Package Import Relationship accordingly. [ATTACHMENT filename='elements_out_of_scope.png']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992815 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00845: (2026x Refresh1) Filtering

- page_id: `303992815`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992815/2026x+Refresh1+Filtering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:23.505+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

632682683

632682687

632682686

INLINE

The modeling tool filtering mechanism allows filtering types of model elements in particular lists. For example, you can filter the types of model elements that are displayed in the Model Browser, or you can select them when searching.

To filter the types of the model elements that are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab' space='']**Containment** tab

1. In the Model Browser, open the Containment tab (in case it was closed or another tab was open).
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button_in_model_browser.png'] and select Filter . [ATTACHMENT filename='containment_tab_filter.png'] The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting element types' space=''] dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_element_type_dialog.png']
3. Do one of the following:
  - Click to clear the check box next to the model element type, which you do not want to be displayed in the Containment tab.
  - Click to select the check box next to the model element type, which you want to be displayed in the **Containment** tab. An element cannot be filtered if it has a stereotype applied to it, too. You can only filter elements that have a custom stereotype applied, under the condition that the stereotype has the **Hide Metatype** property set to *true*in its customization.
4. Click OK . Items in the Containment tab are now filtered.

Containment

Model

Containment

To filter the types of model elements when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Searching' space='']

1. From the Edit main menu, select Find . The Find dialog opens.
2. Next to the Type box, click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting element types' space=''] dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_element_tyle_dialog_actor.png']
3. Click to select the check box of the model element type that you want to search for.
4. Click OK . You are now back to the Find dialog then click Find . The Search Results tab opens in the Model Browser.

632682682

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting element types' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994485 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00846: (2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items

- page_id: `303994485`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994485/2026x+Refresh1+Filtering+by+Legend+items
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:17.412+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Legends
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Tip

1947346740

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Tip

1947346743

2010910600

**On this page**

**43**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Tip

1947346742

Diagrams showing multiple aspects of the system are often difficult to understand and analyze since they are quite crowded. Filtering diagrams by Legend items enables users to reduce diagrams to the chosen views only, i.e., hide unnecessary layers or show only a few necessary ones in order to make them more comprehensible. The feature is especially useful when there is a need to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Presentation Mode' space=''] to stakeholders only one (e.g., electrical) or a few aspects displayed in the diagram.

700500

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Filtering a diagram by the selected Legend item *iElectricity*. 
Filtering preference is set to *Show*(on the left) and *Hide***(on the right).

##### Filtering diagram by Legend items

Once you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends' space='']or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends automatically' space=''] a Legend and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying Legend Items' space=''] for it, you can filter your diagram by Legend items to highlight the chosen layers.

You can filter the diagram directly from:

- .
- (mainly used when the Legend shape is not displayed).

##### Selecting filtering preferenceSelecting filtering preference

Before using the filter, you need to select the filtering preference. There are two options you can choose from: *Show* or *Hide*.

Selecting the *Show* preference allows you to display the element symbols filtered by a Legend item while hiding all the other element symbols. Selecting the Hide preference allows you to hide the element symbols filtered by a Legend item while all the other element symbols of the diagram are displayed. Keep in mind that only one preference can be selected - the *Show* and *Hide* preferences cannot be combined.

###### Selecting the filtering preference for a specific diagram

To select the filtering preference for a specific diagram

1. Open a diagram you want to filter.
2. In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='legends_button.png'] > Filter by Legend Items .
3. In the open dialog, select either *Show* or *Hide*to set the preference to filter your diagram by. The default filtering preference is *Show*. You can change it via the .If there are multiple Legends on the diagram, the same preference applies to all of them.If the applied filter is saved, the filtering preference remains as is once the diagram is closed and reopened. Otherwise, the preference resets to default.
4. Click **OK**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Filtering comments using different filtering preferences:

- Hide : all comments are hidden;
- Show : all comments are shown including anchored elements.

###### default filtering preferenceSelecting the default filtering preference

The default filtering preference is *Show*, however, you can change it via the Project Options. The change applies to all existing and future diagrams of the project, except fordiagrams with an already applied filter.

To select the default filtering preference

1. In the main toolbar, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the General options group, select Legends .
3. Set the **Filtering Preference** option to *Show* or *Hide*. Click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Filtering from legend symbolFiltering when Legend shape is displayed

If the Legend shape is displayed on the diagram pane, you can filter your diagram by Legend items directly from it. To be more specific, you can either filter your diagram from the or the selected . The latter method comes in handy when there is a need to filter the diagram by multiple Legend items at once.

###### **Filtering from smart manipulator**Filtering by Legend items from the smart manipulator toolbar

To filter a diagram by the selected Legend item

1. Open a diagram you want to filter.
2. In the Legend shape on the diagram pane, select a Legend item to filter your diagram by.
3. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The diagram is filtered by the selected Legend item, meaning that the diagram is limited to one specific aspect. If the filtering preference is *Show*, the diagram displays only the element symbols filtered by the Legend item, while the rest of the element symbols are hidden. If the filtering preference is *Hide*, the diagram hides the element symbols filtered by the Legend item while the rest of the element symbols are displayed. TipTo filter the diagram by multiple Legend items, repeat the procedure.

To filter a diagram by all Legend items at once

1. Select a Legend shape on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='filtering_by_all_legend_items.png']
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='filter_button_smart_manipulator.png'] . The diagram is filtered by all items specified in the Legend, meaning that all diagram aspects are either hidden or displayed.

###### **Filtering from legend item shortcut menu**Filtering by Legend items from the shortcut menu

To filter a diagram by the selected Legend items from the shortcut menu

1. In the Legend shape on a diagram, select the Legend item(s) to filter your diagram by.
2. Right-click and then in the shortcut menu, select Filtered . [ATTACHMENT filename='filtering_by_multiple_legend_items.png']

##### Filtering from diagram toolbarFiltering when Legend shape is not displayed

Alternatively, you can filter your diagram by the selected Legend items directly from the diagram toolbar. This is the primary filtering method when the Legend shape is not displayed on the diagram. In this case, you can select whether to filter the diagram by a single or multiple Legend items as well.

To filter a diagram by the selected Legend item(s)

1. Open a diagram you want to filter.
2. In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='legends_button.png'] > Filter by Legend Items .
3. In the open dialog, specify the .
4. Select the Legend items to filter your diagram by.
5. Click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Managing and removing the applied filter

Once diagram layers are filtered, the following notification appears above the diagram:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Click **Clear**to clear the applied filter.
- Click **Save** to keep the filter applied after the diagram is reloaded.

Alternatively, to clear the applied filter, you can:

- click[IMAGE alt='' src='']in the smart manipulator toolbar of the selected Legend item.
- uncheck the box next to **Filtered** in the Legend item shortcut menu.
- clear the corresponding boxes in the **Filter by Legend Items**([IMAGE alt='' src=''] > **Filter by Legend Items**) dialog.

The diagram filtered by Legend items is read-only, meaning that it cannot be edited unless the filter is removed.

1010241802

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures on this page is the *Layers* sample model that comes with the SysML v1 plugin. To open this model, do one of the following:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='layers.mdzip'] .
- Open the model from the <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1 directory .

1947346737

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends automatically' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying Legend Items' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Presentation Mode' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="956019e0-6e5c-4994-b9e5-1de0796c50df"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1947346740</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="790cc1bf-09e8-493c-8590-564dceaba408"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1947346743</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="688d04a6-c2ad-48b4-a54d-8436af970e53"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2010910600</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6674f44-f1ba-4ff0-9858-653ecc84a330"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88208694-60cf-4966-aa25-a6811ebae15c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1947346742</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Diagrams showing multiple aspects of the system are often difficult to understand and analyze since they are quite crowded. Filtering diagrams by Legend items enables users to reduce diagrams to the chosen views only, i.e., hide unnecessary layers or show only a few necessary ones in order to make them more comprehensible. The feature is especially useful when there is a need to <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Presentation Mode" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[present]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to stakeholders only one  (e.g., electrical) or a few aspects displayed in the diagram.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b84324b0-e50b-4748-9268-7d1a18957783"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">700</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pyh3TlHklqg" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">500</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 0.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="legend_filtering_preference_results.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">Filtering a diagram by the selected Legend item <em>iElectricity</em>. <br />F<span>iltering preference is set to <em>Show </em>(o<span>n the left</span>)</span> <span>and <em>Hide</em></span><em> </em>(on the right)<span>.</span></h6><h3>Filtering diagram by Legend items</h3><p>Once you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[created ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends automatically" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[automatically extracted]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> a Legend and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specifying Legend Items" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specified Legend items]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for it, you can filter your diagram by Legend items to highlight the chosen layers.</p><p>You can filter the diagram directly from:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Filtering from legend symbol"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[the Legend shape on the diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Filtering from diagram toolbar"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[the diagram toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (mainly used when the Legend shape is not displayed).</li></ul><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="465ab0c6-385d-4f82-94d8-3f441cad466e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Selecting filtering preference</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Selecting filtering preference</h3><p>Before using the filter, you need to select the filtering preference. There are two options you can choose from: <em>Show</em> or <em>Hide</em>.</p><p>Selecting the <em>Show</em> preference allows you to display the element symbols filtered by a Legend item while hiding all the other element symbols. Selecting the Hide preference allows you to hide the element symbols filtered by a Legend item while all the other element symbols of the diagram are displayed. Keep in mind that only one preference can be selected - the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Show</em> and <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Hide</em> preferences cannot be combined. </p><h4>Selecting the filtering preference for a specific diagram</h4><p>To select the filtering preference for a specific diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a diagram you want to filter.</li><li>In the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="legends_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <span>&gt; </span><strong>Filter by Legend Items</strong><span>.</span><span><br /></span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>In the open dialog, select either <em>Show</em> or <em>Hide </em>to set the preference to filter your diagram by.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5f32562-3e58-4593-8e46-2bfba7ac0a3e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The default filtering preference is <em>Show</em>. You can change it via the <ac:link ac:anchor="default filtering preference"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Legends project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>If there are multiple Legends on the diagram, the same preference applies to all of them. </li><li>If the applied filter is saved, the filtering preference remains as is once the diagram is closed and reopened. Otherwise, the preference resets to default.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering_preference.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b21e7032-f85d-4c58-95f1-758fc24d7f76"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Filtering comments using different <span>filtering </span> <span>preferences</span>:</p><ul><li><em>Hide</em>: all comments are hidden;</li><li><em>Show</em>: all <span>comments are shown including anchored elements.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1319a14-bc2d-4daf-adc6-6e6201c693c5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">default filtering preference</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Selecting the default filtering preference </span></h4><p><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">The default filtering preference is <em>Show</em>, however, you can change it via the Project Options. The change applies to all existing and future diagrams of the project, except for </span>diagrams with an already applied filter.</p><p><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">To select the default filtering preference </span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the main toolbar, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>General</strong> options group, select <strong>Legends</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Set the <strong>Filtering Preference</strong> option to <em>Show</em> or <em>Hide</em>. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_the_filtering_preference_for_all_diagrams_of_the_project.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="567ea062-f115-4aa6-812d-a8372a057c8f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Filtering from legend symbol</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Filtering when Legend shape is displayed </h3><p>If the Legend shape is displayed on the diagram pane, you can filter your diagram by Legend items directly from it. To be more specific, you can either filter your diagram from the <ac:link ac:anchor="Filtering from smart manipulator"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator toolbar of the Legend shape]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or the selected <ac:link ac:anchor="Filtering from legend item shortcut menu"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Legend item(s) shortcut menu]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <span>The latter method comes in handy when there is a need to filter the diagram by multiple Legend items at once. </span></p><h4><strong> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="956ddb82-e1c3-4b99-bbcb-e2b9854f3f29"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Filtering from smart manipulator</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro> </strong>Filtering by Legend items from the smart manipulator toolbar</h4><p>To filter a diagram by the selected Legend item</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a diagram you want to filter.</li><li>In the Legend shape on the diagram pane, select a Legend item to filter your diagram by. </li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_button_smart_manipulator.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_by_legend_items_from_smart_manipulator.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br />The diagram is filtered by the selected Legend item, meaning that the diagram is limited to one specific aspect. <span>If the filtering preference is <em>Show</em>, the diagram displays only the element symbols filtered by the Legend item, <span>while the rest of the element symbols are hidden</span>. <span>If the filtering preference is <em>Hide</em>, the diagram hides the element symbols filtered by the Legend item while the rest of the element symbols are displayed.  </span> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f6e69bc-5d9c-4440-9159-d7bf631e9082"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To filter the diagram by multiple Legend items, repeat the procedure.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><span> <br /></span></p><p><span>To filter a diagram by all Legend items at once</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Legend shape on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering_by_all_legend_items.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the smart manipulator toolbar, click<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_button_smart_manipulator.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The diagram is filtered by all items specified in the Legend, meaning that all diagram aspects are either hidden or displayed. </li></ol><h4><strong> <span> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a2a7349f-c42c-4529-b1f8-d9d1220fa72f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Filtering from legend item shortcut menu</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro> </span> </strong>Filtering by Legend items from the shortcut menu</h4><p>To filter a diagram by the selected Legend items from the shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Legend shape on a diagram, select the Legend item(s) to filter your diagram by.</li><li>Right-click and then in the shortcut menu, select <strong>Filtered</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering_by_multiple_legend_items.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef7a0f54-179e-4bca-a342-1fe3b52bb0fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Filtering from diagram toolbar</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Filtering when Legend shape is not displayed </h3><p>Alternatively, you can filter your diagram by the selected Legend items directly from the diagram toolbar. This is the primary filtering method when the Legend shape is not displayed on the diagram. In this case, you can select whether to filter the diagram by a single or multiple Legend items as well.</p><p><br />To filter a diagram by the selected Legend item(s)</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a diagram you want to filter.</li><li>In the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="legends_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> &gt; </span> <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Filter by Legend Items</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In the open dialog, specify the <ac:link ac:anchor="Selecting filtering preference"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filtering preference]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Select the Legend items to filter your diagram by.</span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <br /></span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Click <strong>OK</strong>. </span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Filtering_by_Legend_items_from_the_diagram_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /></span></li></ol><h3>Managing and removing the applied filter</h3><p>Once diagram layers are filtered, the following notification appears above the diagram:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="notification_filtering_diagrams_by_legend_items.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Clear </strong>to clear the applied filter. </p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target _mce_tagged_br">Click <strong>Save</strong> to keep the filter applied after the diagram is reloaded. </p></li></ul><p>Alternatively, to clear the applied filter, you can:</p><ul><li><p>click<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="smart_manipulaor_filter_by_legend_items.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>in the smart manipulator toolbar of the selected Legend item.</p></li><li><p>uncheck the box next to <strong>Filtered</strong> in the Legend item shortcut menu.</p></li><li><p>clear the corresponding boxes in the <strong>Filter by Legend Items </strong>(<ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="12"><ri:attachment ri:filename="legends_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> &gt; <strong>Filter by Legend Items</strong>) dialog.</p></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="235f807c-52a5-4a82-be07-479a937bd680"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The diagram filtered by Legend items is read-only, meaning that it cannot be edited unless the filter is removed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26a85e54-d74a-4dab-bcef-29dcd497037e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1010241802</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures on this page is the <em>Layers</em> sample model that comes with the SysML v1 plugin. To open this model, do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download <em><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="layers.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items" /></ri:attachment></ac:link></em>. </li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Open the model from the </span><em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\SysML v1 </em>directory<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="10e7f92e-538a-46f2-ac5a-a570dc6ff10b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1947346737</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends automatically" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specifying Legend Items" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Presentation Mode" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994829 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00847: (2026x Refresh1) Filtering table data

- page_id: `303994829`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994829/2026x+Refresh1+Filtering+table+data
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:49.442+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Basic tasks in tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Using wild card

936188854

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Using wild card

936188865

936188853

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Using wild card

936188864

By filtering data in the table you can quickly find the elements you need. This is especially handy when you are working with a large table containing many rows and columns. Elements can be filtered by a phrase specified in the Filter box of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Table Criteria area' space='']. The following figure shows the Generic table before and after filtering.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Filtered rows and columns in the Generic table.

##### Filtering a table

To filter a table

1. In the Table toolbar's Filter box, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='filter_settings.png'] icon and specify the .
2. In the input field, write the keyword to search the table and either press **Enter**or click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to see the results. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Disabled filter during table loadingFiltering is disabled if the table is still loading, as indicated by the warning icon [IMAGE alt='' src='']. Once the table is loaded and the warning disappears, either press **Enter** or click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to filter the table. You can also track the table loading status in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Tables' space=''], [IMAGE alt='' src=''] icon stands for **Loading** and [IMAGE alt='' src=''] for **Loading completed**.
3. Once the table is filtered, the filter icon is marked with a green check mark [ATTACHMENT filename='filter_green_chek_mark.png'] .

##### Specifying filter settingsSpecifying filter settings

The search for the phrase can be performed either in the whole table or in selected columns. You can specify filter settings by clicking [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the Filter box. The list with the following main areas opens:

- Specify the columns to search (1). You can search the entire table by selecting the **All** option or selecting one or several particular columns.
- Specify case sensitivity (2).
- Specify wild card or regular expression (3). Using wild cardIn the **Filter**box, type a phrase using the following wildcard characters:“*” substitutes any range of characters. If your search phrase is “*dd”, all the elements containing “dd” in their names will be found. For example, “Address” and “ThreadDeath”.“?” substitutes only one character. If your search phrase is “?dd”, only the elements with “dd” in their names starting from the second character can be found. For example, “Address”. “ThreadDeath” cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase “dd”.Be sure the **Match Anywhere**command is unchecked when using wildcard characters!
- Specify how the results should match your keywords (4).
- Specify if the children/parent elements should be displayed (5). 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The main areas in the filter settings.

You can save the filter criteria for a particular table. The filter criteria will be saved even after reloading the project or restarting the modeling tool. To learn more, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Saving the filter criteria' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6dc6cca-5f16-458f-9639-0d0efb20776a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">936188854</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47087013-9dd8-47c7-a26e-7409d34f8fb6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">936188865</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="607ad6ee-f816-4841-9238-5a7f4a3f234e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">936188853</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9465a5ac-66cc-41f8-8cff-bc5f24ad2554"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3779cbf8-7f5e-44aa-938c-64b74fe7cb53"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">936188864</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>By filtering data in the table you can quickly find the elements you need. This is especially handy when you are working with a large table containing many rows and columns. Elements can be filtered by a phrase specified in the Filter box of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Table Criteria area" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table Criteria area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The following figure shows the Generic table before and after filtering.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 20.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_in_generic_table_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br />Filtered rows and columns in the Generic table.</h6><h3>Filtering a table</h3><p>To filter a table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Table toolbar's <strong>Filter</strong> box, click the <ac:image ac:title="Filter settings" ac:alt="Filter settings"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_settings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> icon and specify the <ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying filter settings"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filter settings]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the input field, write the keyword to search the table and either press <strong>Enter </strong>or click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering_press_enter_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button to see the results. <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering_press_enter.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0905b272-27a9-4af1-9167-b51a0fb30e77"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Disabled filter during table loading</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Filtering is disabled if the table is still loading, as indicated by the warning icon <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering_disabled.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. Once the table is loaded and the warning disappears, either press <strong>Enter</strong> or click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filtering_press_enter_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button to filter the table. You can also track the table loading status in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Table info area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="status_loading_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> icon stands for <strong>Loading</strong> and <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="status_loading_complete_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> for <strong>Loading completed</strong>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Once the table is filtered, the filter icon is marked with a green check mark <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_green_chek_mark.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. </li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ded46565-d860-442d-b141-11d6d38c90f6"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Specifying filter settings</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Specifying filter settings</h3><p>The search for the phrase can be performed either in the whole table or in selected columns. You can specify filter settings by clicking <ac:image ac:title="Filter settings" ac:alt="Filter settings"><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_settings.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Filter box. The list with the following main areas opens:</p><ul><li><p>Specify the columns to search (1).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="40996788-1d4a-46ee-bb72-057e434a5588"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can search the entire table by selecting the <strong>All</strong> option or selecting one or several particular columns.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Specify case sensitivity (2).</p></li><li><p>Specify wild card or regular expression (3).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dea48d02-4c40-45e4-aaeb-a54461c04c84"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Using wild card</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In the <strong>Filter </strong>box, type a phrase using the following wildcard characters:</p><ul><li>“*” substitutes any range of characters. If your search phrase is “*dd”, all the elements containing “dd” in their names will be found. For example, “A<u>dd</u>ress” and “Threa<u>dD</u>eath”.</li><li>“?” substitutes only one character. If your search phrase is “?dd”, only the elements with “dd” in their names starting from the second character can be found. For example, “A<u>dd</u>ress”. “Threa<u>dD</u>eath” cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase “dd”.</li></ul><p>Be sure the <strong>Match Anywhere </strong>command is unchecked when using wildcard characters!</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Specify how the results should match your keywords (4).</p></li><li><p>Specify if the children/parent elements should be displayed (5).<br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="quickFind_in_generic_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Filtering table data" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 20.0px;">The main areas in the filter settings.</h6></li></ul><p>You can save the filter criteria for a particular table. The filter criteria will be saved even after reloading the project or restarting the modeling tool. To learn more, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Saving the filter criteria" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992034 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00848: (2026x Refresh1) Find and Replace dialog

- page_id: `303992034`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992034/2026x+Refresh1+Find+and+Replace+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:25.764+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Searching
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The Find and Replace functionality allows replacing one specified model value with another. You can change the values for the following properties:

- Names
- Documentation
- Tag values
- Text included to Notes
- Text included to Text Boxes
- Expressions

To open the **Find and Replace** dialog, do one of the following

- On the main menu, click Edit > Find and Replace .
- Press Ctrl + R.
- On the main toolbar, click the arrow by the Find button [ATTACHMENT filename='find_button_2.png'] , then select Find and Replace .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following table provides descriptions of the **Find and Replace** dialog components.

| Dialog element name | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Find What | Text box with drop- down list | Type or select from the list to search the value of the element to be replaced. |
| Replace With | Text box with drop- down list | Type or select from the list the value of element that will replace the found value. |
| Options area |  |  |
| Type | Text box with | Click the button and select one or more element types from the list provided in the open Select Element Type dialog. You have the option to include standard subtypes and custom types of the selected element type in the search results. Alternatively, you can leave the default value <any>.Select the Include Subtypes check box to include the subtypes of the selected elements, e.g., models and profiles of Packages.Select the Include Custom Types check box to show the custom types extending the selected element types, e.g., a Smart Package extending a Package. |
| Scope | Text box with | Click the button and select a package wherein the content search will be performed. |
| Load diagrams and autoloadable used projects in read-write mode | Check box | Select to load all elements to be included in the search if the model has diagrams or used projects that are not loaded. Used project will not be loaded and elements from it will not be included into the search, if the load mode of that used project is Manual load. |
| Include elements from standard/system profiles | Check box | Select to include elements from standard/system profiles. |
| Include elements from additional content | Check box | Select to include additional elements from Smart packages if Smart packages fall into the search scope. |

You may see an error when trying to replace a value with an invalid one.

- For example, if the value type is boolean , you may replace values from true to false or from false to true , but if you will try to replace the value true or false to other, for example, to Motor, an error message will be displayed.
- You will not be allowed to change the Integer value to String when it is a part of the value. For example, if you have the 120 value, trying to replace 20 with AB will cause an error.

For example, if you have the <em>120</em> value, trying to replace <em>20</em> with <em>AB</em> will cause an error.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991947 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00849: (2026x Refresh1) Find dialog

- page_id: `303991947`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991947/2026x+Refresh1+Find+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:21.625+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Searching
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

599760352

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

599760355

599760349

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

599760354

#### EXCERPT: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

INLINE

The **Find** dialog provides a quick way to search for specific model elements. Use it to search for elements by name, applied stereotype name, tag value and documentation content. The dialog can be opened from several different places in your modeling tool. To complete a simple search, use the main area of the dialog, or click the **Options** button, to specify criteria that will reduce your search results to only applicable ones.

To search for elements using the Find dialog

1. Open the Find dialog in one of the following ways:

1. 
  - In the main menu, click Edit > Find .
  - In the main toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='find.png'] .
  - Press Ctrl+Shift+F.
2. Specify the search criteria in the .
3. If you would like your search to be more specific, click the Options button to expand the **Options** area , and select the desired options.
4. When you finish specifying your search criteria, click the Find button.

After completing the steps above, the search results are displayed in the **Search Results** tab of the model browser.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The **Find** dialog with the extended **Options** area.

#### TIP: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

Searching tips

- To search for textual information in an active diagram, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the diagram toolbar or press Ctrl+F .
- To quickly access your search results, add them to Favorites or save them as the contents of a Smart Package.

#### EXPAND: Click to learn how to add search results to Favorites

Click to learn how to add search results to Favorites

1. In the Search Results tab of the Model browser, select the element(s) you want to add to Favorites. Press and hold the Shift button to select multiple items.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites.png'] on the toolbar of the Search Results tab. Then select Add Selected to Favorites from the menu.

The selected search results are now added to Favorites. They can be accessed by clicking [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and selecting the desired element under the **Go to Favorites** group.

#### EXPAND: Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

Click to learn how to save search results as the content of a Smart Package

1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='save_search_results_button.png'] on the toolbar of the Search Results tab containing the search results you want to add to a Smart Package.
2. From the open menu, select one of the following:
  - Save Query as Smart Package if you want the content of a newly created Smart Package to be dynamic. The content of the Smart Package will then be updated according to your search. The specified search criteria will be saved as the Query property value.
  - Save Results as Smart Package if you want the content of a newly created Smart Package to be static. In this case, your model is not queried, and new elements meeting the specified search criteria are not automatically included in the Smart Package.
3. In the open dialog, select the Package that will own the created Smart Package.

A new Smart Package containing all the search results from the **Search Results** tab is created under the selected owner.

main

##### Main area of the Find dialog

Use the main area of the **Find**dialog to specify the most common search criteria, such as an element name or the scope of the search. The table below provides detailed descriptions of all the elements in the main area.

Find dialog options

| Dialog element name | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| What | Text box with drop- down list | The What box allows you to search for model elements by the text used in their names or textual properties. To search for desired model elements, enter the text you want to find. To search in element names, select the Search in names check box. To search in textual properties including Documentation, select the Search in all texts check box. Note that both of these options are available only if the Options area is collapsed. To make your search more specific, click as illustrated below.You may use wildcard characters (,?) in your search phrase. "" means zero, one, or more characters. If your search phrase is "dd", all the elements containing "dd" in their names can be found, e.g. "Address" and "ThreadDeath". "?" means exactly one character. If your search phrase is "?dd", only the elements with "dd" in their names, beginning with the second character (e.g., "Address"), can be found. "ThreadDeath" cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase "dd". Be sure to check the Match exactly command when using wildcard characters! |
| Type | Text box with | Click and select one or more element types from the list provided in the open Select Element Type dialog. You have the option to include standard subtypes and custom types of the selected element type in the search results.Select the Include Subtypes check box to include the subtypes of the selected elements, e.g., models and profiles of Packages.Select the Include Custom Types check box to show the custom types extending the selected element types, e.g., a Smart Package extending a Package. |
| Scope | Text box with | The scope refers to the package of your model in which the content search will be performed. Click and select the desired package. Specify the search scope before opening the Find dialog by performing one of the following:Right-clicking a package in the Model Browser, and selecting Find from its shortcut menu.Selecting a package in the Model Browser and pressing Ctrl + F. |
| Properties | Text box with | The box is available only when the Options area is expanded. Click , select the properties applicable to your search and specify their values. The property list in the open dialog contains only the properties of the element types selected in the Type box. Select at least one element type to make the button available. Keep in mind that the property list contains primitive value properties, e.g. string, boolean, integer, and a non-primitive value property named Applied Stereotype. |

options

##### Options area of the Find dialog

The **Options** area allows you to select from a number of additional search options that will make your search more specific and narrow down the search results.

| Dialog element name | Dialog element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Clear Previous Results | Check box | Select to remove all previous search results from the Search Results tab in the Model Browser. |
| Load diagrams and autoloadable used projects | Check box | Select to load all elements to be included in the search if the model has diagrams or used projects that are not loaded. If the load mode of the used project is Manual load, the used project will not be loaded and elements from it will not be included in the search. |
| Include elements from standard/system profiles | Check box | Select to include elements from standard/system profiles. |
| Include elements from additional content | Check box | Select to include additional elements from Smart packages if Smart packages fall into the search scope. By default, this check box is not selected and elements from Smart packages are excluded from search results. This enables you to avoid search results being duplicated by results outside of Smart packages. |
| Search data unused in diagrams | Check box | Select to search for only those elements that do not have symbols in any diagram. Note that the check box is unavailable if the Find dialog is opened by using the Find in Diagram command in the diagram shortcut menu. |
| Show results in new window | Check box | Select to display the results of every subsequent search in a new Search Results tab of the Model Browser. |

##### Advanced search options

The **Find** dialog offers a number of advanced search options allowing you to narrow down search results. Below are some specific cases illustrating how search criteria can be defined.

To find an element by an element ID

1. Open the Find dialog.
2. In the What box, enter the prefix " xmi.id =" followed by the ID of the element you want to find.
3. Click the Find button. [ATTACHMENT filename='find_dialog_fragment1.png']

To find abstract Classes

1. Open the Find dialog.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_long.png'] next to the Type box, and select Class .
3. Expand the Options area if it is not yet expanded.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_long.png'] next to the Properties box, select the Is Abstract property, and set its value to true .
5. Click the Find button.

To find the elements with a specific stereotype and a certain phrase in their documentation

1. Open the Find dialog.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_long.png'] next to the Type box, select the element type you want to find (e.g, Actor ) and select the Include Subtypes check box.
3. Expand the Options area if it is not yet expanded .
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_long.png'] next to the Properties box, select the Applied Stereotype property, and select a custom stereotype (e.g., «BusinessActor») as its value.
5. In the same dialog, select the Documentation property and enter a search phrase as its value.
6. Click the Find button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
