In this case, your model is not queried, and new elements meeting the specified search criteria are not automatically included in the Smart Package.</li></ul></li><li>In the open dialog, select the Package that will own the created Smart Package.</li></ol><p>A new Smart Package containing all the search results from the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab is created under the selected owner.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a60565a7-a1ff-4dc8-9cad-39d230b9090b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">main</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Main area of the Find dialog</h3><p>Use the main area of the <strong>Find </strong>dialog to specify the most common search criteria, such as an element name or the scope of the search. The table below provides detailed descriptions of all the elements in the main area. </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1db7736-6786-4f3c-9bab-b0936123d7ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Find dialog options</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Dialog</strong> <strong>element</strong> <strong>name</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Dialog element type</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>What</strong></p></td><td><p>Text box with drop- down list</p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>The <strong>What</strong> box allows you to search for model elements by the text used in their names or textual properties. To search for desired model elements, enter the text you want to find. To search in element names, select the <strong>Search in names</strong> check box. To search in textual properties including Documentation, select the <strong>Search in all texts</strong> check box. Note that both of these options are available only if the <strong>Options</strong> area is collapsed. To make your search more specific, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="search_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> as illustrated below.<br /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><ac:image ac:height="181" ac:width="256"><ri:attachment ri:filename="match_exactly.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>You may use wildcard characters (,?) in your search phrase. &quot;&quot; means zero, one, or more characters. If your search phrase is &quot;dd&quot;, all the elements containing &quot;dd&quot; in their names can be found, e.g. &quot;A<span style="text-decoration: underline;">dd</span>ress&quot; and &quot;Threa<span style="text-decoration: underline;">dD</span>eath&quot;. &quot;?&quot; means exactly one character. If your search phrase is &quot;?dd&quot;, only the elements with &quot;dd&quot; in their names, beginning with the second character (e.g., &quot;A<u>dd</u>ress&quot;), can be found. &quot;Threa<span style="text-decoration: underline;">dD</span>eath&quot; cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase &quot;dd&quot;. Be sure to check the <strong>Match exactly</strong> command when using wildcard characters!</p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Type</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper">Text box with <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select one or more element types from the list provided in the open <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Selecting element types" /><ac:link-body><strong>Select Element Type</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> dialog. You have the option to include standard <span>subtypes </span>and custom types of the selected element type in the search results.</p><ul><li>Select the<strong> Include Subtypes</strong> check box to include the subtypes of the selected elements, e.g., models and profiles of Packages.</li><li>Select the <strong>Include Custom Types</strong> check box to show the custom types extending the selected element types, e.g., a Smart Package extending a Package.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_element_type_find_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Scope</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper">Text box with <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper">The scope refers to the package of your model in which the content search will be performed. Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select the desired package. Specify the search scope before opening the <strong>Find</strong> dialog by performing one of the following:<ul><li>Right-clicking a package in the Model Browser, and selecting <strong>Find</strong> from its shortcut menu.</li><li>Selecting a package in the Model Browser and pressing Ctrl + F.</li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Properties</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper">Text box with <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>The box is available only when the <strong>Options</strong> area is expanded. Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, select the properties applicable to your search and specify their values. The property list in the open dialog contains only the properties of the element types selected in the <strong>Type</strong> box. Select at least one element type to make the button available. Keep in mind that the property list contains primitive value properties, e.g. string, boolean, integer, and a non-primitive value property named <strong>Applied Stereotype</strong>.</p></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="403b09e8-59f8-4b2b-90c8-cba1b6570620"><ac:parameter ac:name="">options</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Options area of the Find dialog</h3><p>The <strong>Options</strong> area allows you to select from a number of additional search options that will make your search more specific and narrow down the search results.</p><p><br /></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td colspan="1"><p><strong>Dialog</strong> <strong>element</strong> <strong>name</strong></p></td><td colspan="1"><p><strong>Dialog element type</strong></p></td><td colspan="1"><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><strong>Clear Previous</strong> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><strong>Results</strong></p></td><td colspan="1">Check box</td><td colspan="1">Select to remove all previous search results from the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab in the Model Browser.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Load diagrams and autoloadable used projects</strong></td><td colspan="1">Check box</td><td colspan="1">Select to load all elements to be included in the search if the model has diagrams or used projects that are not loaded. If the load mode of the used project is <strong>Manual load</strong>, the used project will not be loaded and elements from it will not be included in the search.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Include elements from standard/system profiles</strong></td><td colspan="1">Check box</td><td colspan="1">Select to include elements from standard/system profiles.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Include elements from additional content</strong></td><td colspan="1">Check box</td><td colspan="1">Select to include additional elements from Smart packages if Smart packages fall into the search scope. By default, this check box is not selected and elements from Smart packages are excluded from search results. This enables you to avoid search results being duplicated by results outside of Smart packages.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><strong>Search</strong> <strong>data</strong> <strong>unused in diagrams</strong></p></td><td colspan="1">Check box</td><td colspan="1">Select to search for only those elements that do not have symbols in any diagram. Note that the check box is unavailable if the <strong>Find</strong> dialog is opened by using the <strong>Find in Diagram</strong> command in the diagram shortcut menu.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p><strong>Show</strong> <strong>results</strong> <strong>in</strong> <strong>new window</strong></p></td><td colspan="1">Check box</td><td colspan="1">Select to display the results of every subsequent search in a new <strong>Search Results</strong> tab of the Model Browser.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Advanced search options</h3><p>The <strong>Find</strong> dialog offers a number of advanced search options allowing you to narrow down search results. Below are some specific cases illustrating how search criteria can be defined.<br /> </p><p>To find an element by an element ID</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Find</strong> dialog.</li><li>In the <strong>What</strong> box, enter the prefix &quot;<a href="http://xmi.id">xmi.id</a>=&quot; followed by the ID of the element you want to find.</li><li>Click the <strong>Find</strong> button.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_dialog_fragment1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To find abstract Classes</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Find</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the <strong>Type</strong> box, and select <strong>Class</strong>.</li><li>Expand the <strong>Options</strong> area if it is not yet expanded.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the <strong>Properties</strong> box, select the <strong>Is</strong><strong>Abstract</strong> property, and set its value to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Find</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To find the elements with a specific stereotype and a certain phrase in their documentation</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Find</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the <strong>Type</strong> box, select the element type you want to find (e.g, <strong>Actor</strong>) and select the <strong>Include Subtypes</strong> check box.</li><li>Expand the <strong>Options</strong> area if it is not yet expanded<span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);">.</span></li><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_long.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> next to the <strong>Properties</strong> box, select the <strong>Applied Stereotype</strong> property, and select a custom stereotype (e.g., «BusinessActor») as its value. <span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span></li><li>In the same dialog, select the <strong>Documentation</strong> property and enter a search phrase as its value.</li><li>Click the <strong>Find</strong> button.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991978 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00850: (2026x Refresh1) Find TODO

- page_id: `303991978`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991978/2026x+Refresh1+Find+TODO
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:22.872+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Searching
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Command **Find TODO** performs a quick search for all *To Do* values.

You can specify *To Do* values in the element Specification window, the **To** **Do** property cell. You can use the **To Do** property cell for adding the notes about the work you need to do. Then you can quickly find the elements that have the **To Do** properties defined.

Results of this kind of search are displayed in the **Search Results** tab on the Model Browser.

To start the **Find TODO** search, do one of the following

- On the main menu, click Edit > Find TODO .
- On the main toolbar, click the arrow nearby the [ATTACHMENT filename='find_button_2.png'] button and then select Find TODO .

The **Search Results** tab opens with results represented in it.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f14189ed-2da7-44ac-8704-8a1bb678b3f0"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Command <strong>Find TODO</strong> performs a quick search for all <em>To Do</em> values.</p><p>You can specify <em>To Do</em> values in the element Specification window, the <strong>To</strong> <strong>Do</strong> property cell. You can use the <strong>To Do</strong> property cell for adding the notes about the work you need to do. Then you can quickly find the elements that have the <strong>To Do</strong> properties defined.</p><p>Results of this kind of search are displayed in the <strong>Search Results</strong> tab on the Model Browser.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To start the <strong>Find TODO</strong> search, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Edit</strong> &gt; <strong>Find TODO</strong>.</li><li>On the main toolbar, click the arrow nearby the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="find_button_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Find TODO" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button and then select <strong>Find TODO</strong>.</li></ul><p>The <strong>Search Results</strong> tab opens with results represented in it.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991061 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00851: (2026x Refresh1) Finding predefined diagram aspects

- page_id: `303991061`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991061/2026x+Refresh1+Finding+predefined+diagram+aspects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:18.275+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Diagram aspects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are predefined aspects that are only included in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling' space=''] or other customizations. The predefined diagram aspects are the following: <<optical>>, <<mechanical>>, <<electrical>>, <<software>>, <<conceptual>>, and <<logical>>. The procedure below explains how to find them in your project. You can use predefined aspects in your project, or you can create custom aspects. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating custom aspects' space='']>]]>

To find predefined aspects in the project when SysML v1 Plugin is installed

1. In the Containment tree, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select the **Show Auxiliary Resources** option.
2. Expand packages *MD Customization for SysML* > *additional_stereotypes > aspects*.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The location of the predefined diagram aspects in the project when SysML v1 plugin is installed. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample model:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>There are predefined aspects that are only included in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML v1 Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or other customizations. The predefined diagram aspects are the following: &lt;&lt;optical&gt;&gt;, &lt;&lt;mechanical&gt;&gt;, &lt;&lt;electrical&gt;&gt;, &lt;&lt;software&gt;&gt;, &lt;&lt;conceptual&gt;&gt;, and &lt;&lt;logical&gt;&gt;. The procedure below explains how to find them in your project. You can use predefined aspects in your project, or you can create custom aspects. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating custom aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create custom aspects >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To find predefined aspects in the project when SysML v1 Plugin is installed<br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c862e06a-c893-4a1b-997d-4d17e23e6a95"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the Containment tree, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Finding predefined diagram aspects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>,</span> and select the <strong>Show Auxiliary Resources</strong> option.</span></li><li data-uuid="2f08a521-fcfc-4108-b2c3-0eac5e922c40"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Expand packages <em>MD Customization for SysML</em> &gt; <em>additional_stereotypes &gt; aspects</em>.</span></li></ol><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="predefined_diagram_aspects.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Finding predefined diagram aspects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>The location of the predefined diagram aspects in the project when SysML v1 plugin is installed. The illustration displays concepts from SysML Plugin.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Diagram aspects</strong> sample model:</p><ul><li data-uuid="fdfe3c38-14a7-4c38-a3c2-195a61ba0968"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram aspects.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>. </span></li><li data-uuid="2d4f398e-f34b-44e1-9dfc-fb162e3b5339"><span class="confluence-link">Find in the modeling tool: <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\SysML v1\diagram aspects.mdzip.</span></em></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997384 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00852: (2026x Refresh1) Finishing and canceling merge

- page_id: `303997384`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997384/2026x+Refresh1+Finishing+and+canceling+merge
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:06.900+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Model Merge
- labels: ['finish-merging', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Availability

1077613678

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Availability

1077613680

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Availability

1077613679

To finish the merge procedure

- On the Merge window, click the Finish Merging button and a ll accepted changes will be copied to the target.

After the merge results are copied to the project, do not forget to either save or commit the project to the server.

To cancel the merge procedure

- On the Merge window, click the Cancel button. The question dialog box opens asking you if you want to cancel the merge procedure.
  - Click Yes if you are sure that you want to cancel merge procedure.
  - Click No and you will be returned back to the Merge window.

Also you can cancel merge procedure by clicking **Close** button on the **Merge** window.

**#diagramannotationson Turning on merged diagram annotations**

Before finishing the merge procedure, you can show merged diagram annotations. For this, click the **Annotate Merged Diagram** button on the toolbar located at the top of the **Merge** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Annotate Merged Diagram button in the Merge Results window toolbar.

#### NOTE: Availability

Availability

This button is available if any positions of symbols in diagrams have been changed in both contributors.

The purpose of the annotation is to mark symbols that have different positions on the same diagrams in ancestor and source projects on a merged diagram. If the Annotate Merged Diagram button is enabled, those symbols are annotated in yellow, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Annotated symbols in a merged diagram.

Click the selected symbol to open a smart manipulator and select a resolution for symbol merging.

1077613677

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Merge' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

server.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To cancel the merge procedure</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">On the </span> <strong style="letter-spacing: ac:macro-id="103582f0-2bd6-4c9a-af50-73f670c37f36"><ac:parameter ac:name="">#diagramannotationson</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Turning on merged diagram annotations</span> </strong> </span></p><p>Before finishing the merge procedure, you can show merged diagram annotations. For this, click the <strong>Annotate Merged Diagram</strong> button on the toolbar located at the top of the <strong>Merge</strong> window.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="annotate_merged_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Finishing and canceling merge" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Annotate Merged Diagram button in the Merge Results window toolbar.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e10c1ec6-74d3-47cb-8e2d-809e8631a44b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Availability</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This button is available if any positions of symbols in diagrams have been changed in both contributors.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The purpose of the annotation is to mark symbols that have different positions on the same diagrams in ancestor and source projects on a merged diagram. If the Annotate Merged Diagram button is enabled, those symbols are annotated in yellow, as shown below.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="merged_diagram_annotation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Finishing and canceling merge" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Annotated symbols in a merged diagram.</h6><p>Click the selected symbol to open a smart manipulator and select a resolution for symbol merging.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c459fad-fe64-4eb4-89c9-845b02c37e3c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1077613677</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model Merge" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992587 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00853: (2026x Refresh1) FMU import

- page_id: `303992587`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992587/2026x+Refresh1+FMU+import
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:04.674+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Importing External Simulation Models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

**32**

FMU can be used for:

- Creating model structure with SysML v1 elements.
- Co-simulating the imported model with help of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation' space='MMA'] (CST). To learn more, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation of SysML models' space='MMA'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SSP Export - Technology Preview' space='MMA'] .

Simulation of FMU 3.0 and merged arrays using Cameo Simulation Toolkit (CST) is not supported.

##### Understanding the FMU Import Options dialog

The **FMU Import Options** dialog enables users to customize the FMU file import into the model. For example, you can specify whether parameters from the FMU file should be imported as ports or value properties. Additionally, you can change the name of the Block (the FMU model name is used by default) and automatically attach the file to the project upon import.

The **FMU Import Options** dialog has the following options:

| Option name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Direction | The direction of the port: In or Out. Inputs and Outputs are selected to be imported as ports by default. |
| Name | The name of the property/parameter. |
| Type | The type of the property, for example, value property. |
| Description | The textual description of the property. |
| As Port | Click to select whether properties should be represented as Flow or Proxy ports. Select None to import the selected properties as value properties. |
| Interface Block | Click to select a compatible interface Block or create a new one. <NEW> is selected by default.The Interface Block column is active only if Proxy Port is selected in the As Port column. |
| Redefine | Click to redefine inherited value or part properties. |
|  | Click to select all listed properties at once. |
|  | Click to deselect all listed properties at once. |
|  | The name of the Block created in the model upon import. By default, the name of the FMU model is used, but you can set the one you like. |
|  | If the checkbox is selected, the imported FMU file is automatically attached to the model.If the Attach file to the project is checked in the FMU Import Options dialog, a previous version of the attached file is replaced with a new one upon import. |
|  | If the checkbox is selected, the FMU properties are split according to dot notation. The FMU properties are represented as Flow or Value properties, owned by an Interface Block. For structured properties, As Port and Interface Block columns are inactive. |
|  | If the checkbox is selected, the arrays (properties with the same name and index) are merged. When the arrays are merged, a single property is created with a multiplicity set. The checkbox is available only when arrays are detected in an imported FMU file.Co-simulation of the merged arrays is not supported. |
|  | When clicked, displays information about the imported FMU. The following parameters are displayed in the expanded section.Format - Always displayedFMI version - Always displayedPlatforms - Always displayedVariables - Always displayedGeneration Date - Dynamic (Displayed, if available)Generation Tool - Dynamic (Displayed, if available)Description - Dynamic (Displayed, if available)Documentation- Dynamic (Displayed, if available) |

##### Importing FMU

To import the FMU file into the model

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click File > Import From > FMU File . [ATTACHMENT filename='fmu_file_import.png']
2. Select the FMU file on your file system and click Open . [ATTACHMENT filename='Import dialog.png']
3. In the **FMU Import Options** dialog, specify which properties from the FMU file should be imported into your model as values and ports and Click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dragging and dropping FMU on diagram

Alternatively, you can import the FMU model by dragging and dropping the selected FMU file directly onto the Block Definition (BDD) and/or Internal Block (IBD) diagrams.

To import the FMU file into the model using BDD/IBD

1. Locate the FMU file on your file system or the Containment tree.
2. Do either:
  - Drag and drop the file from your file system or the Containment tree onto the BDD diagram. A Block with the applied «FMU» stereotype is created in the model after customizing the FMU import via the FMU Import Options dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_fmu_block_bdd.png']
  - Drag and drop the file from your file system or the Containment tree onto the IBD diagram. A Part property is created in the model after customizing the FMU import via the FMU Import Options dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_fmu_block_ibd.png']

###### Dragging and dropping FMU on existing Block

You can drag and drop the FMU file directly onto the Block as implementation as well. Once the FMU file is dropped, the**FMU Import Options** dialog opens. This dialog allows redefining value and part properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Redefining properties using FMU Import Options dialog

In this case, a new Block is created as a subtype of the existing Block together with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Generalization' space=''] relationship.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dropping FMU on an existing Block

**In/Out properties** in the **FMU Import Options** dialog are by default imported into the model as ports of a Flow type.

###### Updating existing FMU Block

An existing FMU Block can be updated by dragging and dropping a FMU file directly on it. This is useful in those cases when there is a need to quickly import properties that were initially left out.

To update a FMU Block

1. Locate the FMU file on your file system or the Containment tree.
2. Drag and drop it on the shape of your FMU Block.
3. In the opened FMU Import Options dialog, select the properties to update the FMU Block with.
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dragging and dropping FMU on the existing FMU Block

Alternatively, you can update the FMU Block directly from the shortcut menu.

To update a FMU Block from the shortcut menu

1. Right-click the Simulink Block.
2. Select Tools > Update from FMU file from the shortcut menu. [ATTACHMENT filename='updating_from_fmu.png']
3. In the opened FMU Import Options dialog, select the properties to update the FMU Block with. [ATTACHMENT filename='updating_fmu.png']
4. Click OK .

Already imported properties are grayed out and cannot be edited in the **FMU Import Options**dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Structured Import

If you select the Structured Import checkbox, then the FMU properties are split according to dot notation. The FMU properties are represented as Flow or Value properties, owned by an Interface Block.For structured properties, **As Port**and **Interface Block** columns are inactive.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### FMU properties split according to dot notation.

##### Merging arrays

If the FMU file has arrays (properties with the same name and index), then selecting the Merge Arrays checkbox in the FMU Import Options dialog merges all the arrays into a single property with the multiplicity set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All the arrays present in the FMU file merged into a single property with the multiplicity set.

##### Viewing FMU details

You can view the details about the imported FMU file in the FMU Import Options dialog. Once you click the **Details**button, the details are displayed in the expanded section of the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Details about the imported FMU file displayed in the expanded section of the FMU Import Options dialog.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation of SysML models' space='MMA']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="028bbd9c-4927-48bc-97d1-da077c11d0f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell>Functional Mock-up Interface, also known as FMI, is a tool-independent open-source standard that supports both model exchange and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Simulation of SysML models" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[co-simulation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of dynamic models in a standardized format. The modeling tool supports all the major FMI versions (1.0, 2.0 and 3.0). A component that implements FMI is called a Functional Mock-up Unit (FMU). The FMU stores a simulation model that adheres to the FMI standard. To be more specific, the FMU file contains an XML description file and implementation in a binary form. <p>FMU can be used for:</p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Creating model structure with SysML v1 elements.</span></li><li>Co-simulating the imported model with help of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Simulation Toolkit]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (CST). To learn more, refer to <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Simulation of SysML models" /></ac:link>.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="SSP Export - Technology Preview" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SSP export]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a30479d1-caf0-4396-8340-e855210f637b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Simulation of FMU 3.0 and merged arrays using Cameo Simulation Toolkit (CST) is not supported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Understanding the FMU Import Options dialog</h3><p>The <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog enables users to customize the FMU file import into the model. For example, you can specify whether parameters from the FMU file should be imported as ports or value properties. Additionally, you can change the name of the Block (the FMU model name is used by default) and automatically attach the file to the project upon import. </p><p>The <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog has the following options:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 68.2727%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 29.2712%;" /><col class="" style="width: 70.6837%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Option name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Direction</strong></td><td>The direction of the port: <em>In</em> or <em>Out</em>. <em>Inputs</em> and <em>Outputs </em>are selected to be imported as ports by default.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Name</strong></td><td>The name of the property/parameter.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Type</strong></td><td>The type of the property, for example, value property.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Description</strong></td><td>The textual description of the property.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>As Port</strong></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click to select whether properties should be represented as Flow or Proxy ports. Select None to import the selected properties as value properties.</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Interface Block</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to select a compatible interface Block or create a new one. <span>&lt;NEW&gt; is selected by default.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01905682-2985-4a08-b900-bef8b85c3585"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Interface Block column is active only if <strong>Proxy Port</strong> is selected in the <strong>As Port</strong> column.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Redefine</strong></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click to redefine inherited value or part properties.</span></td></tr><tr class=""><td style="vertical-align: middle;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="25" ac:width="73"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_all.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Click to select all listed properties at once.</td></tr><tr class=""><td style="vertical-align: middle;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="24" ac:width="88"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_none.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Click to deselect all listed properties at once.</td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align: middle;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="33" ac:width="195"><ri:attachment ri:filename="block_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td>The name of the Block created in the model upon import. By default, the name of the FMU model is used, but you can set the one you like.</td></tr><tr class=""><td style="vertical-align: middle;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="25" ac:width="141"><ri:attachment ri:filename="attach_file_to_the_project.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>If the checkbox is selected, the imported FMU file is automatically attached to the model.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac48cca1-eeec-4efb-8aab-1a26cb98b185"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Attach file to the project</strong> is checked in the <strong>FMU Import Options dialog</strong>, a previous version of the attached file is replaced with a new one upon import.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align: middle;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="26" ac:width="124"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Structured Import_Checkbox.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the checkbox is selected, the FMU properties are split according to dot notation. The checkbox is available <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">only when arrays are detected in an imported FMU file.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="463bed82-7d06-4011-b58f-b15dd70163b6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Co-simulation of the merged arrays is not supported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td style="vertical-align: middle;"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="19" ac:width="68"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Details.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">When clicked, displays information about the imported FMU. The following parameters are displayed in the expanded section.<br /></span></span><ul><li>Format - Always displayed</li><li>FMI version - Always displayed</li><li>Platforms - Always displayed</li><li>Variables - Always displayed</li><li>Generation Date - Dynamic (Displayed, if available)</li><li>Generation Tool - Dynamic (Displayed, if available)</li><li>Description - Dynamic (Displayed, if available)</li><li>Documentation- Dynamic (Displayed, if available)</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Importing FMU</h3><p>To import the FMU file into the model</p><hr /><ol><li>In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click <strong>File</strong> &gt;<strong> Import From </strong>&gt; <strong>FMU File</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="fmu_file_import.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Select the FMU file on your file system and click <strong>Open</strong>. <br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Import dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p>In the <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog, specify which properties from the FMU file should be imported into your model as values and ports and Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="FMU Import Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h4>Dragging and dropping FMU on diagram</h4><p>Alternatively, you can import the FMU model by dragging and dropping the selected FMU file directly onto the Block Definition (BDD) and/or Internal Block (IBD) diagrams.</p><p><br /></p><p>To import the FMU file into the model using BDD/IBD</p><hr /><ol><li>Locate the <strong>FMU file</strong> on your file system or the Containment tree. </li><li>Do either:<br /><ul><li>Drag and drop the file from your file system or the Containment tree onto the BDD diagram. A Part property is created in the model after customizing the FMU import via the<strong> FMU Import Options</strong> dialog.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_fmu_block_ibd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li></ol><h4>Dragging and dropping FMU on existing Block</h4><p>You can drag and drop the FMU file directly onto the Block as implementation as well. Once the FMU file is dropped, the<strong> FMU Import Options</strong> dialog opens. This dialog allows redefining value and part properties.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="redefining_fmu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-gray-bolder,#626f86);">Redefining properties using FMU Import Options dialog</span></h6><p>In this case, a new Block is created as a subtype of the existing Block together with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generalization" /></ac:link> relationship. </p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="fmu_implementation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Dropping FMU on an existing Block</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4836eded-870c-4976-bba3-1c26597c5727"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>In/Out properties</strong> in the <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog are by default imported into the model as ports of a Flow type.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Updating existing FMU Block</h4><p>An existing FMU Block can be updated by dragging and dropping a FMU file directly on it. with.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_existing_fmu_block.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the opened <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog, select the properties to update the FMU Block with.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_fmu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b627fb5d-7372-4afc-9f42-465555b849ba"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Already imported properties are grayed out and cannot be edited in the <strong>FMU Import /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Structured Import</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span The FMU <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">properties are represented as Flow or Value properties, owned by an Interface Block. </span>For structured properties, <strong>As Port </strong>and <strong>Interface Block</strong> columns are inactive.<br /><br /></span></span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Structured FMU_Enabled.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">FMU properties split according to dot notation.</h6><h3>Merging arrays</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the FMU file has arrays (properties with the same name and index), then selecting the Merge Arrays checkbox in the FMU Import Options dialog merges all the arrays into a single property with the multiplicity set.<br /><br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Merge Array result_Comparison.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">All the arrays present in the FMU file merged into a single property with the multiplicity set.</h6><h3>Viewing FMU details</h3><p>You can view the details about the imported FMU file in the FMU Import Options dialog. Once you click the <strong>Details </strong>button, the details are displayed in the expanded section of the dialog.<br /><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Details_displayed.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) FMU import" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Details about the imported FMU file displayed in the expanded section of the FMU Import Options dialog.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MMA" ri:content-title="Simulation of SysML models" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990619 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00854: (2026x Refresh1) Forecast Kinds in Time Periods dialog

- page_id: `303990619`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990619/2026x+Refresh1+Forecast+Kinds+in+Time+Periods+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:50.464+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs > (2026x Refresh1) Time Periods dialog
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

These set of instructions will explain how to choose**Forecast Kinds**and how to specify their values.

There are three types of Forecast Kind:

- - to divide the forecast into equal periods.
- - to divide the forecast into specific dates.
- **Specified** **Periods** - to divide the forecast into specific time periods.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

periodicTo specify Periodic Forecast Kind

1. Select **Periodic** as the **Forecast Kind.**
2. Specify From Date by doing one of the following:
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='alt+down.png'] , or Alt+Down, and select an already existing date from the list.
  - Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select a date from the opened**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**dialog.
3. Specify forecast periods in the **Number of Periods** field.
4. Select **Length Unit:** Years, Months, or Days.
5. Specify **Period Length** of the forecast according to the **Length Unit.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An Example of a Periodic Forecast Kind.

All data defined in the**Time Periods** dialog is displayed automatically in columns for each period.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Periodic Forecast Kind in the Model

#### NOTE: Specified Dates

Specified Dates

For Specified Dates Forecast Kind, you only need to specify the Forecast Dates. The previous fields are uneditable.

specifieddatesTo specify Specified Dates Forecast Kind

1. Select Specified Dates as the Forecast Kind.
2. Under Forecast Dates, click [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dot_edit_small.png'] to open the Select ISO8601 Date Time dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_forecast_dates_property.png']
3. Select the existing ISO8601DateTime element (date) from the List. You can create a new one by taking the following steps.
  - In the **Select ISO8601 Date Time** dialog, make sure **Creation Mode** is on.
  - Select the appropriate package from the list in which you want to store a new **ISO8601DateTime** element (date).
  - Click the Create button. The new date element's specification window opens automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_ISO8601_date_time_dialog.png']
  - In the Value field, click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] , and select an appropriate date from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Date and Time Settings dialog' space=''] dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_value_property_of_ISO8601DateTime.png']
  - Click Close, or create more dates by repeating steps c. and d.
4. Click**OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An Example of a Specified Dates Forecast Kind.

All data defined in the **Time Periods** dialog are displayed in columns in the table. In this case, three dates (2016-09-23, 2017-09-23, and 2018-03-23) were automatically divided into two columns (from 2016-09-23 to 2017-09-23, and from 2017-09-23 to 2018-03-23).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Specified Dates Forecast Kind in the Model

specifiedperiodsTo specify Specified Periods Forecast Kind

1. Select Specified Periods as the Forecast Kind.
2. In the Time Periods field, click [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dot_edit_small.png'] to open the Select Time Period dialog.
3. In the opened dialog, you can do the following:
  - Click the Tree [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2018-04-29 at 11.59.07 AM.png'] button and expand the tree until you find an existing Timeline element ( [ATTACHMENT filename='timeline_icon.png'] ) with existing [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and double-click on each one. Skip to Step 4.
  - Click the List [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2018-04-29 at 11.56.13 AM.png'] button and double-click each existing Time Periods ( [ATTACHMENT filename='time_periods_icon.png'] ). Skip to Step 4.
  - Create a new Timeline element with Time Periods . Here's how:
    - Ensure that element Creation Mode is turned on.
    - Select the appropriate package from the list in which you want to store a new Timeline element.
    - Click the Create button. The Specification window of the newly created Timeline opens automatically.
    - Type a name for the new Timeline, and click Close .
    - Select the newly created Timeline element.
    - Click the Create button, and select Time Period . The Specification window of the newly created Time Period opens automatically. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_time_periods.png']
    - Specify a Name, Start Date, and End Date. [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_time_period_start_and_end_dates.png']
    - Click Close.
4. Click OK.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Specified Periods Forecast Kind.

All time periods defined in the **Time Periods** dialog are displayed in the table as columns. In this case, three periods (2016 period, 2017 period, and 2018 period) with their specific start and end dates were defined and displayed as columns in the SV-9 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Specified Periods Forecast Kind in the Model

## PAGE 00855: (2026x Refresh1) Formatting symbols

- page_id: `303995069`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995069/2026x+Refresh1+Formatting+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:04.968+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2038795777

2038795781

2038795780

| Definition | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Symbol | The term "symbol" means a visual representation of some model elements in the diagram. Symbols are subdivided into shapes and paths (lines in the model, for displaying various relationships). |
| Symbol properties | Every symbol may have its own style: color, font, size, and so forth. Symbol properties may be defined for the concrete symbol, for all symbol of one element, or according to the diagram type. |

You may define the symbol properties in the following ways:

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog .
- In the Symbol tab on the Properties panel at the bottom of the Model Browser .
- In the Project Options dialog. Using this dialog you can change all available symbol properties, create your own style for the project, apply different symbol properties for different diagrams, define stereotype properties that may be bound to the symbol, and more.
- On the diagram toolbar. Using this toolbar you can change the color, font, path style of a symbol, and paste the symbol properties.

2038795776

**Related pages**

true****

Symbols are subdivided into shapes and paths (lines in the model, for displaying various relationships).</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Symbol properties</strong></p></td><td><p>Every symbol may have its own style: color, font, size, and so forth. Symbol properties may be defined for the concrete symbol, for all symbol of one element, or according to the diagram type.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p>You may define the symbol properties in the following ways:</p><ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the <strong>Symbol</strong> tab on the Properties panel at the bottom of the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a0687278-e1b1-4dd6-b076-87b98f20ed3d">Model Browser</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. Using this dialog you can change all available symbol properties, create your own style for the project, apply different symbol properties for different diagrams, define stereotype properties that may be bound to the symbol, and more.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar. Using this toolbar you can change the color, font, path style of a symbol, and paste the symbol properties.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="61ebd3ca-1c4c-4ffd-8b9d-675559af3905"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2038795776</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e3fd9faf-650b-4fac-9416-a5e0922bbeb9"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998126 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00856: (2026x Refresh1) Formatting template code

- page_id: `303998126`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998126/2026x+Refresh1+Formatting+template+code
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:59.291+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard template language
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard supports the **Tab** key to help you format template code at ease. The **Tab** key is useful to indent velocity code. You can press the **Tab** key after the symbol "#" or "$" followed by the velocity code. The **Tab** key can help you format the template code, but it does not affect the output of a generated report. See figure below for example.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The velocity code in the figure above uses the **Tab** key to indent code. However, the generated report does not include those indents. The**Tab** key support is available in *RTF*, *DOCX*, and *ODT* format files.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard supports the <strong>Tab</strong> key to help you format template code at ease. The <strong>Tab</strong> key is useful to indent velocity code. You can press the <strong>Tab</strong> key after the symbol &quot;#&quot; or &quot;$&quot; followed by the velocity code. The <strong>Tab</strong> key can help you format the template code, but it does not affect the output of a generated report. See figure below for example.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Indented Template Code " ac:alt="Indented Template Code "><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 59.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Formatting template code" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The velocity code in the figure above uses the <strong>Tab</strong> key to indent code. However, the generated report does not include those indents. The<strong> Tab</strong> key support is available in <em>RTF</em>, <em>DOCX</em>, and <em>ODT</em> format files.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995795 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00858: (2026x Refresh1) Forward traceability - realization

- page_id: `303995795`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995795/2026x+Refresh1+Forward+traceability+-+realization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:03.364+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Traceability > (2026x Refresh1) Predefined traceability rules
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The forward traceability ensures that all specified artifacts are covered by elements from the lower abstraction level.

| Property Name | Description | Applied for | Reference through... | Value elements type | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Manifested By Artifacts | The property shows artifacts that physically render the current component. | Component | Relationships: Manifestation | Artifact | Example |
| Realizing Component | The property shows the components representing the class realization in the Implementation model. | Class | Relationships: Abstraction | Component | Example |
| Realizing Classifier | The property shows classifiers that realize components through component realization. | Component | Relationships: Component Realization, Realization | Classifier | Example |
| Realizing Class | The property shows the classes representing the use case realization in the Design model. | Use Class | Relationships: Abstraction | Class | Example |
| Realizing Use Case | The property shows the realizing use cases of the current use case in the lower level of abstraction thus demonstrating how the use case is implemented. For example, the Requirements Use Case realizes the Business Use Case. | Use Case | Relationships: Abstraction | Use Case | Example |
| Realizing Classifier | The property shows the classifiers conforming to the contract specified by the interface and related with this interface through the interface realization relationship. | Interface | Relationships: Interface Realization | Classifier | Example |
| Realizing Element, All Realizing Elements.Available in Enterprise and Architect editions only. | The Realizing Element property gathers the source elements of the abstraction relationship.The All Realizing Elements property gathers recursively (performs a realizing element search for the result) source elements of the abstraction relationship. | Element | Relationships: Abstraction, Component Realization, Interface Realization. | Element | Example |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990676 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00859: (2026x Refresh1) FractionDigits

- page_id: `303990676`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990676/2026x+Refresh1+FractionDigits
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:51.898+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) XML schemas > (2026x Refresh1) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDfractionDigits. The name and type of such an attribute do not make sense.

- value – to Attribute initial value.

#### PANEL: fractionDigits XML representation summary

fractionDigits XML representation summary

```text
<fractionDigits
```

```text
fixed = boolean : false
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
value = nonNegativeInteger
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</fractionDigits>
```

#### PANEL: fractionDigits XML code sample

fractionDigits XML code sample

```text
The following is the definition of a user-derived datatype which could be used to represent the magnitude of a person's body temperature on the Celsius scale. This definition would appear in a schema authored by an end-user and shows how to define a datatype by specifying facet values which constrain the range of the base type.
```

```text
<simpleType name='celsiusBodyTemp'>
```

```text
<restriction base='decimal'>
```

```text
<totalDigits value='4'/>
```

```text
<fractionDigits value='1'/>
```

```text
<minInclusive value='36.4'/>
```

```text
<maxInclusive value='40.5'/>
```

```text
</restriction>
```

```text
</simpleType>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae0fa29d-a5ab-446b-a7d0-1fb577775db2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1402678557</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="006e90e6-0eed-44a2-a901-c005bf96002f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1402678559</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55fe271e-aa5b-4656-a9b5-5372a65ad8bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1402678558</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bca70f5b-4ce0-4dce-94a0-8e7477757142"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Allocation Matrix" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06919204-f283-417d-9007-7a7c5f5b8d2c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1402678556</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4a9b5310-953b-409e-9e13-5898010b90bd" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990087 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00862: (2026x Refresh1) Functional analysis

- page_id: `303990087`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990087/2026x+Refresh1+Functional+analysis
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:15.231+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Functional analysis is necessary for a user to understand in detail how a system works or should behave. Functional analysis is a continuation of Use Case scenario refinements, using diagrams describing the behavior of a system, e.g., SysML Activity, SysML Sequences, or SysML State Machine diagrams. A new SysML Activity diagram should be created for every function of a Use Case allocated to a particular block or part. Although there are two or even more Swimlane partitions, you should choose only those functions that are nested under the partition representing the Block which captures your system.

The following chapters describe each feature's functionality.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Functional analysis is necessary for a user to understand in detail how a system works or should behave. <span>Functional analysis is a continuation of Use Case scenario refinements, using diagrams describing the behavior of a system, e.g.,  <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e9ec4ca5-0256-4bda-ae4e-c09b1643a9c2">SysML Activity, SysML Sequences, or SysML State Machine diagrams</ac:inline-comment-marker>. A new SysML Activity diagram should be created for every function of a Use Case allocated to a particular block or part. Alt<span>hough there are two or even more <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6012bc6c-658a-4ec4-9287-2455da85980f">Swimlane</ac:inline-comment-marker> partitions, you should choose only those functions that are nested under the partition representing the Block which captures <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="89bf5843-7dd2-4454-bd1b-d6039794885f">your system</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span>. </span></span></p><p><span>The following chapters describe each feature's functionality.</span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="22934b67-10a8-40e3-b5c9-7c155401a4ec" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994749 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00863: (2026x Refresh1) Functions of Specification window

- page_id: `303994749`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994749/2026x+Refresh1+Functions+of+Specification+window
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:47.888+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Specification window
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1291722198

1291722200

1291722199

Using Specification window you can perform various actions that are necessary when working with model elements. Every model element has its own specification.

The following table describes only common functions of the Specification window.

| Function | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add/modify modelelement propertiesand inner elements | Choose the desired property group and specify its properties in the general specification pane. |
| Open referencedelementspecifications | From the shortcut menu, select Open Specification or click . When another element specification is open, you can work with both specifications in the same window. |
| Navigate betweenopen specifications | To navigate between open specifications, use the Back and Forward buttons. |
| Manage relationships | View all relationships in which the element participates. Modify the name of the relationship.View/change the direction of the relationship.Modify the target element.Create new outgoing or incoming relationships. |
| Add/edit elementdocumentation | Add element description, describe use case scenarios, or add other relevant information that can be used for generating reports or technical documentation. Documentation also can be written in HTML using the . |
| Manage elementhyperlinks | Open, edit, add, or remove from the selected model element to a file, address, other element / symbol, or requirements. |
| Manage element tags and tagged values | Create tag definitions, default tagged values. |
| Manage elementconstraints | to an element. |
| Track elementsymbol usage in diagrams | Find out in which diagrams the symbol is used. Open these diagrams directly from the specification window. |
| Select modelelement in theContainment tree | From the shortcut menu, choose Select in Containment Tree or click . |
| View element traceability | Monitor element changes and the impact of those changes using t. |

Select a specification property and from the shortcut menu, select the actionyou want to perform.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990536 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00864: (2026x Refresh1) Gateways

- page_id: `303990536`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990536/2026x+Refresh1+Gateways
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:43.785+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Gateway allows you to control the flow of a process through a sequence flow. The term Gateway implies that there is a gating mechanism that either allows or disallows passage through the Gateway. Tokens that arrive at the gateway can be merged as inputs and/or split as outputs.

If the flow of a process does not need to be controlled, process does not need a gateway.

Types of Gateways are as follows:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Gateway allows you to control the flow of a process through a sequence flow. The term Gateway implies that there is a gating mechanism that either allows or disallows passage through the Gateway. Tokens that arrive at the gateway can be merged as inputs and/or split as outputs.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35c3d694-56d8-480d-b108-857840b84e81"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the flow of a process does not need to be controlled, process does not need a gateway.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">Types of Gateways are as follows: </p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ec3547c1-14ec-4061-bf1f-4aeac661b320" /></p>
````

1487850766

**Description**

A Generalization link shows that one class provides grouping criteria for sets of other classes. Generic class may define sets of properties, that are applicable to all the specific classes. The Generalization link can be among classes.
