<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998610 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00867: (2026x Refresh1) Generalization and specialization

- page_id: `303998610`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998610/2026x+Refresh1+Generalization+and+specialization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:48.078+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Entity-relationship (ER) modeling and diagrams
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An ER diagram has a support for generalization / specialization modeling. Generalization and Specialization are really the same relationship, with a different direction of classification. Generalization is bottom-up, while specialization is top-down. Thus, they use the same model element.

Generalizations can be joined into generalization sets (trees of generalizations), which allow specifying additional properties on a group of generalizations - such as disjointness and completeness constraints.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of generalization in ER diagram.

Disjointness and completeness constraints are specified using the **Is Disjoint**(*true*for disjoint, *false*for overlapping specialization) and **Is Covering**(*true*for total, *false*for partial specialization) properties. They can be set in either the relationship shortcut menu or the Specification window.

There are 4 combinations of these two settings. The “breadloaf” symbol joining generalizations into a tree shows these 4 variations (See the following figures).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of overlapping and partial specialization in ER diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of overlapping and total specialization in ER diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of disjoint and partial specialization in ER diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of disjoint and total specialization in ER diagram.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

UML terminology (covering / not covering) is used for the completeness property name in the Specification window. Other names, more familiar for data modelers, are total / partial and complete / incomplete. These terms are analogous and can be used interchangeably.

In the specialization hierarchies, there can be several ways to assign an entity instance to a specific entity subtype. You can determine it when you decide to which subtype a given instance belongs (user-defined specialization). You can also determine it by actual data values of an entity instance (attribute-defined specialization). The latter case can be further subdivided into two subcases - simple attribute-based discrimination (when discrimination is performed by doing simple attribute value comparison) and more complex predicate-based discrimination (when discrimination is specified using more complex, explicitly specified conditions).

Examples of these two cases are shown in the following figures.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of attribute-based discriminator in ER diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a predicate-based discriminator in an ER diagram.

Discriminators are modeled as special constraints placed on individual generalization relationships. The easiest way to access them is from the shortcut menu of the generalization.

The predicate-based discriminator is simpler; just fill in the **Specification**field of the predicate with an appropriate expression text.

The attribute-based discriminator is more complex. First, specify the columns by which you will sort the entities into the corresponding subclasses. This is done by filling in the **Discriminator**field of the generalization set (you can specify one or several columns there). Then, fill in the **Template**field of the predicate. This template field holds an instance specification used as a template or etalon to differentiate the entity instances into appropriate subclasses. Fill in the slots for the same columns you indicated on the generalization set.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The category (also known as union) concept is currently not explicitly supported. Total (but not partial) categories can be “simulated” using the total specialization tree, just visually reversed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An ER diagram has a support for generalization / specialization modeling. Generalization and Specialization are really the same relationship, with a different direction of classification. Generalization is bottom-up, while specialization is top-down. Thus, they use the same model element.</p><p>Generalizations can be joined into generalization sets (trees of generalizations), which allow specifying additional properties on a group of generalizations - such as disjointness and completeness constraints.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 3 Generalization in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generalization and specialization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of generalization in ER diagram.</h6><p>Disjointness and completeness constraints are specified using the <strong>Is Disjoint </strong>(<em>true </em>for disjoint, <em>false </em>for overlapping specialization) and <strong>Is Covering </strong>(<em>true </em>for total, <em>false </em>for partial specialization) properties. They can be set in either the relationship shortcut menu or the Specification window.</p><p>There are 4 combinations of these two settings. The “breadloaf” symbol joining generalizations into a tree shows these 4 variations (See the following figures).</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 4 Overlapping and Partial Specialization in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generalization and specialization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of overlapping and partial specialization in ER diagram.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 5 Overlapping and Total Specialization in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generalization and specialization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of overlapping and total specialization in ER diagram.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 6 Disjoint and Partial Specialization in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generalization and specialization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of disjoint and partial specialization in ER diagram.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 7 Disjoint and Total Specialization in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generalization and specialization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of disjoint and total specialization in ER diagram.</h6><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b45fd844-fae4-459e-b0a9-d0e26c8f51dc"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>UML terminology (covering / not covering) is used for the completeness property name in the Specification window. Other names, more familiar for data modelers, are total / partial and complete / incomplete. These terms are analogous and can be used interchangeably.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In the specialization hierarchies, there can be several ways to assign an entity instance to a specific entity subtype. You can determine it when you decide to which subtype a given instance belongs (user-defined specialization). You can also determine it by actual data values of an entity instance (attribute-defined specialization). The latter case can be further subdivided into two subcases - simple attribute-based discrimination (when discrimination is performed by doing simple attribute value comparison) and more complex predicate-based discrimination (when discrimination is specified using more complex, explicitly specified conditions).</p><p style="text-align: left;">Examples of these two cases are shown in the following figures.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 8 Attribute-based Discriminator in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generalization and specialization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of attribute-based discriminator in ER diagram.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 9 Predicate-based Discriminator in ER Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generalization and specialization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a predicate-based discriminator in an ER diagram.</h6><p>Discriminators are modeled as special constraints placed on individual generalization relationships. The easiest way to access them is from the shortcut menu of the generalization.</p><p>The predicate-based discriminator is simpler; just fill in the <strong>Specification </strong>field of the predicate with an appropriate expression text.</p><p>The attribute-based discriminator is more complex. First, specify the columns by which you will sort the entities into the corresponding subclasses. This is done by filling in the <strong>Discriminator </strong>field of the generalization set (you can specify one or several columns there). Then, fill in the <strong>Template </strong>field of the predicate. This template field holds an instance specification used as a template or etalon to differentiate the entity instances into appropriate subclasses. Fill in the slots for the same columns you indicated on the generalization set.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3aee853b-c72e-474b-b837-0e4f2ab6fede"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The category (also known as union) concept is currently not explicitly supported. Total (but not partial) categories can be “simulated” using the total specialization tree, just visually reversed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996334 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00868: (2026x Refresh1) Generate Output pane

- page_id: `303996334`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996334/2026x+Refresh1+Generate+Output+pane
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:35.717+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard dialog
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Generate Output** pane in Report Wizard allows you to configure report files by selecting the report files’ output location and image format, and displaying the report in the viewer.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Generate Output pane.

The table below describes the function of each option in the **Generate Output** pane of Report Wizard.

| Component Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Report file | Shows the report file’s location and name. The default report location will be \data\template_folder\reports\. The default report name will be the same as the report name defined by the user. |
|  | Opens the Select Location dialog in order to locate the report file. |
| Report image format | Selects an image format for your report: JPG, PNG, SVG, EMF, or WMF.Note:Use *.JPG and *.PNG for any template format.Use *.SVG for text, HTML, DOCX, and DocBooK templates.Use *.EMF and *.WMF for text and Microsoft Office templates (RTF, DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX). |
| Auto image size | Changes the size and orientation of an image before inserting it into a document. There are four options available:No Resize-Image will not be resized or rotated.Fit image to paper (large only)-Large image will be fit to within the paper size.Fit and rotate (clockwise) image to paper (large only)-Large image will be fit towithin the paper size and rotated clockwise.Fit and rotate (counter-clockwise) image to paper (large only)- Large image will be fit to within the paper size and rotated counter-clockwise. |
| Display empty value as | Empty text-Leave an empty value of the report output blank.Custom text as-Enter a custom value for an empty value. The default value is NA. |
|  | Displays a complete report in the viewer. Otherwise, the report will be created and kept in a selected location. |
|  | Generates a report. |
|  | Cancels the report generation process and closes the Report Wizard dialog. |
|  | Provides the Help content. |

Displaying an empty value with a text feature was deprecated. Or, you can select one of the following two options:

- Use a template code to replace empty value with text. For example: Using normal 
codeUsing macro 
code#if (!$var || $var == "")Empty#end#macro (replaceNullValueWithText $var)#if (!var || $var == "")EMPTY#end#end

- Use report wizard environment option to configure empty.

Add the property “template.output.on.blank.field” and value in the Report Wizard Environment option to enable this feature. The value of this property will be inserted in the generated output when the variable is empty (see *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard Environment options' space='']*).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

There are four options available:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li><strong>No Resize-</strong>Image will not be resized or rotated.</li><li><strong>Fit image to paper (large only)-</strong>Large image will be fit to within the paper size.</li><li><strong>Fit and rotate (clockwise) image to paper (large only)-</strong>Large image will be fit to<br />within the paper size and rotated clockwise.</li><li><strong>Fit and rotate (counter-clockwise) image to paper (large only)-</strong> <br />Large image will be fit to within the paper size and rotated counter-clockwise.</li></ol></li></ul></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Display empty value as</td><td><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>Empty text-</strong>Leave an empty value of the report output blank.</li><li><strong>Custom text as-</strong>Enter a custom value for an empty value. The default value is <strong>NA</strong>.</li></ul></li></ul></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displayinviewer_option.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Displays a complete report in the viewer. Otherwise, the report will be created and kept in a selected location.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Generates a report.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Cancel_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td>Cancels the report generation process and closes the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Help_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generate Output pane" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></td><td colspan="1">Provides the Help content.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ae31dea-c121-4d00-b575-7d8b1cd6fe74"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Displaying an empty value with a text feature was deprecated. Or, you can select one of the following two options:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Use a template code to replace empty value with text. For example:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p>Using normal <br class="_mce_tagged_br" />code</p></th><th><p>Using macro <br class="_mce_tagged_br" />code</p></th></tr><tr><td><pre class="_mce_tagged_br">#if (!$var || $var == &quot;&quot;)</pre><pre class="_mce_tagged_br"> Empty</pre><pre class="_mce_tagged_br">#end</pre></td><td><pre>#macro (replaceNullValueWithText $var)</pre><pre> #if (!var || $var == &quot;&quot;)</pre><pre> EMPTY</pre><pre> #end</pre><pre>#end</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><ul><li>Use report wizard environment option to configure empty.</li></ul><p>Add the property “template.output.on.blank.field” and value in the Report Wizard Environment option to enable this feature. The value of this property will be inserted in the generated output when the variable is empty (see <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard Environment options" /></ac:link></em>).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996750 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00869: (2026x Refresh1) Generating a differences report

- page_id: `303996750`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996750/2026x+Refresh1+Generating+a+differences+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:18.563+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Model Merge > (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing and managing merge results
- labels: ['report', 'reporting', 'generating-report', 'report-templates', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1056652507

1056652509

1056652508

When you're done reviewing differences and wish to see them *on paper*, you can simply export all the diagrams with visible changes to a Microsoft Word document using one of the predefined templates or your custom report template.

To generate a differences report using a predefined template

1. Click the Report button in the toolbar that is located at the top of the Merge window. [ATTACHMENT filename='merge_toolbar_report.png']
2. Choose the preferred template – Full Report for a more detailed overview, including the entire Merged Result tree with all changes, or Diagrams Report . [ATTACHMENT filename='reports_shortcut_menu.png']
3. The Save Report dialog opens. Specify the location in your file system for your report to be stored, type the file name and click Save .

If you need to slightly modify the report, for example, to show less data or add your company's information, you can edit the generated Microsoft Word document as per your needs.

However, if you require more control over what's represented in reports, you can customize report templates and generate differences reports of any desired depth and scope.

##### Creating custom report templates

All the .docx files located in *<configuration files directory>\data\merge reports* are treated as report templates, therefore you can copy and edit any of the existing templates, then save it as your custom report template with a different name.

You will see your saved template in the drop-down list of available templates when you click the **Report** button [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

You can read more about creating and modifying report templates in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Template Creation Tutorial' space='']**Report Wizard Template Creation Tutorial**.

1056652506

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing and managing merge results' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Configuration files' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Specify the location in your file system for your report to be stored, type the file name and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>If you need to slightly modify the report, for example, to show less data or add your company's information, you can edit the generated Microsoft Word document as per your needs.</p><p>However, if you require more control over what's represented in reports, you can customize report templates and generate differences reports of any desired depth and scope.</p><h3>Creating custom report templates</h3><p>All the .docx files located in <em>&lt;configuration files directory&gt;\data\merge reports</em> are treated as report templates, therefore you can copy and edit any of the existing templates, then save it as your custom report template with a different name.</p><p>You will see your saved template in the drop-down list of available templates when you click the <strong>Report</strong> button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="report_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p>You can read more about creating and modifying report templates in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Template Creation Tutorial" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Report Wizard Template Creation Tutorial</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1953b4d9-a38b-49fc-b2f7-5323ba96c83d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1056652506</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing and managing merge results" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Configuration files" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992089 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00870: (2026x Refresh1) Generating a project comparison differences report

- page_id: `303992089`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992089/2026x+Refresh1+Generating+a+project+comparison+differences+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:30.321+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Comparing projects > (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing comparison results
- labels: ['report', 'reporting', 'generating-report', 'report-templates', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you're done reviewing differences and wish to see them *on paper*, you can simply export all the diagrams with visible changes to a Microsoft Word document using one of the predefined templates or your custom report template.

To generate a differences report using a predefined template

1. Click the Report button in the Difference Viewer toolbar. [ATTACHMENT filename='difference_viewer_toolbar.png']
2. Choose the preferred template – Full Report for a more detailed overview, including the entire Differences tree with all changes, or Diagrams Report . [ATTACHMENT filename='reports_shortcut_menu.png']
3. The Save Report dialog opens. Specify the location in your file system for your report to be stored, type the file name and click Save .

If you need to slightly modify the report, for example, to show less data or add your company's information, you can edit the generated Microsoft Word document as per your needs.

However, if you require more control over what's represented in reports, you can customize report templates and generate differences reports of any desired depth and scope.

The difference reports cannot be customized/extended with the model information using the usual Report Wizard helper tools.

##### Creating custom report templates

All the .docx files found in the Merge Reports directory are treated as report templates, therefore you can copy and edit any of the existing templates, then save it as your custom report template with a different name.You will see your saved template in the drop-down list of available templates when you click the **Report** button [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

You can read more about creating and modifying report templates in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Template Creation Tutorial' space='']**Report Wizard Template Creation Tutorial**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you're done <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6b3fcb5e-da83-4e54-aeba-d442a9f9fd5f">reviewing</ac:inline-comment-marker> differences and wish to see them <em>on paper</em>, you can simply export all the diagrams with visible changes to a Microsoft Word document using one of the predefined templates or your custom report template.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate a differences report using a predefined template</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Report </strong>button in the<strong> Difference Viewer</strong> toolbar. <br /><ac:image ac:height="55"><ri:attachment ri:filename="difference_viewer_toolbar.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating a project comparison differences report" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Choose the preferred template – <strong class="inline-comment-marker"> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7df30601-2a98-4df1-8211-0c279e157cad">Full Report</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong> for a more detailed overview, including the entire <strong>Differences</strong> tree with all changes, or <strong>Diagrams Report</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Report shortcut menu" ac:alt="Report shortcut menu"><ri:attachment ri:filename="reports_shortcut_menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating a project comparison differences report" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>The <strong>Save Report</strong> dialog opens. Specify the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="53902d52-44e4-4948-8f05-d0d42f82be77">location</ac:inline-comment-marker> in your file system for your report to be stored, type the file name and click <strong>Save</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>If you need to slightly modify the report, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="324c69e9-de1f-43ec-8efe-11d97dd22b80">for example,</ac:inline-comment-marker> to show less data or add your company's information, you can edit the generated Microsoft Word document as per your needs.</p><p>However, if you require more control over what's represented in reports, you can <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="626a2b05-8cea-47db-8802-64511a6c44ee">customize</ac:inline-comment-marker> report templates and generate differences reports of any desired depth and <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ecf6af8d-98f7-44ac-93ea-3c950744d202">scope</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="952e0da7-91d5-4333-8c14-ce12ca3b69bd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The difference reports cannot be customized/extended with the model information using the usual Report Wizard helper tools.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Creating custom report templates</h3><p class="inline-comment-marker">All the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3465320f-d47a-4a5f-9ed7-cfb98dcf9920">.docx</ac:inline-comment-marker> files found in the Merge Reports directory are treated as report templates, therefore you can copy and edit any of the existing templates, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2a2129a8-5c36-4e55-b3b6-e628f7a13a83">then save it as your custom report template with a different name. </ac:inline-comment-marker>You will see your saved template in the drop-down list of available templates when you click the <strong>Report</strong> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="cde43cac-8ca1-45be-9b08-081268e85b1a">button</ac:inline-comment-marker> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="report_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</p><p>You can read more about <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b42a1619-fb27-4189-ae45-6bbc8d5716cd">creating</ac:inline-comment-marker> and modifying report templates in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Template Creation Tutorial" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Report Wizard Template Creation Tutorial</strong> </ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999235 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00871: (2026x Refresh1) Generating a report from Teamwork Cloud Server

- page_id: `303999235`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999235/2026x+Refresh1+Generating+a+report+from+Teamwork+Cloud+Server
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:42.766+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Generating reports from the command line
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1300364630

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1300364632

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1300364631

INLINE

Apart from the mandatory commands described in [using the Generate command to generate reports](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Using+the+Generate+command+to+generate+reports), you can use the following command arguments in the command line to generate a report from the Teamwork Cloud (TWCloud) server.

- -server , -login , and -password are key arguments to log on to TWCloud server. You will be prompted for a password when trying to enter -server and -login without a password.
- -pversion is a key argument to specify a TWCloud project version. If either you do not specify any version, or the version you specify does not match any version in the TWCloud repository, Report Wizard will generate the latest project version of the specified branch.
- -branch option is used to retrieve a particular project branch. If you do not specify any branch, the trunk would be specified.
- -updatemodule A Boolean parameter. True for updating the used module of the project to the latest version. False by default, when the user does not specify this option in the command.
- **-tag** is an option to specify a Teamwork project. You can specify the tagName to search for a project version. The returned project version may be more than one. You may use this command argument with "**-incremental true**". If you use this command with **-branch,**it will return a matching project version in the specified branch.
- **-projectpassword** is required to specify a password to open a password-protected Teamwork Cloud project. The password is required for the project to generate a report.
- **-usagepackage** specifies the name of one or more packages in Project Usages of a TWCloud project to be included in the report. Package entries must be separated by a semi-colon (;).
- **-usageelement** specifies the name of one or more elements in the Project Usages of TWCloudproject to be included in the report. Element entries must be separated by a semi-colon (;). NoteFor TWCloud projects, **-package** / **-element** is used to select one or more packages/elements only in the Primary Model of TWCloud projects. To select packages/elements in Project Usages, specify **-usagepackage** / **-usageelement**. You can also set **-includeAuxiliary** as true if you want to make auxiliary resources available for selection. This **-usagepackage** / **-usageelement** argument can be used with **-package** / **-element** to select packages/elements in both Primary Model and Project Usages.

If, for example, you want to generate a Web Publisher 2.0 report from a TWCloud project by getting the latest project version of the trunk branch, type the following:

```text

```

The following code generates a Class Specification Report report from the TWCloud project by getting the project version number 10 from MyBranch branch of the MyProject project.

```text

The following code generates a specific project version by specifying tagName.

The following code generates a specific version of a project without specifying its branch.

The following code generates a specific version of a project from a branch release.

The following code generates a set of reports from a specific tag.

```text

```

The following code generates a report through an SSL secured connection.

```text

```

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

The specified project version would be used to generate the report as a first priority, in case the specified project version does not belong in the specified branch.

#### WARNING: Note

Note

If the project version specified is not the latest, the **-updatemodule**option would not affect the **generate** command.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000310 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00872: (2026x Refresh1) Generating an Actual Measurements table from a Typical Measurements table

- page_id: `304000310`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000310/2026x+Refresh1+Generating+an+Actual+Measurements+table+from+a+Typical+Measurements+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:59.644+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures > (2026x Refresh1) Measurements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Typical Measurements table' space='']Typical Measurements table can be instantiated by creating an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Actual Measurements table' space='']Actual Measurementstable.

To generate anActual Measurements table from a Typical measurements table

1. Create a Typical Measurements table with at least one measurable Property Set added.
2. On the table toolbar, click the Evaluate Measurements button. A new Actual Measurements table is created.
3. Add actual values for all Actual Measurements . The Actual Property Set is created and the rows representing its slots defined by Measurements are added to the Actual Measurements table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A new Actual Measurements table is created.</li><li class="confluence-link">Add <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">actual values for all Actual Measurements</span>.<br /><span>The Actual Property Set is created and the rows representing its slots defined by Measurements are added to the Actual Measurements table. </span></li></ol>
